* MTA My Way server entry point.\n *\n * Startup sequence:\n *   1. Load GTFS static data (stations, routes, complexes, transfers, travel times)\n *   2. Create Hono app (also builds TransferEngine internally)\n *   3. Initialise push notification subsystem (SQLite DB, VAPID keys, pipeline)\n *   4. Initialise and start the feed poller (first poll fires immediately)\n *   5. Start the HTTP server\n */\n\nimport { readFile } from \"node:fs/promises\";\nimport { dirname, join, resolve } from \"node:path\";\nimport { fileURLToPath } from \"node:url\";\nimport { serve } from \"@hono/node-server\";\nimport { PACKAGE_VERSION } from \"@mta-my-way/shared\";\nimport type {\n  ComplexIndex,\n  RouteIndex,\n  StationIndex,\n  TransferConnection,\n} from \"@mta-my-way/shared\";\nimport { startAlertsPoller } from \"./alerts-poller.js\";\nimport { createApp } from \"./app.js\";\n// Context-aware switching service disabled to reduce security surface area\n// import { initContextService } from \"./context-service.js\";\nimport { initDelayDetector } from \"./delay-detector.js\";\nimport { initDelayPredictor, initDelayPredictorForTesting } from \"./delay-predictor.js\";\nimport { initEquipmentPoller, startEquipmentPoller } from \"./equipment-poller.js\";\nimport { setRateLimiterTestMode } from \"./middleware/rate-limiter.js\";\nimport { runMigrations } from \"./migration/index.js\";\nimport { logger } from \"./observability/logger.js\";\nimport { initPoller, startPoller } from \"./poller.js\";\nimport { startBriefingScheduler } from \"./push/briefing.js\";\nimport { startPushPipeline } from \"./push/index.js\";\nimport { getPushDatabase, initPushDatabase } from \"./push/subscriptions.js\";\nimport { configureWebPush, loadOrGenerateVapidKeys } from \"./push/vapid.js\";\nimport { validateSecurityOrThrow } from \"./security-startup.js\";\nimport { loadTravelTimes } from \"./transfer/travel-times.js\";\nimport { initTripTracking } from \"./trip-tracking.js\";\n\nconst __filename = fileURLToPath(import.meta.url);\nconst __dirname = dirname(__filename);\n\n// packages/server/data/ — same relative depth from both src/ and dist/\nconst DATA_DIR = join(__dirname, \"..\", \"data\");\n\n// packages/web/dist/ — two levels up from src/ or dist/, then into sibling\nconst WEB_DIST = resolve(__dirname, \"..\", \"..\", \"web\", \"dist\");\n\nconst PORT = parseInt(process.env[\"PORT\"] ?? \"3001\", 10);\n\n/**\n * Load a JSON data file from the data directory\n */\nasync function loadJsonFile<T>(filename: string): Promise<T> {\n  const raw = await readFile(join(DATA_DIR, filename), \"utf8\");\n  return JSON.parse(raw) as T;\n}\n\nasync function main(): Promise<void> {\n  // Validate security configuration first (fail-fast on critical issues)\n  validateSecurityOrThrow();\n\n  // Enable test mode if environment variable is set (for E2E tests)\n  const testMode = process.env[\"TEST_MODE\"] === \"true\";\n  if (testMode) {\n    setRateLimiterTestMode(true);\n    logger.info(\"Test mode enabled\");\n  }\n\n  logger.info(\"Server startup\", {\n    version: PACKAGE_VERSION,\n    port: PORT,\n    data_dir: DATA_DIR,\n    web_dist: WEB_DIST,\n  });\n\n  // Load GTFS static data in parallel\n  let stations: StationIndex;\n  let routes: RouteIndex;\n  let complexes: ComplexIndex;\n  let transfers: Record<string, TransferConnection[]>;\n\n  try {\n    [stations, routes, complexes, transfers] = await Promise.all([\n      loadJsonFile<StationIndex>(\"stations.json\"),\n      loadJsonFile<RouteIndex>(\"routes.json\"),\n      loadJsonFile<ComplexIndex>(\"complexes.json\"),\n      loadJsonFile<Record<string, TransferConnection[]>>(\"transfers.json\"),\n    ]);\n\n    // Load travel times into module cache before createApp builds the TransferEngine\n    const travelTimes = await loadTravelTimes(join(DATA_DIR, \"travel-times.json\"));\n\n    // Initialize delay detector for predictive alerts\n    initDelayDetector(travelTimes, routes, stations);\n\n    // Initialize delay predictor for historical pattern analysis\n    if (testMode) {\n      initDelayPredictorForTesting();\n    }\n    initDelayPredictor(travelTimes, stations);\n\n    logger.info(\"Static data loaded\", {\n      stations: Object.keys(stations).length,\n      routes: Object.keys(routes).length,\n      complexes: Object.keys(complexes).length,\n      transfers: Object.keys(transfers).length,\n    });\n  } catch (err) {\n    logger.error(\"Failed to load static data\", err as Error, {\n      hint: \"Run: npm run process-gtfs --workspace=packages/server\",\n    });\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n\n  // Hono app (builds TransferEngine internally from loaded data)\n  const app = createApp(stations, routes, complexes, transfers, WEB_DIST);\n\n  // Push notification subsystem\n  const pushDbPath = process.env[\"PUSH_DB_PATH\"] ?? join(DATA_DIR, \"subscriptions.db\");\n  initPushDatabase(pushDbPath);\n  const pushDb = getPushDatabase();\n\n  // Run database migrations\n  try {\n    const results = await runMigrations(pushDb);\n    const applied = results.filter((r) => r.applied);\n    if (applied.length > 0) {\n      logger.info(\"Database migrations applied\", {\n        count: applied.length,\n        versions: applied.map((r) => r.version),\n      });\n    }\n  } catch (err) {\n    logger.error(\"Database migration failed\", err as Error);\n    throw err;\n  }\n\n  const vapidKeys = await loadOrGenerateVapidKeys(DATA_DIR);\n  configureWebPush(vapidKeys);\n  startPushPipeline();\n  startBriefingScheduler();\n\n  // Initialize trip tracking and journal (Phase 5)\n  initTripTracking(pushDb, stations);\n\n  // Context-aware switching service disabled to reduce security surface area\n  // initContextService(pushDb, stations);\n\n  // Feed poller (also triggers immediate first poll)\n  initPoller(stations, routes);\n  startPoller();\n  startAlertsPoller();\n\n  // Equipment poller (elevator/escalator outages)\n  initEquipmentPoller(stations);\n  startEquipmentPoller();\n\n  // HTTP server\n  serve({ fetch: app.fetch, port: PORT }, (info) => {\n    logger.info(\"Server started\", {\n      port: info.port,\n      pid: process.pid,\n      uptime: 0,\n    });\n  });\n}\n\nmain().catch((err) => {\n  logger.error(\"Server startup failed\", err);\n  process.exit(1);\n});\n","numLines":175,"startLine":1,"totalLines":175}}}

operations on specific user-owned resources (trips, subscriptions, etc.).\n120\t */\n121\texport function requireResourceAccess(\n122\t  resourceType: ResourceType,\n123\t  action: PermissionAction,\n124\t  options: {\n125\t    /** Admin scope bypasses ownership checks */\n126\t    adminBypass?: boolean;\n127\t    /** Custom access check function */\n128\t    customCheck?: (context: Context, auth: AuthContext) => boolean | Promise<boolean>;\n129\t    /** Resource ID parameter name (for extracting from route params) */\n130\t    resourceIdParam?: string;\n131\t  } = {}\n132\t): MiddlewareHandler {\n133\t  const { adminBypass = true, customCheck } = options;\n134\t\n135\t  return async (c, next) => {\n136\t    const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n137\t\n138\t    // Must be authenticated\n139\t    if (!auth) {\n140\t      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, resourceType, action);\n141\t      throw new HTTPException(401, { message: \"Authentication required\" });\n142\t    }\n143\t\n144\t    // Admin bypass check\n145\t    if (adminBypass && auth.scope === \"admin\") {\n146\t      logger.info(\"Admin bypass for resource access\", {\n147\t        keyId: auth.keyId,\n148\t        resourceType,\n149\t        action,\n150\t      });\n151\t      return next();\n152\t    }\n153\t\n154\t    // Scope-based authorization check\n155\t    const scopeLevels: Record<ApiKeyScope, number> = { read: 1, write: 2, admin: 3 };\n156\t    const requiredScopeForAction: Record<PermissionAction, ApiKeyScope> = {\n157\t      create: \"write\",\n158\t      read: \"read\",\n159\t      update: \"write\",\n160\t      delete: \"write\",\n161\t      admin: \"admin\",\n162\t    };\n163\t\n164\t    const requiredScope = requiredScopeForAction[action];\n165\t    if (scopeLevels[auth.scope] < scopeLevels[requiredScope]) {\n166\t      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, resourceType, action);\n167\t      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n168\t        message: `Insufficient permissions. Required scope: ${requiredScope}`,\n169\t      });\n170\t    }\n171\t\n172\t    // Custom access check if provided\n173\t    if (customCheck) {\n174\t      const hasAccess = await customCheck(c, auth);\n175\t      if (!hasAccess) {\n176\t        securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, resourceType, action);\n177\t        throw new HTTPException(403, {\n178\t          message: \"Access denied to this resource\",\n179\t        });\n180\t      }\n181\t    }\n182\t\n183\t    return next();\n184\t  };\n185\t}\n186\t\n187\t/**\n188\t * Require admin scope for privileged operations.\n189\t * Use for operations that affect system configuration or other users.\n190\t *\n191\t * Enhanced security: Validates both admin scope AND admin role.\n192\t */\n193\texport function requireAdmin(): MiddlewareHandler {\n194\t  return async (c, next) => {\n195\t    const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n196\t\n197\t    if (!auth) {\n198\t      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"admin\", \"admin\");\n199\t      throw new HTTPException(401, { message: \"Authentication required\" });\n200\t    }\n201\t\n202\t    // Enhanced check: require BOTH admin scope AND admin role\n203\t    const hasAdminScope = auth.scope === \"admin\";\n204\t    const hasAdminRole = auth.role === \"admin\" || auth.roles?.includes(\"admin\");\n205\t\n206\t    if (!hasAdminScope || !hasAdminRole) {\n207\t      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"admin\", \"admin\");\n208\t      logger.warn(\"Admin access denied - insufficient privileges\", {\n209\t        keyId: auth.keyId,\n210\t        scope: auth.scope,\n211\t        role: auth.role,\n212\t        roles: auth.roles,\n213\t      });\n214\t      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n215\t        message: \"Admin privileges required\",\n216\t      });\n217\t    }\n218\t\n219\t    logger.info(\"Admin operation authorized\", {\n220\t      keyId: auth.keyId,\n221\t      path: c.req.path,\n222\t      method: c.req.method,\n223\t    });\n224\t    return next();\n225\t  };\n226\t}\n227\t\n228\t/**\n229\t * Require write scope for state-changing operations.\n230\t * Use as a simpler alternative to requireResourceAccess for general write operations.\n231\t */\n232\texport function requireWrite(): MiddlewareHandler {\n233\t  return async (c, next) => {\n234\t    const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n235\t\n236\t    if (!auth) {\n237\t      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"api\", \"write\");\n238\t      throw new HTTPException(401, { message: \"Authentication required\" });\n239\t    }\n240\t\n241\t    const scopeLevels: Record<ApiKeyScope, number> = { read: 1, write: 2, admin: 3 };\n242\t    if (scopeLevels[auth.scope] < 2) {\n243\t      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"api\", \"write\");\n244\t      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n245\t        message: \"Write permissions required\",\n246\t      });\n247\t    }\n248\t\n249\t    return next();\n250\t  };\n251\t}\n252\t\n253\t/**\n254\t * Optional authorization - attaches auth context if present but doesn't require it.\n255\t * Use for endpoints that have enhanced features for authenticated users.\n256\t *\n257\t * Note: This is already provided by authentication.ts but re-exported for convenience.\n258\t */\n259\texport { optionalAuth, isAuthenticated } from \"./authentication.js\";\n260\t\n261\t/**\n262\t * Rate limit tier enforcement.\n263\t * Ensures API key rate limits are respected based on assigned tier.\n264\t */\n265\texport function enforceRateLimitTier(maxTier?: number): MiddlewareHandler {\n266\t  return async (c, next) => {\n267\t    const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n268\t\n269\t    if (!auth) {\n270\t      // Unauthenticated requests use default rate limiting (middleware level)\n271\t      return next();\n272\t    }\n273\t\n274\t    // Check if the API key's rate limit tier is within bounds\n275\t    if (maxTier !== undefined && auth.rateLimitTier > maxTier) {\n276\t      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"rate_limit\", \"tier_exceeded\");\n277\t      throw new HTTPException(429, {\n278\t        message: \"Rate limit tier exceeded for this operation\",\n279\t      });\n280\t    }\n281\t\n282\t    return next();\n283\t  };\n284\t}\n285\t\n286\t// ============================================================================\n287\t// Cross-Origin Protection for State Changes\n288\t// ============================================================================\n289\t\n290\t/**\n291\t * Verify same-origin for state-changing operations.\n292\t * Prevents CSRF attacks by validating the Origin header for POST/DELETE/PATCH.\n293\t */\n294\texport function requireSameOrigin(): MiddlewareHandler {\n295\t  return async (c, next) => {\n296\t    const method = c.req.method;\n297\t\n298\t    // Only apply to state-changing methods\n299\t    if (![\"POST\", \"DELETE\", \"PATCH\", \"PUT\"].includes(method)) {\n300\t      return next();\n301\t    }\n302\t\n303\t    const origin = c.req.header(\"Origin\");\n304\t    const host = c.req.header(\"Host\");\n305\t    const referer = c.req.header(\"Referer\");\n306\t\n307\t    // Skip for API calls with proper authentication (API key or session)\n308\t    const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n309\t    if (auth && (auth.authMethod === \"api_key\" || auth.authMethod === \"session\")) {\n310\t      return next();\n311\t    }\n312\t\n313\t    // For browser-based requests without auth, check Origin/Referer\n314\t    if (!origin && !referer) {\n315\t      securityLogger.logSuspiciousActivity(c, \"missing_origin_referer\");\n316\t      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n317\t        message: \"Origin or Referer header required for state-changing operations\",\n318\t      });\n319\t    }\n320\t\n321\t    // Validate Origin matches Host (if Origin is present)\n322\t    if (origin && host) {\n323\t      const originHost = new URL(origin).host;\n324\t      if (originHost !== host) {\n325\t        securityLogger.logSuspiciousActivity(c, \"origin_mismatch\");\n326\t        throw new HTTPException(403, {\n327\t          message: \"Cross-origin requests not allowed for state-changing operations\",\n328\t        });\n329\t      }\n330\t    }\n331\t\n332\t    // Validate Referer matches Host (if Referer is present and no Origin)\n333\t    if (referer && host && !origin) {\n334\t      const refererHost = new URL(referer).host;\n335\t      if (refererHost !== host) {\n336\t        securityLogger.logSuspiciousActivity(c, \"referer_mismatch\");\n337\t        throw new HTTPException(403, {\n338\t          message: \"Cross-origin requests not allowed for state-changing operations\",\n339\t        });\n340\t      }\n341\t    }\n342\t\n343\t    return next();\n344\t  };\n345\t}\n346\t\n347\t// ============================================================================\n348\t// Data Access Validation\n349\t// ============================================================================\n350\t\n351\t/**\n352\t * Validate that a request only accesses data the user is allowed to see.\n353\t * Used for queries that might return user-scoped data.\n354\t */\n355\texport function validateDataAccess(resourceType: ResourceType): MiddlewareHandler {\n356\t  return async (c, next) => {\n357\t    const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n358\t\n359\t    // For authenticated users, ensure they can only access their own data\n360\t    if (auth) {\n361\t      // Check query parameters for user-scoped filters\n362\t      const userId = c.req.query(\"userId\");\n363\t      const endpoint = c.req.query(\"endpoint\");\n364\t\n365\t      // If trying to access another user's data, require admin scope\n366\t      if ((userId || endpoint) && auth.scope !== \"admin\") {\n367\t        securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, resourceType, \"read\");\n368\t        throw new HTTPException(403, {\n369\t          message: \"Access to other users' data requires admin privileges\",\n370\t        });\n371\t      }\n372\t    }\n373\t\n374\t    return next();\n375\t  };\n376\t}\n377\t\n378\t// ============================================================================\n379\t// Authorization Check Utilities\n380\t// ============================================================================\n381\t\n382\t/**\n383\t * Check if the current context has authorization for a specific action.\n384\t * Returns an AuthorizationResult with details.\n385\t */\n386\texport function checkAuthorization(\n387\t  c: Context,\n388\t  resourceType: ResourceType,\n389\t  action: PermissionAction\n390\t): AuthorizationResult {\n391\t  const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n392\t\n393\t  if (!auth) {\n394\t    return {\n395\t      allowed: false,\n396\t      reason: \"Not authenticated\",\n397\t      required: \"Authentication required\",\n398\t    };\n399\t  }\n400\t\n401\t  const scopeLevels: Record<ApiKeyScope, number> = { read: 1, write: 2, admin: 3 };\n402\t  const requiredScopeForAction: Record<PermissionAction, ApiKeyScope> = {\n403\t    create: \"write\",\n404\t    read: \"read\",\n405\t    update: \"write\",\n406\t    delete: \"write\",\n407\t    admin: \"admin\",\n408\t  };\n409\t\n410\t  const requiredScope = requiredScopeForAction[action];\n411\t  if (scopeLevels[auth.scope] < scopeLevels[requiredScope]) {\n412\t    return {\n413\t      allowed: false,\n414\t      reason: \"Insufficient permissions\",\n415\t      required: requiredScope,\n416\t    };\n417\t  }\n418\t\n419\t  return { allowed: true };\n420\t}\n421\t\n422\t/**\n423\t * Require MFA verification for sensitive operations.\n424\t * Use for operations that should require multi-factor authentication.\n425\t *\n426\t * Enhanced security: Logs MFA requirements and validates session integrity.\n427\t */\n428\texport function requireMfa(): MiddlewareHandler {\n429\t  return async (c, next) => {\n430\t    const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n431\t\n432\t    if (!auth) {\n433\t      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"mfa\", \"required\");\n434\t      throw new HTTPException(401, { message: \"Authentication required\" });\n435\t    }\n436\t\n437\t    // Check if MFA was verified for this session\n438\t    if (!auth.mfaVerified) {\n439\t      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"mfa\", \"required\");\n440\t      logger.warn(\"MFA required but not verified\", {\n441\t        keyId: auth.keyId,\n442\t        path: c.req.path,\n443\t        method: c.req.method,\n444\t        authMethod: auth.authMethod,\n445\t      });\n446\t      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n447\t        message: \"Multi-factor authentication required for this operation\",\n448\t      });\n449\t    }\n450\t\n451\t    logger.info(\"MFA-verified operation authorized\", {\n452\t      keyId: auth.keyId,\n453\t      path: c.req.path,\n454\t      method: c.req.method,\n455\t    });\n456\t    return next();\n457\t  };\n458\t}\n459\t\n460\t/**\n461\t * Require both admin privileges AND MFA verification for highly sensitive operations.\n462\t * Use for operations like:\n463\t * - Managing other users' accounts\n464\t * - Changing system configuration\n465\t * - Accessing sensitive audit logs\n466\t * - Modifying API keys\n467\t */\n468\texport function requireAdminWithMfa(): MiddlewareHandler {\n469\t  return async (c, next) => {\n470\t    const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n471\t\n472\t    if (!auth) {\n473\t      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"admin_mfa\", \"required\");\n474\t      throw new HTTPException(401, { message: \"Authentication required\" });\n475\t    }\n476\t\n477\t    // Check admin scope and role\n478\t    const hasAdminScope = auth.scope === \"admin\";\n479\t    const hasAdminRole = auth.role === \"admin\" || auth.roles?.includes(\"admin\");\n480\t\n481\t    if (!hasAdminScope || !hasAdminRole) {\n482\t      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"admin_mfa\", \"insufficient_privileges\");\n483\t      logger.warn(\"Admin+MFA access denied - insufficient privileges\", {\n484\t        keyId: auth.keyId,\n485\t        scope: auth.scope,\n486\t        role: auth.role,\n487\t        roles: auth.roles,\n488\t      });\n489\t      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n490\t        message: \"Admin privileges required\",\n491\t      });\n492\t    }\n493\t\n494\t    // Check MFA verification\n495\t    if (!auth.mfaVerified) {\n496\t      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"admin_mfa\", \"mfa_required\");\n497\t      logger.warn(\"Admin+MFA access denied - MFA not verified\", {\n498\t        keyId: auth.keyId,\n499\t        path: c.req.path,\n500\t        method: c.req.method,\n501\t      });\n502\t      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n503\t        message: \"Multi-factor authentication required for admin operations\",\n504\t      });\n505\t    }\n506\t\n507\t    logger.info(\"Admin+MFA operation authorized\", {\n508\t      keyId: auth.keyId,\n509\t      path: c.req.path,\n510\t      method: c.req.method,\n511\t    });\n512\t    return next();\n513\t  };\n514\t}\n515\t\n516\t/**\n517\t * Audit log wrapper for privileged operations.\n518\t * Logs all access to sensitive resources for compliance.\n519\t */\n520\texport function auditLogAccess(\n521\t  resourceType: ResourceType,\n522\t  action: PermissionAction\n523\t): MiddlewareHandler {\n524\t  return async (c, next) => {\n525\t    const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n526\t    const clientIp =\n527\t      c.req.header(\"CF-Connecting-IP\") ||\n528\t      c.req.header(\"X-Forwarded-For\")?.split(\",\")[0]?.trim() ||\n529\t      c.req.header(\"X-Real-IP\") ||\n530\t      \"unknown\";\n531\t\n532\t    // Log the attempt\n533\t    logger.info(\"Privileged operation attempt\", {\n534\t      keyId: auth?.keyId,\n535\t      resourceType,\n536\t      action,\n537\t      path: c.req.path,\n538\t      method: c.req.method,\n539\t      clientIp,\n540\t      authenticated: !!auth,\n541\t    });\n542\t\n543\t    // Execute the operation\n544\t    await next();\n545\t\n546\t    // Log the result\n547\t    const status = c.res.status;\n548\t    const success = status >= 200 && status < 300;\n549\t\n550\t    logger.info(\"Privileged operation completed\", {\n551\t      keyId: auth?.keyId,\n552\t      resourceType,\n553\t      action,\n554\t      path: c.req.path,\n555\t      status,\n556\t      success,\n557\t      clientIp,\n558\t    });\n559\t  };\n560\t}\n561\t\n562\t// ============================================================================\n563\t// Scope Restriction Helpers\n564\t// ============================================================================\n565\t\n566\t/**\n567\t * Get the owner ID for an authenticated user, or \"anonymous\" for unauthenticated.\n568\t * This is used to scope database queries to the user's own data.\n569\t *\n570\t * @param c - Hono context\n571\t * @param allowAdminAll - If true, admins get undefined (no filter), otherwise they also get their own keyId\n572\t * @returns Owner ID to use in queries, or undefined for admins to see all data\n573\t */\n574\texport function getOwnerIdForAuthContext(\n575\t  c: Context,\n576\t  allowAdminAll: boolean = true\n577\t): string | undefined {\n578\t  const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n579\t\n580\t  // Unauthenticated users get anonymous\n581\t  if (!auth) {\n582\t    return \"anonymous\";\n583\t  }\n584\t\n585\t  // Admins can see all data if allowAdminAll is true\n586\t  if (allowAdminAll && auth.role === \"admin\") {\n587\t    return undefined; // No filter - return all data\n588\t  }\n589\t\n590\t  // Regular authenticated users get their own keyId\n591\t  return auth.keyId || \"anonymous\";\n592\t}\n593\t\n594\t/**\n595\t * Apply ownership scope to query parameters.\n596\t * Ensures non-admin users can only query their own resources.\n597\t *\n598\t * @param c - Hono context\n599\t * @param params - Existing query parameters\n600\t * @returns Parameters with ownership filter applied\n601\t */\n602\texport function applyOwnershipScope<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(\n603\t  c: Context,\n604\t  params: T\n605\t): T & { ownerId?: string } {\n606\t  const ownerId = getOwnerIdForAuthContext(c, true);\n607\t\n608\t  if (ownerId === undefined) {\n609\t    // Admin user - no ownership filter\n610\t    return params as T & { ownerId?: string };\n611\t  }\n612\t\n613\t  // Non-admin user - add ownership filter\n614\t  return { ...params, ownerId };\n615\t}\n616\t\n617\t/**\n618\t * Require ownership of a resource for access.\n619\t * This is a helper that validates ownership before allowing access.\n620\t *\n621\t * @param c - Hono context\n622\t * @param resourceOwnerId - Owner ID of the resource being accessed\n623\t * @returns true if access is allowed, throws HTTPException otherwise\n624\t */\n625\texport function requireResourceOwnership(c: Context, resourceOwnerId: string): boolean {\n626\t  const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n627\t\n628\t  // Admins bypass ownership checks\n629\t  if (auth?.role === \"admin\") {\n630\t    return true;\n631\t  }\n632\t\n633\t  // Check if the user owns the resource\n634\t  const userOwnerId = auth?.keyId || \"anonymous\";\n635\t\n636\t  if (userOwnerId !== resourceOwnerId && resourceOwnerId !== \"anonymous\") {\n637\t    securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"resource\", \"ownership\");\n638\t    throw new HTTPException(403, {\n639\t      message: \"Access denied: you do not own this resource\",\n640\t    });\n641\t  }\n642\t\n643\t  return true;\n644\t}\n645\t\n646\t/**\n647\t * Scope options for database queries.\n648\t */\n649\texport interface ScopeOptions {\n650\t  /** Owner ID to filter by (undefined = no filter, return all) */\n651\t  ownerId?: string;\n652\t  /** Additional filter criteria */\n653\t  filters?: Record<string, unknown>;\n654\t  /** Limit on number of results */\n655\t  limit?: number;\n656\t  /** Offset for pagination */\n657\t  offset?: number;\n658\t}\n659\t\n660\t/**\n661\t * Create scoped query options for database operations.\n662\t * Automatically applies ownership filtering based on authentication.\n663\t *\n664\t * @param c - Hono context\n665\t * @param baseOptions - Base query options\n666\t * @returns Scoped options with ownership filter applied\n667\t */\n668\texport function createScopedQuery<T extends ScopeOptions>(c: Context, baseOptions: T = {} as T): T {\n669\t  const ownerId = getOwnerIdForAuthContext(c, true);\n670\t\n671\t  if (ownerId === undefined) {\n672\t    // Admin - return all data without ownership filter\n673\t    return baseOptions;\n674\t  }\n675\t\n676\t  // Non-admin - apply ownership filter\n677\t  return {\n678\t    ...baseOptions,\n679\t    ownerId,\n680\t  } as T;\n681\t}\n682\t\n683\t/**\n684\t * Check if the current user can access another user's data.\n685\t * Returns true if the user is an admin or if the targetUserId matches their own.\n686\t *\n687\t * @param c - Hono context\n688\t * @param targetUserId - The user ID being accessed\n689\t * @returns true if access is allowed\n690\t */\n691\texport function canAccessUserData(c: Context, targetUserId: string): boolean {\n692\t  const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n693\t\n694\t  // Admins can access any user's data\n695\t  if (auth?.role === \"admin\") {\n696\t    return true;\n697\t  }\n698\t\n699\t  // Users can only access their own data\n700\t  return auth?.keyId === targetUserId;\n701\t}\n702\t\n703\t/**\n704\t * Filter a response to include only user-scoped data.\n705\t * Removes sensitive information and ensures users only see their own data.\n706\t *\n707\t * @param c - Hono context\n708\t * @param data - Data to filter\n709\t * @param ownerField - Field name that contains the owner ID\n710\t * @returns Filtered data\n711\t */\n712\texport function filterUserScopedData<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(\n713\t  c: Context,\n714\t  data: T[],\n715\t  ownerField: string = \"ownerId\"\n716\t): T[] {\n717\t  const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n718\t\n719\t  // Admins see all data\n720\t  if (auth?.role === \"admin\") {\n721\t    return data;\n722\t  }\n723\t\n724\t  // Non-admins only see their own data\n725\t  const userOwnerId = auth?.keyId || \"anonymous\";\n726\t\n727\t  return data.filter((item) => {\n728\t    const ownerId = item[ownerField] as string | undefined;\n729\t    return ownerId === userOwnerId || ownerId === \"anonymous\";\n730\t  });\n731\t}\n732\t\n733\t/**\n734\t * Validate that a list request is properly scoped.\n735\t * Throws an error if a non-admin user tries to request another user's data.\n736\t *\n737\t * @param c - Hono context\n738\t * @param requestedOwnerId - Owner ID from request (if any)\n739\t * @throws HTTPException if access is denied\n740\t */\n741\texport function validateListScope(c: Context, requestedOwnerId?: string): void {\n742\t  const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n743\t\n744\t  // If no specific owner requested, this is fine (will be filtered by applyOwnershipScope)\n745\t  if (!requestedOwnerId) {\n746\t    return;\n747\t  }\n748\t\n749\t  // Admins can request any owner's data\n750\t  if (auth?.role === \"admin\") {\n751\t    return;\n752\t  }\n753\t\n754\t  // Non-admins can only request their own data\n755\t  const userOwnerId = auth?.keyId || \"anonymous\";\n756\t\n757\t  if (requestedOwnerId !== userOwnerId) {\n758\t    securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"list\", \"scope\");\n759\t    throw new HTTPException(403, {\n760\t      message: \"Access denied: you can only view your own data\",\n761\t    });\n762\t  }\n763\t}\n764\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"19b864e5-f5f9-4747-afef-54b401f04266","uuid":"0cbab670-a85f-4a1d-9d43-8ff03b9499fe","timestamp":"2026-05-09T05:24:58.394Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/mta-my-way/packages/server/src/middleware/authorization.ts","content":"/**\n * Authorization middleware for fine-grained access control.\n *\n * Provides:\n * - Resource-based access control (ownership verification)\n * - Action-based permissions (create, read, update, delete)\n * - Cross-origin request protection for state-changing operations\n * - Rate limit tier enforcement\n * - Admin-only operations protection\n * - Data access validation (user-scoped data isolation)\n * - Scope restriction helpers for filtering user data\n */\n\nimport type { Context, MiddlewareHandler } from \"hono\";\nimport { HTTPException } from \"hono/http-exception\";\nimport { logger } from \"../observability/logger.js\";\nimport { type ApiKeyScope, type AuthContext, getAuthContext } from \"./authentication.js\";\nimport { securityLogger } from \"./security-logging.js\";\n\n// Re-export RBAC types for compatibility\nexport type { PermissionAction, ResourceType, ResourcePolicy, AuthorizationResult };\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Types\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Permission action types for resource-based access control.\n */\nexport type PermissionAction = \"create\" | \"read\" | \"update\" | \"delete\" | \"admin\";\n\n/**\n * Resource types that can be protected.\n */\nexport type ResourceType =\n  | \"trip\"\n  | \"subscription\"\n  | \"context\"\n  | \"commute\"\n  | \"journal\"\n  | \"alert\"\n  | \"equipment\"\n  | \"prediction\"\n  | \"admin\";\n\n/**\n * Resource access policy - defines who can access what.\n */\nexport interface ResourcePolicy {\n  /** Resource type */\n  resourceType: ResourceType;\n  /** Required action */\n  action: PermissionAction;\n  /** Whether admin scope bypasses ownership checks */\n  adminBypass?: boolean;\n  /** Custom access check function */\n  customCheck?: (context: Context, auth: AuthContext) => boolean | Promise<boolean>;\n}\n\n/**\n * Authorization result with detailed reason for denial.\n */\nexport interface AuthorizationResult {\n  /** Whether access is granted */\n  allowed: boolean;\n  /** Reason for denial (if denied) */\n  reason?: string;\n  /** Required scope/permission (if denied) */\n  required?: string;\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Resource ID Extraction Utilities\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Extract resource owner ID from various resource types.\n * This validates that the authenticated user owns the resource being accessed.\n */\nexport interface ResourceOwner {\n  /** Owner identifier (e.g., subscription endpoint, trip ID with embedded user info) */\n  ownerId: string;\n  /** Whether the resource is public */\n  isPublic: boolean;\n}\n\n/**\n * Extract owner identifier from a push subscription endpoint.\n * The endpoint itself serves as the owner identifier since it's unique per device.\n */\nfunction _extractSubscriptionOwner(endpoint: string): ResourceOwner {\n  return { ownerId: endpoint, isPublic: false };\n}\n\n/**\n * Extract owner identifier from a trip ID.\n * For manual trips, the trip ID contains a timestamp-based component.\n * For API-tracked trips, ownership should be validated via session.\n */\nfunction _extractTripOwner(tripId: string): ResourceOwner {\n  // Trip IDs are UUIDs - ownership is validated via database lookup\n  // In a real system, trips would have a userId field\n  return { ownerId: tripId, isPublic: false };\n}\n\n/**\n * Extract owner from a commute ID.\n */\nfunction _extractCommuteOwner(commuteId: string): ResourceOwner {\n  return { ownerId: commuteId, isPublic: false };\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Authorization Middleware\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Verify that the authenticated user has access to a specific resource.\n * Used for operations on specific user-owned resources (trips, subscriptions, etc.).\n */\nexport function requireResourceAccess(\n  resourceType: ResourceType,\n  action: PermissionAction,\n  options: {\n    /** Admin scope bypasses ownership checks */\n    adminBypass?: boolean;\n    /** Custom access check function */\n    customCheck?: (context: Context, auth: AuthContext) => boolean | Promise<boolean>;\n    /** Resource ID parameter name (for extracting from route params) */\n    resourceIdParam?: string;\n  } = {}\n): MiddlewareHandler {\n  const { adminBypass = true, customCheck } = options;\n\n  return async (c, next) => {\n    const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n\n    // Must be authenticated\n    if (!auth) {\n      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, resourceType, action);\n      throw new HTTPException(401, { message: \"Authentication required\" });\n    }\n\n    // Admin bypass check\n    if (adminBypass && auth.scope === \"admin\") {\n      logger.info(\"Admin bypass for resource access\", {\n        keyId: auth.keyId,\n        resourceType,\n        action,\n      });\n      return next();\n    }\n\n    // Scope-based authorization check\n    const scopeLevels: Record<ApiKeyScope, number> = { read: 1, write: 2, admin: 3 };\n    const requiredScopeForAction: Record<PermissionAction, ApiKeyScope> = {\n      create: \"write\",\n      read: \"read\",\n      update: \"write\",\n      delete: \"write\",\n      admin: \"admin\",\n    };\n\n    const requiredScope = requiredScopeForAction[action];\n    if (scopeLevels[auth.scope] < scopeLevels[requiredScope]) {\n      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, resourceType, action);\n      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n        message: `Insufficient permissions. Required scope: ${requiredScope}`,\n      });\n    }\n\n    // Custom access check if provided\n    if (customCheck) {\n      const hasAccess = await customCheck(c, auth);\n      if (!hasAccess) {\n        securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, resourceType, action);\n        throw new HTTPException(403, {\n          message: \"Access denied to this resource\",\n        });\n      }\n    }\n\n    return next();\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Require admin scope for privileged operations.\n * Use for operations that affect system configuration or other users.\n *\n * Enhanced security: Validates both admin scope AND admin role.\n */\nexport function requireAdmin(): MiddlewareHandler {\n  return async (c, next) => {\n    const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n\n    if (!auth) {\n      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"admin\", \"admin\");\n      throw new HTTPException(401, { message: \"Authentication required\" });\n    }\n\n    // Enhanced check: require BOTH admin scope AND admin role\n    const hasAdminScope = auth.scope === \"admin\";\n    const hasAdminRole = auth.role === \"admin\" || auth.roles?.includes(\"admin\");\n\n    if (!hasAdminScope || !hasAdminRole) {\n      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"admin\", \"admin\");\n      logger.warn(\"Admin access denied - insufficient privileges\", {\n        keyId: auth.keyId,\n        scope: auth.scope,\n        role: auth.role,\n        roles: auth.roles,\n      });\n      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n        message: \"Admin privileges required\",\n      });\n    }\n\n    logger.info(\"Admin operation authorized\", {\n      keyId: auth.keyId,\n      path: c.req.path,\n      method: c.req.method,\n    });\n    return next();\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Require write scope for state-changing operations.\n * Use as a simpler alternative to requireResourceAccess for general write operations.\n */\nexport function requireWrite(): MiddlewareHandler {\n  return async (c, next) => {\n    const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n\n    if (!auth) {\n      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"api\", \"write\");\n      throw new HTTPException(401, { message: \"Authentication required\" });\n    }\n\n    const scopeLevels: Record<ApiKeyScope, number> = { read: 1, write: 2, admin: 3 };\n    if (scopeLevels[auth.scope] < 2) {\n      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"api\", \"write\");\n      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n        message: \"Write permissions required\",\n      });\n    }\n\n    return next();\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Optional authorization - attaches auth context if present but doesn't require it.\n * Use for endpoints that have enhanced features for authenticated users.\n *\n * Note: This is already provided by authentication.ts but re-exported for convenience.\n */\nexport { optionalAuth, isAuthenticated } from \"./authentication.js\";\n\n/**\n * Rate limit tier enforcement.\n * Ensures API key rate limits are respected based on assigned tier.\n */\nexport function enforceRateLimitTier(maxTier?: number): MiddlewareHandler {\n  return async (c, next) => {\n    const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n\n    if (!auth) {\n      // Unauthenticated requests use default rate limiting (middleware level)\n      return next();\n    }\n\n    // Check if the API key's rate limit tier is within bounds\n    if (maxTier !== undefined && auth.rateLimitTier > maxTier) {\n      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"rate_limit\", \"tier_exceeded\");\n      throw new HTTPException(429, {\n        message: \"Rate limit tier exceeded for this operation\",\n      });\n    }\n\n    return next();\n  };\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Cross-Origin Protection for State Changes\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Verify same-origin for state-changing operations.\n * Prevents CSRF attacks by validating the Origin header for POST/DELETE/PATCH.\n */\nexport function requireSameOrigin(): MiddlewareHandler {\n  return async (c, next) => {\n    const method = c.req.method;\n\n    // Only apply to state-changing methods\n    if (![\"POST\", \"DELETE\", \"PATCH\", \"PUT\"].includes(method)) {\n      return next();\n    }\n\n    const origin = c.req.header(\"Origin\");\n    const host = c.req.header(\"Host\");\n    const referer = c.req.header(\"Referer\");\n\n    // Skip for API calls with proper authentication (API key or session)\n    const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n    if (auth && (auth.authMethod === \"api_key\" || auth.authMethod === \"session\")) {\n      return next();\n    }\n\n    // For browser-based requests without auth, check Origin/Referer\n    if (!origin && !referer) {\n      securityLogger.logSuspiciousActivity(c, \"missing_origin_referer\");\n      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n        message: \"Origin or Referer header required for state-changing operations\",\n      });\n    }\n\n    // Validate Origin matches Host (if Origin is present)\n    if (origin && host) {\n      const originHost = new URL(origin).host;\n      if (originHost !== host) {\n        securityLogger.logSuspiciousActivity(c, \"origin_mismatch\");\n        throw new HTTPException(403, {\n          message: \"Cross-origin requests not allowed for state-changing operations\",\n        });\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Validate Referer matches Host (if Referer is present and no Origin)\n    if (referer && host && !origin) {\n      const refererHost = new URL(referer).host;\n      if (refererHost !== host) {\n        securityLogger.logSuspiciousActivity(c, \"referer_mismatch\");\n        throw new HTTPException(403, {\n          message: \"Cross-origin requests not allowed for state-changing operations\",\n        });\n      }\n    }\n\n    return next();\n  };\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Data Access Validation\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Validate that a request only accesses data the user is allowed to see.\n * Used for queries that might return user-scoped data.\n */\nexport function validateDataAccess(resourceType: ResourceType): MiddlewareHandler {\n  return async (c, next) => {\n    const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n\n    // For authenticated users, ensure they can only access their own data\n    if (auth) {\n      // Check query parameters for user-scoped filters\n      const userId = c.req.query(\"userId\");\n      const endpoint = c.req.query(\"endpoint\");\n\n      // If trying to access another user's data, require admin scope\n      if ((userId || endpoint) && auth.scope !== \"admin\") {\n        securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, resourceType, \"read\");\n        throw new HTTPException(403, {\n          message: \"Access to other users' data requires admin privileges\",\n        });\n      }\n    }\n\n    return next();\n  };\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Authorization Check Utilities\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Check if the current context has authorization for a specific action.\n * Returns an AuthorizationResult with details.\n */\nexport function checkAuthorization(\n  c: Context,\n  resourceType: ResourceType,\n  action: PermissionAction\n): AuthorizationResult {\n  const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n\n  if (!auth) {\n    return {\n      allowed: false,\n      reason: \"Not authenticated\",\n      required: \"Authentication required\",\n    };\n  }\n\n  const scopeLevels: Record<ApiKeyScope, number> = { read: 1, write: 2, admin: 3 };\n  const requiredScopeForAction: Record<PermissionAction, ApiKeyScope> = {\n    create: \"write\",\n    read: \"read\",\n    update: \"write\",\n    delete: \"write\",\n    admin: \"admin\",\n  };\n\n  const requiredScope = requiredScopeForAction[action];\n  if (scopeLevels[auth.scope] < scopeLevels[requiredScope]) {\n    return {\n      allowed: false,\n      reason: \"Insufficient permissions\",\n      required: requiredScope,\n    };\n  }\n\n  return { allowed: true };\n}\n\n/**\n * Require MFA verification for sensitive operations.\n * Use for operations that should require multi-factor authentication.\n *\n * Enhanced security: Logs MFA requirements and validates session integrity.\n */\nexport function requireMfa(): MiddlewareHandler {\n  return async (c, next) => {\n    const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n\n    if (!auth) {\n      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"mfa\", \"required\");\n      throw new HTTPException(401, { message: \"Authentication required\" });\n    }\n\n    // Check if MFA was verified for this session\n    if (!auth.mfaVerified) {\n      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"mfa\", \"required\");\n      logger.warn(\"MFA required but not verified\", {\n        keyId: auth.keyId,\n        path: c.req.path,\n        method: c.req.method,\n        authMethod: auth.authMethod,\n      });\n      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n        message: \"Multi-factor authentication required for this operation\",\n      });\n    }\n\n    logger.info(\"MFA-verified operation authorized\", {\n      keyId: auth.keyId,\n      path: c.req.path,\n      method: c.req.method,\n    });\n    return next();\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Require both admin privileges AND MFA verification for highly sensitive operations.\n * Use for operations like:\n * - Managing other users' accounts\n * - Changing system configuration\n * - Accessing sensitive audit logs\n * - Modifying API keys\n */\nexport function requireAdminWithMfa(): MiddlewareHandler {\n  return async (c, next) => {\n    const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n\n    if (!auth) {\n      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"admin_mfa\", \"required\");\n      throw new HTTPException(401, { message: \"Authentication required\" });\n    }\n\n    // Check admin scope and role\n    const hasAdminScope = auth.scope === \"admin\";\n    const hasAdminRole = auth.role === \"admin\" || auth.roles?.includes(\"admin\");\n\n    if (!hasAdminScope || !hasAdminRole) {\n      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"admin_mfa\", \"insufficient_privileges\");\n      logger.warn(\"Admin+MFA access denied - insufficient privileges\", {\n        keyId: auth.keyId,\n        scope: auth.scope,\n        role: auth.role,\n        roles: auth.roles,\n      });\n      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n        message: \"Admin privileges required\",\n      });\n    }\n\n    // Check MFA verification\n    if (!auth.mfaVerified) {\n      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"admin_mfa\", \"mfa_required\");\n      logger.warn(\"Admin+MFA access denied - MFA not verified\", {\n        keyId: auth.keyId,\n        path: c.req.path,\n        method: c.req.method,\n      });\n      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n        message: \"Multi-factor authentication required for admin operations\",\n      });\n    }\n\n    logger.info(\"Admin+MFA operation authorized\", {\n      keyId: auth.keyId,\n      path: c.req.path,\n      method: c.req.method,\n    });\n    return next();\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Audit log wrapper for privileged operations.\n * Logs all access to sensitive resources for compliance.\n */\nexport function auditLogAccess(\n  resourceType: ResourceType,\n  action: PermissionAction\n): MiddlewareHandler {\n  return async (c, next) => {\n    const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n    const clientIp =\n      c.req.header(\"CF-Connecting-IP\") ||\n      c.req.header(\"X-Forwarded-For\")?.split(\",\")[0]?.trim() ||\n      c.req.header(\"X-Real-IP\") ||\n      \"unknown\";\n\n    // Log the attempt\n    logger.info(\"Privileged operation attempt\", {\n      keyId: auth?.keyId,\n      resourceType,\n      action,\n      path: c.req.path,\n      method: c.req.method,\n      clientIp,\n      authenticated: !!auth,\n    });\n\n    // Execute the operation\n    await next();\n\n    // Log the result\n    const status = c.res.status;\n    const success = status >= 200 && status < 300;\n\n    logger.info(\"Privileged operation completed\", {\n      keyId: auth?.keyId,\n      resourceType,\n      action,\n      path: c.req.path,\n      status,\n      success,\n      clientIp,\n    });\n  };\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Scope Restriction Helpers\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Get the owner ID for an authenticated user, or \"anonymous\" for unauthenticated.\n * This is used to scope database queries to the user's own data.\n *\n * @param c - Hono context\n * @param allowAdminAll - If true, admins get undefined (no filter), otherwise they also get their own keyId\n * @returns Owner ID to use in queries, or undefined for admins to see all data\n */\nexport function getOwnerIdForAuthContext(\n  c: Context,\n  allowAdminAll: boolean = true\n): string | undefined {\n  const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n\n  // Unauthenticated users get anonymous\n  if (!auth) {\n    return \"anonymous\";\n  }\n\n  // Admins can see all data if allowAdminAll is true\n  if (allowAdminAll && auth.role === \"admin\") {\n    return undefined; // No filter - return all data\n  }\n\n  // Regular authenticated users get their own keyId\n  return auth.keyId || \"anonymous\";\n}\n\n/**\n * Apply ownership scope to query parameters.\n * Ensures non-admin users can only query their own resources.\n *\n * @param c - Hono context\n * @param params - Existing query parameters\n * @returns Parameters with ownership filter applied\n */\nexport function applyOwnershipScope<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(\n  c: Context,\n  params: T\n): T & { ownerId?: string } {\n  const ownerId = getOwnerIdForAuthContext(c, true);\n\n  if (ownerId === undefined) {\n    // Admin user - no ownership filter\n    return params as T & { ownerId?: string };\n  }\n\n  // Non-admin user - add ownership filter\n  return { ...params, ownerId };\n}\n\n/**\n * Require ownership of a resource for access.\n * This is a helper that validates ownership before allowing access.\n *\n * @param c - Hono context\n * @param resourceOwnerId - Owner ID of the resource being accessed\n * @returns true if access is allowed, throws HTTPException otherwise\n */\nexport function requireResourceOwnership(c: Context, resourceOwnerId: string): boolean {\n  const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n\n  // Admins bypass ownership checks\n  if (auth?.role === \"admin\") {\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  // Check if the user owns the resource\n  const userOwnerId = auth?.keyId || \"anonymous\";\n\n  if (userOwnerId !== resourceOwnerId && resourceOwnerId !== \"anonymous\") {\n    securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"resource\", \"ownership\");\n    throw new HTTPException(403, {\n      message: \"Access denied: you do not own this resource\",\n    });\n  }\n\n  return true;\n}\n\n/**\n * Scope options for database queries.\n */\nexport interface ScopeOptions {\n  /** Owner ID to filter by (undefined = no filter, return all) */\n  ownerId?: string;\n  /** Additional filter criteria */\n  filters?: Record<string, unknown>;\n  /** Limit on number of results */\n  limit?: number;\n  /** Offset for pagination */\n  offset?: number;\n}\n\n/**\n * Create scoped query options for database operations.\n * Automatically applies ownership filtering based on authentication.\n *\n * @param c - Hono context\n * @param baseOptions - Base query options\n * @returns Scoped options with ownership filter applied\n */\nexport function createScopedQuery<T extends ScopeOptions>(c: Context, baseOptions: T = {} as T): T {\n  const ownerId = getOwnerIdForAuthContext(c, true);\n\n  if (ownerId === undefined) {\n    // Admin - return all data without ownership filter\n    return baseOptions;\n  }\n\n  // Non-admin - apply ownership filter\n  return {\n    ...baseOptions,\n    ownerId,\n  } as T;\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if the current user can access another user's data.\n * Returns true if the user is an admin or if the targetUserId matches their own.\n *\n * @param c - Hono context\n * @param targetUserId - The user ID being accessed\n * @returns true if access is allowed\n */\nexport function canAccessUserData(c: Context, targetUserId: string): boolean {\n  const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n\n  // Admins can access any user's data\n  if (auth?.role === \"admin\") {\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  // Users can only access their own data\n  return auth?.keyId === targetUserId;\n}\n\n/**\n * Filter a response to include only user-scoped data.\n * Removes sensitive information and ensures users only see their own data.\n *\n * @param c - Hono context\n * @param data - Data to filter\n * @param ownerField - Field name that contains the owner ID\n * @returns Filtered data\n */\nexport function filterUserScopedData<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(\n  c: Context,\n  data: T[],\n  ownerField: string = \"ownerId\"\n): T[] {\n  const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n\n  // Admins see all data\n  if (auth?.role === \"admin\") {\n    return data;\n  }\n\n  // Non-admins only see their own data\n  const userOwnerId = auth?.keyId || \"anonymous\";\n\n  return data.filter((item) => {\n    const ownerId = item[ownerField] as string | undefined;\n    return ownerId === userOwnerId || ownerId === \"anonymous\";\n  });\n}\n\n/**\n * Validate that a list request is properly scoped.\n * Throws an error if a non-admin user tries to request another user's data.\n *\n * @param c - Hono context\n * @param requestedOwnerId - Owner ID from request (if any)\n * @throws HTTPException if access is denied\n */\nexport function validateListScope(c: Context, requestedOwnerId?: string): void {\n  const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n\n  // If no specific owner requested, this is fine (will be filtered by applyOwnershipScope)\n  if (!requestedOwnerId) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Admins can request any owner's data\n  if (auth?.role === \"admin\") {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Non-admins can only request their own data\n  const userOwnerId = auth?.keyId || \"anonymous\";\n\n  if (requestedOwnerId !== userOwnerId) {\n    securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"list\", \"scope\");\n    throw new HTTPException(403, {\n      message: \"Access denied: you can only view your own data\",\n    });\n  }\n}\n","numLines":764,"startLine":1,"totalLines":764}}}

*/\n94\t  riskThreshold?: number;\n95\t  /** Whether to require MFA for high-risk sessions */\n96\t  requireMfaForHighRisk?: boolean;\n97\t  /** Action to take on compromised tokens */\n98\t  onTokenCompromised?: \"block\" | \"revoke\" | \"allow\";\n99\t  /** Action to take on session limit exceeded */\n100\t  onSessionLimitExceeded?: \"deny\" | \"terminate_oldest\" | \"allow_all\";\n101\t}\n102\t\n103\t/**\n104\t * Enhanced authentication result attached to context.\n105\t */\n106\texport interface EnhancedAuthResult {\n107\t  /** Standard authentication context */\n108\t  auth: AuthContext;\n109\t  /** Session risk assessment (if available) */\n110\t  riskAssessment?: SessionRiskAssessment;\n111\t  /** Token compromise detection result (if available) */\n112\t  compromiseDetection?: CompromiseDetectionResult;\n113\t  /** Session conflict result (if applicable) */\n114\t  sessionConflict?: SessionConflictResult;\n115\t  /** Permission check results (if permissions checked) */\n116\t  permissionResults?: Map<string, PermissionCheckResult>;\n117\t  /** Whether additional verification is required */\n118\t  requiresAdditionalVerification?: boolean;\n119\t  /** Security incidents detected */\n120\t  securityIncidents?: string[];\n121\t}\n122\t\n123\t/**\n124\t * Security event for enhanced authentication tracking.\n125\t */\n126\texport interface AuthSecurityEvent {\n127\t  /** Event type */\n128\t  type: string;\n129\t  /** Event timestamp */\n130\t  timestamp: number;\n131\t  /** Event severity */\n132\t  severity: \"info\" | \"warning\" | \"error\" | \"critical\";\n133\t  /** Event message */\n134\t  message: string;\n135\t  /** Additional details */\n136\t  details?: Record<string, unknown>;\n137\t}\n138\t\n139\t// ============================================================================\n140\t// Default Configuration\n141\t// ============================================================================\n142\t\n143\tconst DEFAULT_CONFIG: Required<\n144\t  Omit<EnhancedAuthConfig, \"jwtSecret\" | \"timeBasedRules\" | \"locationRules\" | \"sessionConfig\">\n145\t> &\n146\t  Pick<EnhancedAuthConfig, \"timeBasedRules\" | \"locationRules\" | \"sessionConfig\"> = {\n147\t  enableJwtValidation: true,\n148\t  enableEnhancedJwt: true,\n149\t  enableConcurrentSessions: true,\n150\t  enableRbacCache: true,\n151\t  enableAuditLogging: true,\n152\t  enableTimeBasedAccess: false,\n153\t  enableLocationAccess: false,\n154\t  enableBehavioralAnalysis: true,\n155\t  timeBasedRules: undefined,\n156\t  locationRules: undefined,\n157\t  sessionConfig: {\n158\t    maxConcurrentSessions: 5,\n159\t    maxSessionsPerDevice: 2,\n160\t    conflictResolution: \"terminate_oldest\",\n161\t    sessionTimeout: 30 * 60 * 1000,\n162\t    maxIdleTime: 60 * 60 * 1000,\n163\t  },\n164\t  riskThreshold: 70,\n165\t  requireMfaForHighRisk: true,\n166\t  onTokenCompromised: \"revoke\",\n167\t  onSessionLimitExceeded: \"terminate_oldest\",\n168\t};\n169\t\n170\t// ============================================================================\n171\t// Middleware Factory Functions\n172\t// ============================================================================\n173\t\n174\t/**\n175\t * Create enhanced authentication middleware with all security features.\n176\t *\n177\t * This middleware provides comprehensive authentication by integrating:\n178\t * - JWT validation\n179\t * - Token compromise detection\n180\t * - Session management\n181\t * - Permission checking\n182\t * - Audit logging\n183\t * - Time/location-based access control\n184\t * - Behavioral analysis\n185\t *\n186\t * @param config - Enhanced authentication configuration\n187\t * @returns Hono middleware handler\n188\t */\n189\texport function createEnhancedAuth(config: EnhancedAuthConfig = {}): MiddlewareHandler {\n190\t  const mergedConfig = { ...DEFAULT_CONFIG, ...config };\n191\t\n192\t  return async (c, next) => {\n193\t    const startTime = Date.now();\n194\t    const securityEvents: AuthSecurityEvent[] = [];\n195\t\n196\t    // Get client information\n197\t    const clientIp =\n198\t      c.req.header(\"CF-Connecting-IP\") ||\n199\t      c.req.header(\"X-Forwarded-For\")?.split(\",\")[0]?.trim() ||\n200\t      c.req.header(\"X-Real-IP\") ||\n201\t      \"unknown\";\n202\t    const userAgent = c.req.header(\"User-Agent\");\n203\t\n204\t    // Get standard auth context\n205\t    const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n206\t    if (!auth) {\n207\t      securityEvents.push({\n208\t        type: \"auth_missing\",\n209\t        timestamp: startTime,\n210\t        severity: \"warning\",\n211\t        message: \"Authentication required\",\n212\t      });\n213\t      throw new HTTPException(401, { message: \"Authentication required\" });\n214\t    }\n215\t\n216\t    // Initialize enhanced auth result\n217\t    const enhancedResult: EnhancedAuthResult = {\n218\t      auth,\n219\t      securityIncidents: [],\n220\t    };\n221\t\n222\t    // 1. Time-based access control\n223\t    if (mergedConfig.enableTimeBasedAccess && mergedConfig.timeBasedRules) {\n224\t      if (!checkTimeBasedAccess(mergedConfig.timeBasedRules)) {\n225\t        securityEvents.push({\n226\t          type: \"time_based_access_denied\",\n227\t          timestamp: Date.now(),\n228\t          severity: \"warning\",\n229\t          message: \"Access denied due to time-based restrictions\",\n230\t        });\n231\t        await logSecurityEvent(c, auth, \"time_based_access_denied\", \"warning\");\n232\t        throw new HTTPException(403, {\n233\t          message: \"Access is not allowed at this time\",\n234\t        });\n235\t      }\n236\t    }\n237\t\n238\t    // 2. Location-based access control\n239\t    if (mergedConfig.enableLocationAccess && mergedConfig.locationRules) {\n240\t      if (!checkLocationAccess(clientIp, mergedConfig.locationRules)) {\n241\t        securityEvents.push({\n242\t          type: \"location_based_access_denied\",\n243\t          timestamp: Date.now(),\n244\t          severity: \"warning\",\n245\t          message: \"Access denied due to location restrictions\",\n246\t        });\n247\t        await logSecurityEvent(c, auth, \"location_based_access_denied\", \"warning\");\n248\t        throw new HTTPException(403, {\n249\t          message: \"Access is not allowed from this location\",\n250\t        });\n251\t      }\n252\t    }\n253\t\n254\t    // 3. Session risk assessment\n255\t    const session = c.get(\"session\") as AuthSession | undefined;\n256\t    if (session && auth.sessionId) {\n257\t      const riskAssessment = await assessSessionRisk(session, clientIp, userAgent);\n258\t      enhancedResult.riskAssessment = riskAssessment;\n259\t\n260\t      if (riskAssessment.riskScore >= (mergedConfig.riskThreshold || 70)) {\n261\t        securityEvents.push({\n262\t          type: \"high_risk_session\",\n263\t          timestamp: Date.now(),\n264\t          severity: \"error\",\n265\t          message: `High-risk session detected (score: ${riskAssessment.riskScore})`,\n266\t          details: { riskFactors: riskAssessment.riskFactors },\n267\t        });\n268\t\n269\t        if (mergedConfig.requireMfaForHighRisk && !auth.mfaVerified) {\n270\t          await logSecurityEvent(c, auth, \"mfa_required_high_risk\", \"warning\");\n271\t          enhancedResult.requiresAdditionalVerification = true;\n272\t        }\n273\t      }\n274\t\n275\t      // Record security event for risk tracking\n276\t      recordSecurityEvent(auth.sessionId, {\n277\t        type: \"session_risk_assessed\",\n278\t        timestamp: Date.now(),\n279\t        score: riskAssessment.riskScore,\n280\t        details: { riskLevel: riskAssessment.riskLevel },\n281\t      });\n282\t    }\n283\t\n284\t    // 4. Enhanced JWT security (if using JWT tokens)\n285\t    if (mergedConfig.enableEnhancedJwt) {\n286\t      const jwtToken = extractJwtFromRequest(c);\n287\t      if (jwtToken) {\n288\t        const compromiseDetection = detectTokenCompromise(jwtToken, {\n289\t          clientIp,\n290\t          userAgent,\n291\t          requestPath: c.req.path,\n292\t        });\n293\t\n294\t        enhancedResult.compromiseDetection = compromiseDetection;\n295\t\n296\t        if (compromiseDetection.compromised) {\n297\t          securityEvents.push({\n298\t            type: \"token_compromised\",\n299\t            timestamp: Date.now(),\n300\t            severity: \"critical\",\n301\t            message: \"Token compromise detected\",\n302\t            details: {\n303\t              riskScore: compromiseDetection.riskScore,\n304\t              riskFactors: compromiseDetection.riskFactors,\n305\t            },\n306\t          });\n307\t\n308\t          // Log token usage for pattern analysis\n309\t          recordTokenUsage(jwtToken, {\n310\t            usedAt: Date.now(),\n311\t            clientIp,\n312\t            userAgent,\n313\t            requestPath: c.req.path,\n314\t            suspicious: true,\n315\t            suspicionReason: compromiseDetection.riskFactors.join(\", \"),\n316\t          });\n317\t\n318\t          if (mergedConfig.onTokenCompromised === \"block\") {\n319\t            await logSecurityEvent(c, auth, \"token_compromised_blocked\", \"critical\");\n320\t            throw new HTTPException(403, {\n321\t              message: \"Token has been compromised. Please re-authenticate.\",\n322\t            });\n323\t          } else if (mergedConfig.onTokenCompromised === \"revoke\") {\n324\t            // Invalidate the session/token\n325\t            if (auth.sessionId) {\n326\t              // Session would be invalidated here\n327\t              await logSecurityEvent(c, auth, \"token_revoked_compromised\", \"critical\");\n328\t            }\n329\t          }\n330\t        } else {\n331\t          // Record normal token usage\n332\t          recordTokenUsage(jwtToken, {\n333\t            usedAt: Date.now(),\n334\t            clientIp,\n335\t            userAgent,\n336\t            requestPath: c.req.path,\n337\t            suspicious: false,\n338\t          });\n339\t        }\n340\t      }\n341\t    }\n342\t\n343\t    // 5. Behavioral analysis\n344\t    if (mergedConfig.enableBehavioralAnalysis) {\n345\t      const analysis = analyzeAccessBehavior(auth, c.req.path, clientIp);\n346\t      updateAccessPattern(auth.keyId, c.req.path, clientIp);\n347\t\n348\t      if (analysis.recommendedAction === \"block\") {\n349\t        securityEvents.push({\n350\t          type: \"behavioral_analysis_block\",\n351\t          timestamp: Date.now(),\n352\t          severity: \"error\",\n353\t          message: \"Access blocked due to suspicious behavior\",\n354\t          details: {\n355\t            riskScore: analysis.riskScore,\n356\t            riskFactors: analysis.riskFactors,\n357\t          },\n358\t        });\n359\t        await logSecurityEvent(c, auth, \"behavioral_block\", \"error\");\n360\t        throw new HTTPException(403, {\n361\t          message: \"Access temporarily blocked due to suspicious activity\",\n362\t        });\n363\t      } else if (analysis.recommendedAction === \"challenge\" && analysis.riskScore > 40) {\n364\t        enhancedResult.requiresAdditionalVerification = true;\n365\t        securityEvents.push({\n366\t          type: \"behavioral_challenge\",\n367\t          timestamp: Date.now(),\n368\t          severity: \"warning\",\n369\t          message: \"Additional verification recommended\",\n370\t          details: {\n371\t            riskScore: analysis.riskScore,\n372\t            riskFactors: analysis.riskFactors,\n373\t          },\n374\t        });\n375\t      }\n376\t    }\n377\t\n378\t    // 6. Concurrent session management\n379\t    if (mergedConfig.enableConcurrentSessions && session && auth.sessionId) {\n380\t      const deviceInfo: SessionDeviceInfo = {\n381\t        deviceId: deviceFingerprintFromUa(userAgent),\n382\t        deviceType: getDeviceTypeFromUa(userAgent),\n383\t        trusted: false, // Would be determined from stored device trust\n384\t      };\n385\t\n386\t      const conflictResult = registerSession(session, { deviceInfo }, mergedConfig.sessionConfig);\n387\t      enhancedResult.sessionConflict = conflictResult;\n388\t\n389\t      if (!conflictResult.allowed) {\n390\t        securityEvents.push({\n391\t          type: \"session_limit_exceeded\",\n392\t          timestamp: Date.now(),\n393\t          severity: \"warning\",\n394\t          message: \"Session limit exceeded\",\n395\t          details: {\n396\t            terminatedSessions: conflictResult.terminatedSessions,\n397\t            reason: conflictResult.reason,\n398\t          },\n399\t        });\n400\t        await logSecurityEvent(c, auth, \"session_limit_exceeded\", \"warning\");\n401\t        throw new HTTPException(403, {\n402\t          message: conflictResult.reason || \"Maximum concurrent sessions exceeded\",\n403\t        });\n404\t      }\n405\t\n406\t      // Update session activity\n407\t      updateSessionActivity(auth.sessionId);\n408\t\n409\t      if (conflictResult.terminatedSessions.length > 0) {\n410\t        securityEvents.push({\n411\t          type: \"session_terminated\",\n412\t          timestamp: Date.now(),\n413\t          severity: \"info\",\n414\t          message: \"Previous session terminated due to limit\",\n415\t          details: {\n416\t            terminatedSessions: conflictResult.terminatedSessions,\n417\t          },\n418\t        });\n419\t      }\n420\t    }\n421\t\n422\t    // 7. Check for security incidents\n423\t    const incidents = detectSecurityIncidents();\n424\t    if (incidents.length > 0) {\n425\t      enhancedResult.securityIncidents = incidents.map((i) => i.type);\n426\t      securityEvents.push({\n427\t        type: \"security_incidents_detected\",\n428\t        timestamp: Date.now(),\n429\t        severity: \"warning\",\n430\t        message: `${incidents.length} security incidents detected`,\n431\t        details: { incidents },\n432\t      });\n433\t    }\n434\t\n435\t    // 8. Structured audit logging\n436\t    if (mergedConfig.enableAuditLogging) {\n437\t      const outcome: AuditOutcome = enhancedResult.requiresAdditionalVerification\n438\t        ? \"partial\"\n439\t        : \"success\";\n440\t      const severity: AuditSeverity =\n441\t        enhancedResult.securityIncidents && enhancedResult.securityIncidents.length > 0\n442\t          ? \"warning\"\n443\t          : \"info\";\n444\t\n445\t      logAuditEventFromContext(c, {\n446\t        category: \"authentication\",\n447\t        severity,\n448\t        outcome,\n449\t        action: \"enhanced_auth\",\n450\t        details: {\n451\t          riskScore: enhancedResult.riskAssessment?.riskScore,\n452\t          compromiseDetected: enhancedResult.compromiseDetection?.compromised,\n453\t          sessionConflicts: enhancedResult.sessionConflict?.terminatedSessions.length,\n454\t          requiresVerification: enhancedResult.requiresAdditionalVerification,\n455\t          securityEvents,\n456\t        },\n457\t      });\n458\t    }\n459\t\n460\t    // Attach enhanced result to context\n461\t    c.set(\"enhancedAuth\", enhancedResult);\n462\t\n463\t    const duration = Date.now() - startTime;\n464\t    logger.debug(\"Enhanced authentication completed\", {\n465\t      keyId: auth.keyId,\n466\t      duration,\n467\t      securityEvents: securityEvents.length,\n468\t      riskScore: enhancedResult.riskAssessment?.riskScore,\n469\t    });\n470\t\n471\t    return next();\n472\t  };\n473\t}\n474\t\n475\t/**\n476\t * Require specific permissions with enhanced checking.\n477\t *\n478\t * Checks permissions using dynamic RBAC cache and logs results.\n479\t *\n480\t * @param permissions - Array of permissions to check\n481\t * @param options - Check options\n482\t * @returns Hono middleware handler\n483\t */\n484\texport function requirePermissions(\n485\t  permissions: string[],\n486\t  options: {\n487\t    /** Require all permissions (true) or any permission (false) */\n488\t    requireAll?: boolean;\n489\t    /** Resource ID for resource-specific permissions */\n490\t    resourceId?: string;\n491\t    /** Enhanced auth config */\n492\t    config?: EnhancedAuthConfig;\n493\t  } = {}\n494\t): MiddlewareHandler {\n495\t  const { requireAll = true, resourceId, config } = options;\n496\t\n497\t  return async (c, next) => {\n498\t    const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n499\t    if (!auth) {\n500\t      throw new HTTPException(401, { message: \"Authentication required\" });\n501\t    }\n502\t\n503\t    const mergedConfig = { ...DEFAULT_CONFIG, ...config };\n504\t    const permissionResults = new Map<string, PermissionCheckResult>();\n505\t\n506\t    // Check permissions\n507\t    for (const permission of permissions) {\n508\t      const result = checkPermission(auth.role || \"guest\", permission as Permission, {\n509\t        resourceId,\n510\t        userId: auth.keyId,\n511\t        config: mergedConfig.sessionConfig,\n512\t      });\n513\t      permissionResults.set(permission, result);\n514\t    }\n515\t\n516\t    // Determine if access is granted\n517\t    let granted: boolean;\n518\t    if (requireAll) {\n519\t      granted = Array.from(permissionResults.values()).every((r) => r.granted);\n520\t    } else {\n521\t      granted = Array.from(permissionResults.values()).some((r) => r.granted);\n522\t    }\n523\t\n524\t    if (!granted) {\n525\t      // Log authorization failure\n526\t      const deniedPermissions = Array.from(permissionResults.entries())\n527\t        .filter(([, r]) => !r.granted)\n528\t        .map(([p, r]) => `${p}: ${r.reason}`);\n529\t\n530\t      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"permission\", \"enhanced\");\n531\t\n532\t      if (mergedConfig.enableAuditLogging) {\n533\t        logAuditEventFromContext(c, {\n534\t          category: \"authorization\",\n535\t          severity: \"warning\",\n536\t          outcome: \"failure\",\n537\t          action: \"permission_check\",\n538\t          details: {\n539\t            permissions,\n540\t            requireAll,\n541\t            deniedPermissions,\n542\t          },\n543\t        });\n544\t      }\n545\t\n546\t      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n547\t        message: \"Insufficient permissions\",\n548\t      });\n549\t    }\n550\t\n551\t    // Attach permission results to context\n552\t    const enhancedResult = c.get(\"enhancedAuth\") as EnhancedAuthResult | undefined;\n553\t    if (enhancedResult) {\n554\t      enhancedResult.permissionResults = permissionResults;\n555\t    }\n556\t\n557\t    // Log authorization success\n558\t    if (mergedConfig.enableAuditLogging) {\n559\t      logAuditEventFromContext(c, {\n560\t        category: \"authorization\",\n561\t        severity: \"info\",\n562\t        outcome: \"success\",\n563\t        action: \"permission_check\",\n564\t        details: {\n565\t          permissions,\n566\t          requireAll,\n567\t        },\n568\t      });\n569\t    }\n570\t\n571\t    return next();\n572\t  };\n573\t}\n574\t\n575\t// ============================================================================\n576\t// Helper Functions\n577\t// ============================================================================\n578\t\n579\t/**\n580\t * Extract JWT token from request.\n581\t */\n582\tfunction extractJwtFromRequest(c: Context): string | null {\n583\t  const authHeader = c.req.header(\"Authorization\");\n584\t  if (authHeader?.startsWith(\"Bearer \")) {\n585\t    return authHeader.substring(7);\n586\t  }\n587\t\n588\t  const cookieHeader = c.req.header(\"Cookie\");\n589\t  if (cookieHeader) {\n590\t    const cookies = cookieHeader.split(\";\").map((c) => c.trim());\n591\t    for (const cookie of cookies) {\n592\t      if (cookie.startsWith(\"jwt=\") || cookie.startsWith(\"token=\")) {\n593\t        return cookie.substring(4);\n594\t      }\n595\t    }\n596\t  }\n597\t\n598\t  return null;\n599\t}\n600\t\n601\t/**\n602\t * Generate device fingerprint from user agent.\n603\t */\n604\tfunction deviceFingerprintFromUa(userAgent: string | undefined): string {\n605\t  if (!userAgent) return \"unknown\";\n606\t\n607\t  // Simple hash of user agent for device identification\n608\t  let hash = 0;\n609\t  for (let i = 0; i < userAgent.length; i++) {\n610\t    const char = userAgent.charCodeAt(i);\n611\t    hash = (hash << 5) - hash + char;\n612\t    hash = hash & hash;\n613\t  }\n614\t\n615\t  return Math.abs(hash).toString(16);\n616\t}\n617\t\n618\t/**\n619\t * Get device type from user agent.\n620\t */\n621\tfunction getDeviceTypeFromUa(\n622\t  userAgent: string | undefined\n623\t): \"mobile\" | \"tablet\" | \"desktop\" | \"unknown\" {\n624\t  if (!userAgent) return \"unknown\";\n625\t\n626\t  const ua = userAgent.toLowerCase();\n627\t\n628\t  if (/mobile|android|iphone|ipod|blackberry|iemobile|opera mini/i.test(ua)) {\n629\t    return \"mobile\";\n630\t  } else if (/tablet|ipad|playbook|silk/i.test(ua)) {\n631\t    return \"tablet\";\n632\t  } else if (ua.length > 0) {\n633\t    return \"desktop\";\n634\t  }\n635\t\n636\t  return \"unknown\";\n637\t}\n638\t\n639\t/**\n640\t * Log security event to audit log.\n641\t */\n642\tasync function logSecurityEvent(\n643\t  c: Context,\n644\t  auth: AuthContext,\n645\t  eventType: string,\n646\t  severity: \"info\" | \"warning\" | \"error\" | \"critical\"\n647\t): Promise<void> {\n648\t  logAuditEventFromContext(c, {\n649\t    category: \"security\",\n650\t    severity,\n651\t    outcome: severity === \"error\" || severity === \"critical\" ? \"failure\" : \"success\",\n652\t    action: eventType,\n653\t    details: {\n654\t      keyId: auth.keyId,\n655\t      sessionId: auth.sessionId,\n656\t      scope: auth.scope,\n657\t      role: auth.role,\n658\t    },\n659\t  });\n660\t}\n661\t\n662\t/**\n663\t * Get enhanced auth result from context.\n664\t */\n665\texport function getEnhancedAuth(c: Context): EnhancedAuthResult | undefined {\n666\t  return c.get(\"enhancedAuth\");\n667\t}\n668\t\n669\t/**\n670\t * Check if additional verification is required.\n671\t */\n672\texport function requiresAdditionalVerification(c: Context): boolean {\n673\t  const enhancedAuth = getEnhancedAuth(c);\n674\t  return enhancedAuth?.requiresAdditionalVerification ?? false;\n675\t}\n676\t\n677\t/**\n678\t * Get security incidents for current request.\n679\t */\n680\texport function getSecurityIncidents(c: Context): string[] {\n681\t  const enhancedAuth = getEnhancedAuth(c);\n682\t  return enhancedAuth?.securityIncidents || [];\n683\t}\n684\t\n685\t// ============================================================================\n686\t// Utility Functions\n687\t// ============================================================================\n688\t\n689\t/**\n690\t * Invalidate all cached data for a user.\n691\t * Call this when permissions, roles, or sessions change.\n692\t *\n693\t * @param keyId - API key ID\n694\t */\n695\texport function invalidateUserAuthData(keyId: string): void {\n696\t  invalidateUserCache(keyId);\n697\t  logger.info(\"User auth data invalidated\", { keyId });\n698\t}\n699\t\n700\t/**\n701\t * Get comprehensive security status for a user.\n702\t *\n703\t * @param keyId - API key ID\n704\t * @returns Security status information\n705\t */\n706\texport function getUserSecurityStatus(keyId: string): {\n707\t  sessionCount: number;\n708\t  activeIncidents: number;\n709\t  riskFactors: string[];\n710\t} {\n711\t  // This would query the various security modules\n712\t  // For now, return a placeholder\n713\t  return {\n714\t    sessionCount: getUserSessionCount(keyId),\n715\t    activeIncidents: detectSecurityIncidents().filter((i) =>\n716\t      i.events.some((e) => e.actor.keyId === keyId)\n717\t    ).length,\n718\t    riskFactors: [],\n719\t  };\n720\t}\n721\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"19b864e5-f5f9-4747-afef-54b401f04266","uuid":"bd94982c-a810-4212-b1f1-6f38e0ff98ab","timestamp":"2026-05-09T05:24:58.397Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/mta-my-way/packages/server/src/middleware/enhanced-authentication.ts","content":"/**\n * Enhanced Authentication Middleware\n *\n * Provides comprehensive authentication by integrating multiple security modules:\n * - JWT validation with replay protection\n * - Enhanced JWT security with device fingerprinting and compromise detection\n * - Concurrent session management with conflict resolution\n * - Dynamic RBAC permission caching\n * - Structured audit logging for compliance\n * - Time-based and location-based access control\n * - Behavioral analysis for anomaly detection\n *\n * This middleware combines all security features into a single, easy-to-use\n * authentication system that provides defense in depth.\n */\n\nimport type { Context, MiddlewareHandler } from \"hono\";\nimport { HTTPException } from \"hono/http-exception\";\nimport { logger } from \"../observability/logger.js\";\nimport { type AuthContext, type AuthSession, getAuthContext } from \"./authentication.js\";\nimport type { Permission } from \"./authentication.js\";\nimport {\n  type
