};\n602\t}\n603\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"19b864e5-f5f9-4747-afef-54b401f04266","uuid":"28af554a-8ef1-4c77-8dae-56687e586946","timestamp":"2026-05-09T05:25:59.658Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/mta-my-way/packages/server/src/middleware/authorization-security.ts","content":"/**\n * Enhanced Authorization Security Module\n *\n * Provides additional security layers for authorization including:\n * - Time-based access control\n * - Location-based access control\n * - Behavioral analysis for anomaly detection\n * - Advanced session security\n * - Resource access pattern analysis\n */\n\nimport type { Context } from \"hono\";\nimport { HTTPException } from \"hono/http-exception\";\nimport { logger } from \"../observability/logger.js\";\nimport { type AuthContext, getAuthContext } from \"./authentication.js\";\nimport { securityLogger } from \"./security-logging.js\";\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Types and Interfaces\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Time-based access control rules.\n */\nexport interface TimeBasedAccessRule {\n  /** Days of week when access is allowed (0-6, 0 = Sunday) */\n  allowedDays?: number[];\n  /** Hour range when access is allowed (0-23) */\n  allowedHours?: { start: number; end: number };\n  /** Timezone for time-based rules (default: UTC) */\n  timezone?: string;\n  /** Exceptions to the rules (specific dates when access is allowed/blocked) */\n  exceptions?: Array<{\n    date: string; // ISO date string\n    allowed: boolean;\n  }>;\n}\n\n/**\n * Location-based access control rules.\n */\nexport interface LocationAccessRule {\n  /** Allowed country codes (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2) */\n  allowedCountries?: string[];\n  /** Blocked country codes */\n  blockedCountries?: string[];\n  /** Require VPN/private connection */\n  requirePrivateConnection?: boolean;\n  /** Trusted IP ranges */\n  trustedIpRanges?: Array<{ ip: string; prefixLength: number }>;\n}\n\n/**\n * Behavioral analysis result.\n */\nexport interface BehavioralAnalysis {\n  /** Risk score (0-100) */\n  riskScore: number;\n  /** Risk level */\n  riskLevel: \"low\" | \"medium\" | \"high\" | \"critical\";\n  /** Risk factors identified */\n  riskFactors: string[];\n  /** Recommended action */\n  recommendedAction: \"allow\" | \"challenge\" | \"block\";\n}\n\n/**\n * Resource access pattern for anomaly detection.\n */\nexport interface AccessPattern {\n  /** Resource type */\n  resourceType: string;\n  /** Typical access count per hour */\n  typicalAccessCount: number;\n  /** Standard deviation */\n  stdDev: number;\n  /** Last access timestamp */\n  lastAccessAt: number;\n  /** Access locations (IPs) */\n  accessLocations: Set<string>;\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Time-Based Access Control\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Check if current time allows access based on time-based rules.\n *\n * @param rules - Time-based access rules\n * @returns true if access is allowed, false otherwise\n */\nexport function checkTimeBasedAccess(rules: TimeBasedAccessRule): boolean {\n  const now = new Date();\n  const timezone = rules.timezone || \"UTC\";\n\n  // Get current time in specified timezone\n  const currentTime = new Date(now.toLocaleString(\"en-US\", { timeZone: timezone }));\n\n  const currentDay = currentTime.getDay();\n  const currentHour = currentTime.getHours();\n\n  // Check exceptions first\n  if (rules.exceptions) {\n    const currentDateStr = now.toISOString().split(\"T\")[0];\n    const exception = rules.exceptions.find((e) => e.date === currentDateStr);\n    if (exception) {\n      return exception.allowed;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Check allowed days\n  if (rules.allowedDays && !rules.allowedDays.includes(currentDay)) {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  // Check allowed hours\n  if (rules.allowedHours) {\n    const { start, end } = rules.allowedHours;\n    if (end < start) {\n      // Range wraps around midnight (e.g., 22:00 - 06:00)\n      if (currentHour < start && currentHour > end) {\n        return false;\n      }\n    } else {\n      // Normal range (e.g., 09:00 - 17:00)\n      if (currentHour < start || currentHour > end) {\n        return false;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  return true;\n}\n\n/**\n * Create middleware for time-based access control.\n *\n * @param rules - Time-based access rules\n */\nexport function requireTimeBasedAccess(rules: TimeBasedAccessRule) {\n  return async (c: Context, next: () => Promise<void>) => {\n    if (!checkTimeBasedAccess(rules)) {\n      securityLogger.logSuspiciousActivity(\n        c,\n        \"time_based_access_denied\",\n        \"Access denied due to time-based restrictions\"\n      );\n      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n        message: \"Access is not allowed at this time\",\n      });\n    }\n    return next();\n  };\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Location-Based Access Control\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Extract country code from IP address (simplified).\n * In production, use a proper GeoIP database.\n *\n * @param ip - IP address\n * @returns Country code or null\n */\nfunction extractCountryFromIp(_ip: string): string | null {\n  // Simplified implementation - in production, use GeoIP database\n  // This is a placeholder that returns null (no location data)\n  return null;\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if IP address is in private range.\n *\n * @param ip - IP address\n * @returns true if private IP\n */\nfunction isPrivateIp(ip: string): boolean {\n  const parts = ip.split(\".\");\n  if (parts.length !== 4) return false;\n\n  const [first, second] = parts.map((p) => parseInt(p, 10));\n\n  // 10.0.0.0/8\n  if (first === 10) return true;\n\n  // 172.16.0.0/12\n  if (first === 172 && second !== undefined && second >= 16 && second <= 31) {\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  // 192.168.0.0/16\n  if (first === 192 && second === 168) return true;\n\n  return false;\n}\n\n/**\n * Check IP against trusted ranges.\n *\n * @param ip - IP address to check\n * @param trustedRanges - Trusted IP ranges\n * @returns true if IP is in trusted range\n */\nfunction isIpInTrustedRange(\n  ip: string,\n  trustedRanges: Array<{ ip: string; prefixLength: number }>\n): boolean {\n  for (const range of trustedRanges) {\n    const ipParts = ip.split(\".\").map((p) => parseInt(p, 10));\n    const rangeIpParts = range.ip.split(\".\").map((p) => parseInt(p, 10));\n\n    if (ipParts.length !== 4 || rangeIpParts.length !== 4) continue;\n\n    const octetsToCheck = Math.floor(range.prefixLength / 8);\n    let match = true;\n\n    for (let i = 0; i < octetsToCheck && i < 4; i++) {\n      if (ipParts[i] !== rangeIpParts[i]) {\n        match = false;\n        break;\n      }\n    }\n\n    if (match) return true;\n  }\n\n  return false;\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if location allows access based on location-based rules.\n *\n * @param clientIp - Client IP address\n * @param rules - Location-based access rules\n * @returns true if access is allowed, false otherwise\n */\nexport function checkLocationAccess(clientIp: string, rules: LocationAccessRule): boolean {\n  // Check private connection requirement\n  if (rules.requirePrivateConnection && !isPrivateIp(clientIp)) {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  // Check trusted IP ranges\n  if (rules.trustedIpRanges && isIpInTrustedRange(clientIp, rules.trustedIpRanges)) {\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  // Check country restrictions (if GeoIP is available)\n  const country = extractCountryFromIp(clientIp);\n  if (country) {\n    if (rules.blockedCountries && rules.blockedCountries.includes(country)) {\n      return false;\n    }\n\n    if (rules.allowedCountries && !rules.allowedCountries.includes(country)) {\n      return false;\n    }\n  }\n\n  return true;\n}\n\n/**\n * Create middleware for location-based access control.\n *\n * @param rules - Location-based access rules\n */\nexport function requireLocationAccess(rules: LocationAccessRule) {\n  return async (c: Context, next: () => Promise<void>) => {\n    const clientIp =\n      c.req.header(\"CF-Connecting-IP\") ||\n      c.req.header(\"X-Forwarded-For\")?.split(\",\")[0]?.trim() ||\n      c.req.header(\"X-Real-IP\") ||\n      \"unknown\";\n\n    if (!checkLocationAccess(clientIp, rules)) {\n      securityLogger.logSuspiciousActivity(\n        c,\n        \"location_based_access_denied\",\n        \"Access denied due to location restrictions\"\n      );\n      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n        message: \"Access is not allowed from this location\",\n      });\n    }\n    return next();\n  };\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Behavioral Analysis\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Access pattern tracking for anomaly detection.\n */\nconst accessPatterns = new Map<string, AccessPattern>();\n\n/**\n * Analyze access behavior for anomalies.\n *\n * @param auth - Authentication context\n * @param resourceType - Resource type being accessed\n * @param clientIp - Client IP address\n * @returns Behavioral analysis result\n */\nexport function analyzeAccessBehavior(\n  auth: AuthContext,\n  resourceType: string,\n  clientIp: string\n): BehavioralAnalysis {\n  const riskFactors: string[] = [];\n  let riskScore = 0;\n\n  const key = `${auth.keyId}:${resourceType}`;\n  const pattern = accessPatterns.get(key);\n\n  if (!pattern) {\n    // First access - low risk\n    return {\n      riskScore: 0,\n      riskLevel: \"low\",\n      riskFactors: [],\n      recommendedAction: \"allow\",\n    };\n  }\n\n  // Check access frequency\n  const now = Date.now();\n  const timeSinceLastAccess = now - pattern.lastAccessAt;\n  const accessCountPerHour = pattern.typicalAccessCount;\n\n  // If accessing much more frequently than normal\n  if (timeSinceLastAccess < 1000 && accessCountPerHour > 100) {\n    riskScore += 30;\n    riskFactors.push(\"Unusually high access frequency\");\n  }\n\n  // Check for new location\n  if (!pattern.accessLocations.has(clientIp)) {\n    // Accessing from a new location\n    const locationCount = pattern.accessLocations.size;\n    if (locationCount > 5) {\n      riskScore += 20;\n      riskFactors.push(\"Access from many different locations\");\n    } else {\n      riskScore += 10;\n      riskFactors.push(\"Access from new location\");\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Determine risk level and action\n  let riskLevel: \"low\" | \"medium\" | \"high\" | \"critical\";\n  let recommendedAction: \"allow\" | \"challenge\" | \"block\";\n\n  if (riskScore < 20) {\n    riskLevel = \"low\";\n    recommendedAction = \"allow\";\n  } else if (riskScore < 50) {\n    riskLevel = \"medium\";\n    recommendedAction = \"allow\";\n  } else if (riskScore < 80) {\n    riskLevel = \"high\";\n    recommendedAction = \"challenge\";\n  } else {\n    riskLevel = \"critical\";\n    recommendedAction = \"block\";\n  }\n\n  return {\n    riskScore,\n    riskLevel,\n    riskFactors,\n    recommendedAction,\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Update access pattern tracking.\n *\n * @param keyId - API key ID\n * @param resourceType - Resource type\n * @param clientIp - Client IP address\n */\nexport function updateAccessPattern(keyId: string, resourceType: string, clientIp: string): void {\n  const key = `${keyId}:${resourceType}`;\n  const pattern = accessPatterns.get(key);\n\n  if (!pattern) {\n    accessPatterns.set(key, {\n      resourceType,\n      typicalAccessCount: 1,\n      stdDev: 0,\n      lastAccessAt: Date.now(),\n      accessLocations: new Set([clientIp]),\n    });\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Update access pattern with exponential moving average\n  const alpha = 0.1; // Smoothing factor\n  const newCount = pattern.typicalAccessCount + 1;\n  const oldMean = pattern.typicalAccessCount;\n\n  pattern.typicalAccessCount = alpha * newCount + (1 - alpha) * oldMean;\n  pattern.lastAccessAt = Date.now();\n  pattern.accessLocations.add(clientIp);\n\n  accessPatterns.set(key, pattern);\n}\n\n/**\n * Create middleware for behavioral analysis.\n *\n * @param resourceType - Resource type to analyze\n * @param options - Analysis options\n */\nexport function analyzeBehavior(\n  resourceType: string,\n  options: {\n    /** Action to take on high risk */\n    onHighRisk?: \"block\" | \"challenge\" | \"allow\";\n    /** Whether to update patterns */\n    updatePatterns?: boolean;\n  } = {}\n) {\n  const { onHighRisk = \"block\", updatePatterns = true } = options;\n\n  return async (c: Context, next: () => Promise<void>) => {\n    const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n    if (!auth) {\n      return next();\n    }\n\n    const clientIp =\n      c.req.header(\"CF-Connecting-IP\") ||\n      c.req.header(\"X-Forwarded-For\")?.split(\",\")[0]?.trim() ||\n      c.req.header(\"X-Real-IP\") ||\n      \"unknown\";\n\n    const analysis = analyzeAccessBehavior(auth, resourceType, clientIp);\n\n    if (updatePatterns) {\n      updateAccessPattern(auth.keyId, resourceType, clientIp);\n    }\n\n    // Take action based on risk level\n    if (analysis.riskLevel === \"critical\" && onHighRisk === \"block\") {\n      securityLogger.logSuspiciousActivity(\n        c,\n        \"behavioral_analysis_block\",\n        `Access blocked: ${analysis.riskFactors.join(\", \")}`\n      );\n      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n        message: \"Access temporarily blocked due to suspicious activity\",\n      });\n    }\n\n    if (analysis.riskLevel === \"high\" && onHighRisk === \"challenge\") {\n      // Set a flag for additional verification\n      c.set(\"requireAdditionalVerification\", true);\n      logger.info(\"High-risk access detected - additional verification recommended\", {\n        keyId: auth.keyId,\n        resourceType,\n        riskFactors: analysis.riskFactors,\n      });\n    }\n\n    return next();\n  };\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Session Security Enhancements\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Session security configuration.\n */\nexport interface SessionSecurityConfig {\n  /** Maximum concurrent sessions per user */\n  maxConcurrentSessions?: number;\n  /** Whether to enforce IP binding */\n  enforceIpBinding?: boolean;\n  /** Whether to enforce device binding */\n  enforceDeviceBinding?: boolean;\n  /** Session inactivity timeout in milliseconds */\n  inactivityTimeout?: number;\n  /** Whether to check for concurrent sessions from different locations */\n  checkConcurrentLocations?: boolean;\n}\n\n/**\n * Default session security configuration.\n */\nconst DEFAULT_SESSION_SECURITY: SessionSecurityConfig = {\n  maxConcurrentSessions: 5,\n  enforceIpBinding: true,\n  enforceDeviceBinding: false,\n  inactivityTimeout: 30 * 60 * 1000, // 30 minutes\n  checkConcurrentLocations: true,\n};\n\n/**\n * Enhanced session security check.\n *\n * Performs comprehensive session security validation.\n *\n * @param auth - Authentication context\n * @param clientIp - Client IP address\n * @param userAgent - User agent string\n * @param config - Security configuration\n * @returns Object indicating if session is secure and any issues found\n */\nexport function checkSessionSecurity(\n  auth: AuthContext,\n  clientIp: string,\n  userAgent: string | undefined,\n  config: SessionSecurityConfig = {}\n): { secure: boolean; issues: string[]; recommendations: string[] } {\n  const mergedConfig = { ...DEFAULT_SESSION_SECURITY, ...config };\n  const issues: string[] = [];\n  const recommendations: string[] = [];\n\n  // Check if session exists\n  if (!auth.sessionId) {\n    return {\n      secure: false,\n      issues: [\"No active session\"],\n      recommendations: [\"Authenticate to establish a session\"],\n    };\n  }\n\n  // Note: These checks would be integrated with the actual session storage\n  // This is a framework for the security checks\n\n  if (mergedConfig.enforceIpBinding) {\n    // IP binding would be checked against stored session IP\n    recommendations.push(\"IP binding is enforced for this session\");\n  }\n\n  if (mergedConfig.enforceDeviceBinding) {\n    // Device binding would be checked against stored device fingerprint\n    recommendations.push(\"Device binding is enforced for this session\");\n  }\n\n  return {\n    secure: issues.length === 0,\n    issues,\n    recommendations,\n  };\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Utility Functions\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Clear access pattern tracking for a key.\n *\n * @param keyId - API key ID\n */\nexport function clearAccessPatterns(keyId?: string): void {\n  if (keyId) {\n    for (const [key] of accessPatterns.entries()) {\n      if (key.startsWith(`${keyId}:`)) {\n        accessPatterns.delete(key);\n      }\n    }\n  } else {\n    accessPatterns.clear();\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Get access pattern statistics.\n *\n * @returns Statistics about tracked access patterns\n */\nexport function getAccessPatternStats(): {\n  totalPatterns: number;\n  patternsByResourceType: Record<string, number>;\n  averageAccessCount: number;\n} {\n  let totalAccessCount = 0;\n  const patternsByResourceType: Record<string, number> = {};\n\n  for (const pattern of accessPatterns.values()) {\n    totalAccessCount += pattern.typicalAccessCount;\n    patternsByResourceType[pattern.resourceType] =\n      (patternsByResourceType[pattern.resourceType] || 0) + 1;\n  }\n\n  return {\n    totalPatterns: accessPatterns.size,\n    patternsByResourceType,\n    averageAccessCount: accessPatterns.size > 0 ? totalAccessCount / accessPatterns.size : 0,\n  };\n}\n","numLines":603,"startLine":1,"totalLines":603}}}

* Security event logging middleware for Hono.\n *\n * Provides centralized logging for security-related events including:\n * - Authentication failures\n * - Authorization failures\n * - Rate limit exceeded\n * - Input validation failures\n * - Suspicious request patterns\n * - Blocked attacks\n */\n\nimport type { Context, MiddlewareHandler } from \"hono\";\n\n/**\n * Security event severity levels.\n */\ntype SecuritySeverity = \"low\" | \"medium\" | \"high\" | \"critical\";\n\n/**\n * Security event types.\n */\ntype SecurityEventType =\n  | \"auth_failure\"\n  | \"authz_failure\"\n  | \"rate_limit_exceeded\"\n  | \"input_validation_failed\"\n  | \"path_traversal_blocked\"\n  | \"hpp_blocked\"\n  | \"suspicious_request\"\n  | \"blocked_attack\"\n  | \"data_exfiltration_attempt\"\n  | \"unusual_activity\";\n\n/**\n * Security event log entry.\n */\ninterface SecurityEvent {\n  event: SecurityEventType;\n  timestamp: string;\n  severity: SecuritySeverity;\n  ip?: string;\n  userAgent?: string;\n  method?: string;\n  path?: string;\n  statusCode?: number;\n  details?: Record<string, unknown>;\n}\n\n/**\n * Security event logger options.\n */\ninterface SecurityLoggerOptions {\n  /** Minimum severity to log (default: \"low\") */\n  minSeverity?: SecuritySeverity;\n  /** Include user agent in logs (default: true) */\n  includeUserAgent?: boolean;\n  /** Include full request path (default: true, set false for PII) */\n  includePath?: boolean;\n  /** Custom log function (default: console.warn) */\n  logFn?: (event: SecurityEvent) => void;\n  /** Sanitize PII from paths (default: true) */\n  sanitizePII?: boolean;\n}\n\n/**\n * Default log function - outputs to console.warn for security events.\n */\nfunction defaultLogFn(event: SecurityEvent): void {\n  console.warn(JSON.stringify(event));\n}\n\n/**\n * Extract client IP address from request headers.\n *\n * Checks Cloudflare connecting IP, X-Forwarded-For, and falls back\n * to a generic identifier.\n */\nfunction extractClientIp(c: Context): string {\n  // Handle cases where context might be incomplete (e.g., utility functions)\n  if (!c?.req?.header) return \"unknown\";\n  return (\n    c.req.header(\"CF-Connecting-IP\") ||\n    c.req.header(\"X-Forwarded-For\")?.split(\",\")[0]?.trim() ||\n    c.req.header(\"X-Real-IP\") ||\n    \"unknown\"\n  );\n}\n\n/**\n * Extract user agent from request headers.\n */\nfunction extractUserAgent(c: Context): string | undefined {\n  // Handle cases where context might be incomplete (e.g., utility functions)\n  if (!c?.req?.header) return undefined;\n  return c.req.header(\"User-Agent\") || undefined;\n}\n\n/**\n * Sanitize PII from request path.\n *\n * Removes potential PII like email addresses, phone numbers, and IDs\n * from the path before logging.\n */\nfunction sanitizePathForLogging(path: string | undefined): string {\n  if (!path) {\n    return \":unknown\";\n  }\n  // Remove potential IDs (UUIDs, numbers)\n  let sanitized = path.replace(\n    /[0-9a-f]{8}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{12}/gi,\n    \":id\"\n  );\n  sanitized = * Severity order for comparison.\n */\nconst SEVERITY_ORDER: Record<SecuritySeverity, number> = {\n  low: 1,\n  medium: 2,\n  high: 3,\n  critical: 4,\n};\n\n/**\n * Check if a severity meets the minimum threshold.\n */\nfunction meetsMinSeverity(severity: SecuritySeverity, min: SecuritySeverity): boolean {\n  return SEVERITY_ORDER[severity] >= SEVERITY_ORDER[min];\n}\n\n/**\n * Security event logger class.\n *\n * Provides methods to log different types of security events.\n */\nexport class SecurityEventLogger {\n  private options: Required<SecurityLoggerOptions>;\n\n  constructor(options: SecurityLoggerOptions = {}) {\n    this.options = {\n      minSeverity: options.minSeverity ?? \"low\",\n      includeUserAgent: options.includeUserAgent ?? true,\n      includePath: options.includePath ?? true,\n      logFn: options.logFn ?? defaultLogFn,\n      sanitizePII: options.sanitizePII ?? true,\n    };\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Log a security event.\n   */\n  log(\n    type: SecurityEventType,\n    severity: SecuritySeverity,\n    context: Context,\n    details?: Record<string, unknown>\n  ): void {\n    if (!meetsMinSeverity(severity, this.options.minSeverity)) {\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const event: SecurityEvent = {\n      event: type,\n      timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),\n      severity,\n    };\n\n    if (this.options.includeUserAgent) {\n      event.userAgent = extractUserAgent(context);\n    }\n\n    event.ip = extractClientIp(context);\n\n    // Handle cases where context might be incomplete (e.g., utility functions)\n    if (context?.req) {\n      event.method = context.req.method;\n      if (this.options.includePath) {\n        event.path = this.options.sanitizePII\n          ? sanitizePathForLogging(context.req.path)\n          : context.req.path;\n      }\n    }\n    if (context?.res) {\n      event.statusCode = context.res.status;\n    }\n\n    if (details) {\n      event.details = details;\n    }\n\n    this.options.logFn(event);\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Log authentication failure.\n   */\n  logAuthFailure(c: Context, reason: string): void {\n    this.log(\"auth_failure\", \"high\", c, { reason });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Log authorization failure.\n   */\n  logAuthzFailure(c: Context, resource: string, action: string): void {\n    this.log(\"authz_failure\", \"medium\", c, { resource, action });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Log rate limit exceeded.\n   */\n  logRateLimitExceeded(c: Context, limit: number, window: number): void {\n    this.log(\"rate_limit_exceeded\", \"medium\", c, { limit, window });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Log input validation failure.\n   */\n  logInputValidationFailure(c: Context, field: string, reason: string): void {\n    this.log(\"path_traversal_blocked\", \"high\", c, {\n      detectedPath: this.options.sanitizePII ? sanitizePathForLogging(path) : path,\n    });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Log HTTP parameter pollution attempt blocked.\n   */\n  logHPPBlocked(c: Context, parameters: string[]): void {\n    this.log(\"hpp_blocked\", \"medium\", c, { parameters });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Log suspicious request pattern.\n   */\n  logSuspiciousRequest(c: Context, pattern: string, description: string): void {\n    this.log(\"suspicious_request\", \"medium\", c, { pattern, description });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Log blocked attack.\n   */\n  logBlockedAttack(c: Context, attackType: string, description: string): void {\n    this.log(\"blocked_attack\", \"critical\", c, { attackType, description });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Log data exfiltration attempt.\n   */\n  logDataExfiltrationAttempt(c: Context, method: string, target: string): void {\n    this.log(\"data_exfiltration_attempt\", \"critical\", c, { method, target });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Log unusual activity.\n   */\n  logUnusualActivity(c: Context, activityType: string, description: string): void {\n    this.log(\"unusual_activity\", \"low\", c, { activityType, description });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Log suspicious activity with severity level.\n   */\n  logSuspiciousActivity(\n    c: Context,\n    activityType: string,\n    description: string,\n    severity: SecuritySeverity = \"medium\"\n  ): void {\n    this.log(\"suspicious_request\", severity, c, { activityType, description });\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Global default security event logger.\n */\nconst defaultLogger = new SecurityEventLogger();\n\n/**\n * Security event logging middleware factory.\n *\n * Creates a middleware that logs security events based on response\n * status codes and other indicators.\n *\n * This middleware automatically logs:\n * - 4xx client errors (potential attacks)\n * - 5xx server errors (potential issues)\n * - Requests with suspicious patterns\n */\nexport function securityLogging(options: SecurityLoggerOptions = {}): MiddlewareHandler {\n  const logger = new SecurityEventLogger(options);\n\n  return async (c, next) => {\n    await next();\n\n    // Log based on response status\n    const status = c.res.status;\n\n    if (status >= 400 && status < 500) {\n      // Client errors - may indicate attack attempts\n      if (status === 401 || status === 403) {\n        logger.logAuthFailure(c, `HTTP ${status}`);\n      } else if (status === 429) {\n        logger.logRateLimitExceeded(c, 0, 0);\n      } else if (status === 400) {\n        logger.logInputValidationFailure(c, \"request\", \"Bad Request\");\n      }\n    } else if (status >= 500) {\n      // Server errors - may indicate issues\n   .map((b) => b.toString(16).padStart(2, \"0\"))\n112\t      .join(\"\");\n113\t\n114\t  const encoder = new TextEncoder();\n115\t  const keyMaterial = await crypto.subtle.importKey(\n116\t    \"raw\",\n117\t    encoder.encode(apiKey),\n118\t    { name: \"PBKDF2\" },\n119\t    false,\n120\t    [\"deriveBits\"]\n121\t  );\n122\t\n123\t  // Use 600,000 iterations per OWASP 2024 attacks.\n144\t * Uses crypto.subtle.timingSafeEqual if available, falls back to constant-time comparison.\n145\t */\n146\tfunction timingSafeEqual(a: string, b: string): boolean {\n147\t  // Ensure encoder.encode(b);\n157\t    try {\n158\t      return crypto.subtle.timingSafeEqual(bufferA, bufferB);\n159\t    } catch {\n160\t      // Fall through to manual implementation if timingSafeEqual fails\n161\t    }\n162\t  }\n163\t\n164\t  // Manual constant-time comparison\n165\t  let result = 0;\n166\t  for (let i = 0; i < a.length; i++) {\n167\t    result |= a.charCodeAt(i) ^ b.charCodeAt(i);\n168\t  }\n169\t  return result === 0;\n170\t}\n171\t\n172\t/**\n173\t * Verify an API key against a hash using timing-safe comparison.\n174\t */\n175\texport async function * Authentication and authorization middleware for Hono.\n *\n * Provides:\n * - API key authentication with proper cryptographic hashing\n * - Request signature verification (HMAC)\n * - Session management with secure session regeneration\n * - Role-based access control\n * - Authentication token management\n * - Account lockout protection\n * - CSRF protection\n * - Audit logging for privileged operations\n * - Password policy validation (via password-management module)\n * - Refresh token mechanism for secure session renewal\n * - Sliding session expiration\n * - OAuth 2.0 framework for third-party integration\n * - TOTP-based multi-factor authentication (MFA)\n * - Enhanced session security (IP binding, device tracking)\n * - Concurrent session management\n *\n * Password-related functions are re-exported from password-management.ts\n * for enhanced security including breach detection, expiration, and history.\n */\n\nimport type { Context, MiddlewareHandler } from \"hono\";\nimport { HTTPException } from \"hono/http-exception\";\nimport { logger } from \"../observability/logger.js\";\nimport { getRolePermissions } from \"./roles.js\";\nimport {\n  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\n  sanitizeObject,\n  sanitizeStringSimple,\n  validateApiKeyFormat,\n  validateSessionTokenFormat,\n  validateSignatureFormat,\n} from \"./sanitization.js\";\nimport { securityLogger } from \"./security-logging.js\";\nimport {\n  type EncryptedData,\n  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\n  configureEncryption,\n  decryptToken,\n  encryptToken,\n  generateTokenFingerprint,\n  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\n  hashToken,\n  isEncryptionConfigured,\n} from \"./token-encryption.js\";\n\n// Re-export password management functions for backward compatibility\nexport {\n  validatePassword,\n  hashPassword,\n  verifyPasswordHash,\n  storePasswordInHistory,\n  getPasswordHistory,\n  clearPasswordHistory,\n  generatePasswordResetToken,\n  validatePasswordResetToken,\n  consumePasswordResetToken,\n  invalidateResetTokensForKey,\n  isPasswordExpired,\n  isPasswordInGracePeriod,\n  getDaysInGracePeriod,\n  getDaysUntilExpiration,\n  shouldWarnPasswordExpiration,\n  generateSecurePassword,\n  getPasswordPolicyDescription,\n  getPasswordPolicyForRole,\n  getExpirationDaysForRole,\n  _setTokenExpirationForTesting,\n  type PasswordPolicy,\n  type PasswordValidationResult,\n  type PasswordHash,\n  type PasswordResetToken,\n  type PasswordHistoryEntry,\n} from \"./password-management.js\";\n\n// ============================================================================\n// API Key Generation and Cryptographic Hashing\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Generate a cryptographically secure random API key.\n * Uses 32 bytes (256 bits) of entropy.\n */\nexport async function generateApiKey(): Promise<string> {\n  const array = new Uint8Array(32);\n  crypto.getRandomValues(array);\n  return Array.from(array, (b) => b.toString(16).padStart(2, \"0\")).join(\"\");\n}\n\n/**\n * Hash an API key using PBKDF2 with SHA-256.\n *\n * Updated to use OWASP 2024 recommended 600,000 iterations for API keys.\n * Note: For user passwords, use hashPassword() from password-management.ts\n * which includes additional security features.\n *\n * @param apiKey - The API key to hash\n * @param salt - Optional salt (generated if not provided, 32 bytes)\n * @returns Promise containing the hash and salt\n */\nexport async function hashApiKey(\n  apiKey: string,\n  salt?: string\n): Promise<{ hash: string; salt: string }> {\n  const actualSalt =\n    salt ||\n    Array.from(crypto.getRandomValues(new Uint8Array(32)))\n      .map((b) => b.toString(16).padStart(2, \"0\"))\n      .join(\"\");\n\n  const encoder = new TextEncoder();\n  const keyMaterial = await crypto.subtle.importKey(\n    \"raw\",\n    encoder.encode(apiKey),\n    { name: \"PBKDF2\" },\n    false,\n    [\"deriveBits\"]\n  );\n\n  // Use 600,000 iterations per OWASP 2024 recommendations\n  const hashBuffer = await crypto.subtle.deriveBits(\n    {\n      name: \"PBKDF2\",\n      salt: encoder.encode(actualSalt),\n      iterations: 600_000,\n      hash: \"SHA-256\",\n    },\n    keyMaterial,\n    256\n  );\n\n  const hash = Array.from(new Uint8Array(hashBuffer))\n    .map((b) => b.toString(16).padStart(2, \"0\"))\n    .join(\"\");\n\n  return { hash, salt: actualSalt };\n}\n\n/**\n * Timing-safe string comparison to prevent timing attacks.\n * Uses crypto.subtle.timingSafeEqual if available, falls back to constant-time comparison.\n */\nfunction timingSafeEqual(a: string, b: string): boolean {\n  // Ensure strings have the same length\n  if (a.length !== b.length) {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  // Use Web Crypto API for timing-safe comparison if available\n  if (typeof crypto !== \"undefined\" && crypto.subtle && crypto.subtle.timingSafeEqual) {\n    const encoder = new TextEncoder();\n    const bufferA = encoder.encode(a);\n    const bufferB = encoder.encode(b);\n    try {\n      return crypto.subtle.timingSafeEqual(bufferA, bufferB);\n    } catch {\n      // Fall through to manual implementation if timingSafeEqual fails\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Manual constant-time comparison\n  let result = 0;\n  for (let i = 0; i < a.length; i++) {\n    result |= a.charCodeAt(i) ^ b.charCodeAt(i);\n  }\n  return result === 0;\n}\n\n/**\n * Verify an API key against a hash using timing-safe comparison.\n */\nexport async function verifyApiKeyHash(\n  apiKey: string,\n  hash: string,\n  salt: string\n): Promise<boolean> {\n  const computed = await hashApiKey(apiKey, salt);\n  return timingSafeEqual(computed.hash, hash);\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Types\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * User roles for RBAC.\n * Imported from rbac.ts for type compatibility.\n */\nexport type UserRole = \"admin\" | \"user\" | \"guest\";\n\n/**\n * API key scope/permission levels.\n */\nexport type ApiKeyScope = \"read\" | \"write\" | \"admin\";\n\n/**\n * Individual permission for RBAC.\n * Imported from rbac.ts for type compatibility.\n */\nexport type Permission =\n  | \"trips:create\"\n  | \"trips:read\"\n  | \"trips:read:own\"\n  | \"trips:update\"\n  | \"trips:update:own\"\n  | \"trips:delete\"\n  | \"trips:delete:own\"\n  | \"trips:track\"\n  | \"trips:track:own\"\n  | \"subscriptions:create\"\n  | \"subscriptions:read\"\n  | \"subscriptions:read:own\"\n  | \"subscriptions:update\"\n  | \"subscriptions:update:own\"\n  | \"subscriptions:delete\"\n  | \"subscriptions:delete:own\"\n  | \"commutes:create\"\n  | \"commutes:read\"\n  | \"commutes:read:own\"\n  | \"commutes:update\"\n  | \"commutes:update:own\"\n  | \"commutes:delete\"\n  | \"commutes:delete:own\"\n  | \"journals:create\"\n  | \"journals:read\"\n  | \"journals:read:own\"\n  | \"journals:update\"\n  | \"journals:update:own\"\n  | \"journals:delete\"\n  | \"journals:delete:own\"\n  | \"alerts:read\"\n  | \"alerts:create\"\n  | \"alerts:update\"\n  | \"alerts:delete\"\n  | \"equipment:read\"\n  | \"equipment:update\"\n  | \"predictions:create\"\n  | \"predictions:read\"\n  | \"predictions:read:own\"\n  | \"admin:users:read\"\n  | \"admin:users:update\"\n  | \"admin:users:delete\"\n  | \"admin:apikeys:read\"\n  | \"admin:apikeys:create\"\n  | \"admin:apikeys:update\"\n  | \"admin:apikeys:delete\"\n  | \"admin:apikeys:rotate\"\n  | \"admin:sessions:read\"\n  | \"admin:sessions:revoke\"\n  | \"admin:audit:read\"\n  | \"admin:system:configure\"\n  | \"admin:roles:manage\"\n  | \"oauth:authorize\"\n  | \"oauth:revoke\"\n  | \"mfa:setup\"\n  | \"mfa:verify\"\n  | \"mfa:disable\"\n  | \"ratelimit:bypass:tier1\"\n  | \"ratelimit:bypass:tier2\"\n  | \"ratelimit:bypass:tier3\";\n\n/**\n * API key metadata.\n */\nexport interface ApiKey {\n  /** Key identifier (not the secret) */\n  keyId: string;\n  /** Hashed API key value (SHA-256 via PBKDF2) */\n  keyHash: string;\n  /** Salt used for hashing */\n  keySalt: string;\n  /** Permission scope */\n  scope: ApiKeyScope;\n  /** User role for RBAC */\n  role?: UserRole;\n  /** Additional permissions beyond role permissions */\n  additionalPermissions?: Permission[];\n  /** Associated user/organization (optional) */\n  owner?: string;\n  /** Rate limit tier (requests per minute) */\n  rateLimitTier: number;\n  /** Whether the key is active */\n  active: boolean;\n  /** Key creation timestamp */\n logger } from \"../observability/logger.js\";\nimport type { AuthSession } from \"./authentication.js\";\nimport { securityLogger } from \"./security-logging.js\";\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Types and Interfaces\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Session priority levels for conflict resolution.\n */\nexport type SessionPriority = \"low\" | \"normal\" | \"high\" | \"critical\";\n\n/**\n * Session conflict resolution strategy.\n */\nexport type ConflictResolution =\n  | \"deny_new\" // Deny new session when limit reached (default)\n  | \"terminate_oldest\" // Terminate oldest session\n  | \"terminate_lowest_priority\" // Terminate lowest priority session\n  | \"terminate_idle\" // Terminate idle sessions first\n  | \"allow_all\"; // Allow all sessions (no limit)\n\n/**\n * Device information for session tracking.\n */\nexport interface SessionDeviceInfo {\n  /** Device ID */\n  deviceId: string;\n  /** Device type */\n  deviceType: \"mobile\" | \"tablet\" | \"desktop\" | \"unknown\";\n  /** OS family */\n  osFamily?: string;\n  /** Browser family */\n  browserFamily?: string;\n  /** Whether this is a trusted device */\n  trusted: boolean;\n}\n\n/**\n * Geographic information for session tracking.\n */\nexport */\n  timezone?: string;\n}\n\n/**\n * Enhanced session data with metadata.\n */\nexport interface EnhancedSession extends AuthSession {\n  /** Session priority */\n  priority: SessionPriority;\n  /** device information */\n  trackDeviceInfo?: boolean;\n  /** Whether to track geographic information */\n  trackGeoInfo?: boolean;\n  /** Whether to enable session pinning */\n  enablePinning?: boolean;\n}\n\n/**\n * Session conflict result.\n */\nexport interface SessionConflictResult {\n  /** Whether the new session is allowed */\n  allowed: boolean;\n  /** Terminated session IDs (if any) */\n  terminatedSessions: string[];\n  /** Reason for denial (if denied) */\n  reason?: string;\n  /** Warning messages */\n  warnings: string[];\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Default Configuration\n// ============================================================================\n\nconst DEFAULT_CONFIG: Required<Omit<ConcurrentSessionConfig, \"conflictResolution\">> & {\n  conflictResolution: ConflictResolution;\n} = {\n  maxConcurrentSessions: 5,\n  maxSessionsPerDevice: 2,\n  conflictResolution: \"terminate_oldest\",\n  sessionTimeout: 30 * 60 * 1000, // 30 minutes\n  maxIdleTime: 60 * 60 * 1000, // 1 hour\n  trackDeviceInfo: true,\n  trackGeoInfo: false,\n  enablePinning: ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * User session registry.\n * Maps keyId to array of sessions.\n */\nconst userSessionRegistry = new Map<string, ============================================================================\n// Session Management Functions\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Start the automatic session cleanup interval.\n */\nexport function startSessionCleanup(config: ConcurrentSessionConfig = {}): void {\n  if (cleanupInterval) {\n    return; // Already running\n  }\n\n  const mergedConfig = { ...DEFAULT_CONFIG, ...config };\n\n  cleanupInterval = setInterval(() => {\n    cleanupExpiredSessions(mergedConfig);\n  }, CLEANUP_INTERVAL_MS);\n\n  logger.info(\"Session cleanup interval started\", {\n    intervalMs: CLEANUP_INTERVAL_MS,\n  });\n}\n\n/**\n * Stop the automatic session cleanup interval.\n */\nexport function stopSessionCleanup(): void {\n  if (cleanupInterval) {\n    clearInterval(cleanupInterval);\n    cleanupInterval = null;\n    logger.info(\"Session cleanup interval stopped\");\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Clean up expired and idle sessions.\n */\nexport function cleanupExpiredSessions(config: ConcurrentSessionConfig = {}): {\n  cleaned: number;\n  remaining: number;\n} {\n  const mergedConfig = { ...DEFAULT_CONFIG, ...config };\n  const now = Date.now();\n  let cleaned = 0;\n\n  for (const [keyId, sessions] of userSessionRegistry.entries()) {\n    const activeSessions = sessions.filter((session) => {\n      // Skip pinned sessions\n      if (session.pinned) {\n        return true;\n      }\n\n      // Check session timeout\n      if (now > session.expiresAt) {\n        logger.info(\"Session expired\", { sessionId: session.sessionId, keyId });\n        removeFromDeviceRegistry(session);\n        cleaned++;\n        return false;\n      }\n\n      // Check idle time\n      const idleTime = now - session.lastActivityAt;\n      if (idleTime > mergedConfig.maxIdleTime) {\n        logger.info(\"Session terminated due to inactivity\", {\n          sessionId: session.sessionId,\n          keyId,\n          idleTime,\n        });\n        removeFromDeviceRegistry(session);\n        cleaned++;\n        return false;\n      }\n\n      return true;\n    });\n\n    if (activeSessions.length === 0) {\n      userSessionRegistry.delete(keyId);\n    } else {\n      userSessionRegistry.set(keyId, activeSessions);\n    }\n  }\n\n  const remaining = Array.from(userSessionRegistry.values()).reduce(\n    (sum, sessions) => sum + sessions.length,\n    0\n  );\n\n  if (cleaned > 0) {\n    logger.info(\"Session cleanup completed\", { cleaned, remaining });\n  }\n\n  return { cleaned, remaining };\n}\n\n/**\n * Remove session from device registry.\n */\nfunction removeFromDeviceRegistry(session: EnhancedSession): void {\n  if (session.deviceInfo?.deviceId) {\n    const deviceSessions = deviceSessionRegistry.get(session.deviceInfo.deviceId);\n    if (deviceSessions) {\n      const index = deviceSessions.indexOf(session.sessionId);\n      if (index > -1) {\n        deviceSessions.splice(index, 1);\n      }\n      if (deviceSessions.length === 0) {\n        deviceSessionRegistry.delete(session.deviceInfo.deviceId);\n      } else {\n        deviceSessionRegistry.set(session.deviceInfo.deviceId, deviceSessions);\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Get all active sessions for a user.\n */\nexport function getUserSessions(keyId: string): EnhancedSession[] {\n  return userSessionRegistry.get(keyId) || [];\n}\n\n/**\n * Get session count for a user.\n */\nexport function getUserSessionCount(keyId: string): number {\n  return   gdpr?: boolean;\n    /** PCI DSS relevant */\n    pciDss?: boolean;\n  };\n  /** Digital signature for integrity */\n  signature?: string;\n}\n\n/**\n * Audit log retention policy.\n */\nexport ============================================================================\n// Permission Checking\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Check if a role has a specific permission.\n *\n * This function implements caching to improve performance for\n * repeated permission checks. */\nexport function checkPermission(\n  role: UserRole,\n  permission: Permission,\n  options: {\n    /** Resource ID for resource-specific checks */\n    resourceId?: string;\n    /** User ID for config.cacheTtl) {\n      cacheStats.hits++;\n\n      if (config.enableAuditLogging && !cached.granted) {\n        logPermissionCheck({\n          role,\n          permission,\n          granted: false,\n          reason: \"Cached denial\",\n        });\n      }\n\n      return {\n        granted: cached.granted,\n        fromCache: true,\n        cacheAge: age,\n        reason: cached.granted ? undefined : \"Permission denied (cached)\",\n      };\n    }\n\n    // Cache entry expired, remove it\n    permissionCache.delete(keyString);\n    cacheStats.evictions++;\n  }\n\n {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_45b99442f141409fb9135423","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# Authorization Audit Report\n2\t\n3\t**Date:** 2026-04-09\n4\t**Application:** MTA My Way\n5\t**Audit Type:** Authorization & Access Control Analysis\n6\t\n7\t---\n8\t\n9\t## Executive Summary\n10\t\n11\tThis audit reviews the authorization and access control implementation in the MTA My Way application. The application has a **comprehensive authentication and authorization framework** with several **critical gaps** in endpoint coverage and resource ownership validation.\n12\t\n13\t**Overall Assessment:** The authorization middleware is well-designed, but several endpoints lack proper access controls, and the current resource ownership validation is insufficient for production use.\n14\t\n15\t---\n16\t\n17\t## 1. Current Authorization Implementation\n18\t\n19\t### 1.1 Authentication Mechanisms\n20\t\n21\t| Mechanism | Status | Implementation |\n22\t|-----------|--------|----------------|\n23\t| API Key Authentication | ✅ Implemented | PBKDF2 hashing (600k iterations), scope-based (read/write/admin) |\n24\t| Session Management | ✅ Implemented | IP binding, device tracking, CSRF tokens, sliding expiration |\n25\t| OAuth 2.0 | ✅ Implemented | PKCE flow, Google/GitHub providers |\n26\t| TOTP MFA | ✅ Implemented | Backup codes, verification window, per-key configuration |\n27\t| Refresh Tokens | ✅ Implemented | Token rotation, family-based invalidation, encryption support |\n28\t\n29\t### 1.2 Authorization Middleware\n30\t\n31\t| Middleware | Purpose | Status |\n32\t|-----------|---------|--------|\n33\t| `requireResourceAccess` | Resource-based access control | ✅ Implemented |\n34\t| `requireAdmin` | Admin-only operations | ✅ Implemented |\n35\t| `requireWrite` | Write operations | ✅ Implemented |\n36\t| `requireMfa` | MFA-verified operations | ✅ Implemented |\n37\t| `requireSameOrigin` | CSRF protection for state changes | ✅ Implemented |\n38\t| `validateDataAccess` | User-scoped data isolation | ✅ Implemented |\n39\t| `auditLogAccess` | Audit logging for privileged ops | ✅ Implemented |\n40\t\n41\t---\n42\t\n43\t## 2. Endpoint Authorization Coverage\n44\t\n45\t### 2.1 Endpoints WITH Authorization\n46\t\n47\t| Endpoint | Method | Authorization | Notes |\n48\t|----------|--------|---------------|-------|\n49\t| `/api/push/subscribe` | POST | `requireResourceAccess(\"subscription\", \"create\")` | ✅ Protected |\n50\t| `/api/push/unsubscribe` | DELETE | `requireResourceAccess(\"subscription\", \"delete\")` | ✅ Protected |\n51\t| `/api/push/subscription` | PATCH | `requireResourceAccess(\"subscription\", \"update\")` | ✅ Protected |\n52\t| `/api/trips` | POST | `requireResourceAccess(\"trip\", \"create\")` | ✅ Protected |\n53\t| `/api/trips/:tripId/notes` | PATCH | `requireResourceAccess(\"trip\", \"update\")` | ✅ Protected |\n54\t| `/api/trips/:tripId` | DELETE | `requireResourceAccess(\"trip\", \"delete\")` | ✅ Protected |\n55\t| `/api/commute/analyze` | POST | `requireResourceAccess(\"commute\", \"create\")` | ✅ Protected |\n56\t| `/api/context/detect` | POST | `requireResourceAccess(\"context\", \"create\")` | ✅ Protected |\n57\t| `/api/context/override` | POST | `requireResourceAccess(\"context\", \"update\")` + audit | ✅ Protected |\n58\t| `/api/context/settings` | PATCH | `requireResourceAccess(\"context\", \"update\")` + audit | ✅ Protected |\n59\t| `/api/auth/mfa/setup` | POST | `requireResourceAccess(\"admin\", \"create\")` | ✅ Protected |\n60\t| `/api/auth/mfa/enable` | POST | `requireResourceAccess(\"admin\", \"update\")` | ✅ Protected |\n61\t| `/api/auth/mfa/disable` | POST | `requireResourceAccess(\"admin\", \"delete\")` | ✅ Protected |\n62\t\n63\t### 2.2 Endpoints WITHOUT Authorization (CRITICAL GAPS)\n64\t\n65\t| Endpoint | Method | Risk Level | Issue |\n66\t|----------|--------|-----------|-------|\n67\t| `/api/trips` | GET | 🔴 HIGH | No authentication required - anyone can view all trips |\n68\t| `/api/trips/:tripId` | GET | 🔴 HIGH | No authentication required - anyone can view specific trip |\n69\t| `/api/journal/stats` | GET | 🔴 HIGH | No authentication required - exposes user statistics |\n70\t| `/api/journal/dates/:startDate/:endDate` | GET | 🔴 HIGH | No authentication required - exposes trip history by date |\n71\t| `/api/journal/summary` | GET | 🟡 MEDIUM | No authentication required - exposes recent trips and stats |\n72\t| `/api/context` | GET | 🟡 MEDIUM | No authentication required - exposes context settings |\n73\t| `/api/context/clear` | POST | 🟡 MEDIUM | No authorization - state modification without auth |\n74\t| `/api/push/vapid-public-key` | GET | 🟢 LOW | Public by design - VAPID key needed for push |\n75\t| `/api/auth/session` | GET | 🟢 LOW | Returns auth status - appropriate for public access |\n76\t| `/api/auth/session/refresh` | POST | 🔴 HIGH | Requires auth but checks in handler, not middleware |\n77\t| `/api/auth/session/revoke` | POST | 🔴 HIGH | Requires auth but checks in handler, not middleware |\n78\t| `/api/auth/mfa/status` | GET | 🔴 HIGH | Requires auth but checks in handler, not middleware |\n79\t| `/api/auth/mfa/verify` | POST | 🔴 HIGH | Requires auth but checks in handler, not middleware |\n80\t| `/api/auth/oauth/providers` | GET | 🟢 LOW | Public by design - provider listing |\n81\t| `/api/auth/oauth/authorize/:providerId` | GET | 🟢 LOW | Initiates OAuth - no auth needed |\n82\t| `/api/auth/oauth/callback/:providerId` | GET | 🟢 LOW | OAuth callback - validates state |\n83\t\n84\t---\n85\t\n86\t## 3. Critical Security Gaps\n87\t\n88\t### 3.1 Resource Ownership Validation (CRITICAL)\n89\t\n90\t**Current Implementation:**\n91\t```typescript\n92\t// authorization.ts - extractTripOwner()\n93\tfunction extractTripOwner(tripId: string): ResourceOwner {\n94\t  // Trip IDs are UUIDs - ownership is validated via database lookup\n95\t  // In a real system, trips would have a userId field\n96\t  return { ownerId: tripId, isPublic: false };\n97\t}\n98\t```\n99\t\n100\t**Issues:**\n101\t1. ⚠️ **No actual ownership validation** - trips don't have a `userId` field\n102\t2. ⚠️ **Comments indicate \"In a real system\"** - this is placeholder code\n103\t3. ⚠️ **Anyone with `write` scope can access any trip** if they know the trip ID\n104\t4. ⚠️ **Subscription ownership is endpoint-based only** - no database-level validation\n105\t\n106\t**Recommendation:** Implement proper user-scoped resource ownership with database lookups.\n107\t\n108\t### 3.2 Inconsistent Authorization Patterns\n109\t\n110\t**Pattern A: Middleware-based (Recommended)**\n111\t```typescript\n112\tapp.post(\"/api/push/subscribe\",\n113\t  requireResourceAccess(\"subscription\", \"create\"),\n114\t  async (c) => { ... }\n115\t);\n116\t```\n117\t\n118\t**Pattern B: Handler-based (Not Recommended)**\n119\t```typescript\n120\tapp.get(\"/api/auth/session\", (c) => {\n121\t  const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n122\t  if (!auth) {\n123\t    return c.json({ authenticated: false });\n124\t  }\n125\t  // ...\n126\t});\n127\t```\n128\t\n129\t**Issue:** Some endpoints check auth inside the handler instead of using middleware, making security analysis difficult and increasing the risk of missing checks.\n130\t\n131\t### 3.3 Missing Authorization on Read Operations\n132\t\n133\tMany read operations that expose user data have **no authentication requirement**:\n134\t\n135\t- `/api/trips` - Returns ALL trips\n136\t- `/api/journal/stats` - Returns user commute statistics\n137\t- `/api/journal/dates/:startDate/:endDate` - Returns trip history\n138\t- `/api/journal/summary` - Returns recent trips + stats\n139\t\n140\t**Risk:** Data exposure, privacy violations, potential scraping of user travel patterns.\n141\t\n142\t---\n143\t\n144\t## 4. Authentication Storage Issues\n145\t\n146\t### 4.1 In-Memory Storage (Production Risk)\n147\t\n148\t**Current:** API keys, sessions, OAuth states, TOTP configs all stored in memory.\n149\t\n150\t```typescript\n151\tconst apiKeys = new Map<string, ApiKey>();\n152\tconst sessions = new Map<string, AuthSession>();\n153\tconst oauthStates = new Map<string, OAuthState>();\n154\tconst totpConfigs = new Map<string, TotpConfig>();\n155\t```\n156\t\n157\t**Issues:**\n158\t1. ❌ All credentials lost on server restart\n159\t2. ❌ Cannot scale horizontally (multiple servers)\n160\t3. ❌ No persistence of OAuth sessions\n161\t4. ❌ Audit logs limited to 10,000 entries, in-memory only\n162\t\n163\t**Recommendation:** Migrate to database-backed storage (Redis for sessions, PostgreSQL for persistent data).\n164\t\n165\t---\n166\t\n167\t## 5. Positive Security Implementations\n168\t\n169\t### 5.1 Strong Cryptography\n170\t- ✅ PBKDF2 with 600,000 iterations (OWASP 2024 recommendation)\n171\t- ✅ Cryptographically secure random generation\n172\t- ✅ SHA-256 for token hashing\n173\t- ✅ HMAC for request signatures\n174\t\n175\t### 5.2 Session Security\n176\t- ✅ Session fixation prevention (regeneration after auth)\n177\t- ✅ IP binding option\n178\t- ✅ Device fingerprinting\n179\t- ✅ Idle timeout (30 minutes)\n180\t- ✅ Absolute expiration (24 hours)\n181\t- ✅ Session hijacking detection\n182\t\n183\t### 5.3 OAuth 2.0 Security\n184\t- ✅ PKCE flow (prevents authorization code interception)\n185\t- ✅ State parameter (CSRF protection)\n186\t- ✅ Nonce for ID token validation\n187\t- ✅ Short-lived state expiration (10 minutes)\n188\t\n189\t### 5.4 MFA Implementation\n190\t- ✅ TOTP with backup codes\n191\t- ✅ Time window validation (±30 seconds)\n192\t- ✅ Single-use backup codes\n193\t- ✅ Per-key configuration\n194\t\n195\t---\n196\t\n197\t## 6. Recommendations\n198\t\n199\t### Priority 1: Critical (Implement Immediately)\n200\t\n201\t1. **Add authentication to read operations** exposing user data:\n202\t   ```typescript\n203\t   // Before:\n204\t   app.get(\"/api/trips\", (c) => { ... });\n205\t\n206\t   // After:\n207\t   app.get(\"/api/trips\", apiKeyAuth({ requiredScope: \"read\" }), (c) => {\n208\t     const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n209\t     // Only return trips for this user\n210\t   });\n211\t   ```\n212\t\n213\t2. **Implement proper resource ownership validation**:\n214\t   - Add `userId` field to trips table\n215\t   - Add `userId` field to subscriptions\n216\t   - Validate ownership in `requireResourceAccess` using database lookups\n217\t   - Use `customCheck` function for database-backed ownership validation\n218\t\n219\t3. **Standardize authorization pattern** - Move handler-based auth checks to middleware:\n220\t   ```typescript\n221\t   // Before:\n222\t   app.get(\"/api/auth/session\", (c) => {\n223\t     const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n224\t     if (!auth) return c.json({ authenticated: false });\n225\t   });\n226\t\n227\t   // After:\n228\t   app.get(\"/api/auth/session\", optionalAuth(), (c) => {\n229\t     const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n230\t     return c.json({ authenticated: !!auth, ... });\n231\t   });\n232\t   ```\n233\t\n234\t### Priority 2: High (Implement Soon)\n235\t\n236\t1. **Add database-backed storage** for:\n237\t   - API keys (persistent credentials)\n238\t   - Sessions (Redis or database)\n239\t   - Audit logs (persistent storage)\n240\t   - TOTP configs\n241\t\n242\t2. **Add user-scoped query filtering**:\n243\t   ```typescript\n244\t   app.get(\"/api/trips\", apiKeyAuth(), (c) => {\n245\t     const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n246\t     const query = validateQuery(c, tripQuerySchema);\n247\t     // Force userId to match authenticated user\n248\t     const userQuery = { ...query, userId: auth.keyId };\n249\t     const trips = getTrips(userQuery);\n250\t   });\n251\t   ```\n252\t\n253\t3. **Add authorization tests** for:\n254\t   - All public endpoints (verify no data leak)\n255\t   - Resource ownership validation\n256\t   - Cross-user data access prevention\n257\t\n258\t### Priority 3: Medium (Future Improvements)\n259\t\n260\t1. **Add role-based access control (RBAC)**:\n261\t   - Define roles beyond scopes (admin, user, guest)\n262\t   - Map roles to permissions\n263\t   - Support role inheritance\n264\t\n265\t2. **Add attribute-based access control (ABAC)**:\n266\t   - Policy-based authorization\n267\t   - Dynamic permissions based on context\n268\t   - Time-based access rules\n269\t\n270\t3. **Add rate limiting per user**:\n271\t   - Track usage per API key\n272\t   - Different limits for different tiers\n273\t   - Burst allowance\n274\t\n275\t---\n276\t\n277\t## 7. Testing Recommendations\n278\t\n279\t### 7.1 Authorization Test Coverage Needed\n280\t\n281\t| Scenario | Test Needed |\n282\t|----------|-------------|\n283\t| Unauthenticated access to protected endpoints | ✅ Covered |\n284\t| Insufficient scope for operations | ✅ Covered |\n285\t| Cross-user data access attempts | ❌ Missing |\n286\t| Resource ownership validation | ❌ Missing |\n287\t| Public endpoint data exposure | ❌ Missing |\n288\t| Session hijacking attempts | ✅ Covered |\n289\t| OAuth flow security | ✅ Covered |\n290\t\n291\t### 7.2 Integration Test Gaps\n292\t\n293\t1. **End-to-end user journey tests**:\n294\t   - User registers → creates trip → views only their trips\n295\t   - User A cannot access User B's subscriptions\n296\t   - Admin can view all data (if required)\n297\t\n298\t2. **Negative tests**:\n299\t   - Attempting to access another user's trips\n300\t   - Attempting to modify another user's subscriptions\n301\t   - Attempting to access admin endpoints without admin scope\n302\t\n303\t---\n304\t\n305\t## 8. Compliance Considerations\n306\t\n307\t### 8.1 Data Privacy (GDPR/CCPA)\n308\t\n309\t| Requirement | Status | Notes |\n310\t|-------------|--------|-------|\n311\t| User data access control | ⚠️ Partial | Trips readable without auth |\n312\t| Right to deletion | ❌ Missing | No user data deletion endpoint |\n313\t| Data export | ❌ Missing | No GDPR data export endpoint |\n314\t| Consent management | ❌ Missing | No consent tracking |\n315\t| Data retention policy | ❌ Missing | No automatic data cleanup |\n316\t\n317\t### 8.2 Security Logging\n318\t\n319\t| Requirement | Status | Notes |\n320\t|-------------|--------|-------|\n321\t| Authentication failures | ✅ Logged | Security logger |\n322\t| Authorization failures | ✅ Logged | Security logger |\n323\t| Privileged operations | ✅ Logged | Audit log middleware |\n324\t| Data access | ⚠️ Partial | Some endpoints lack logging |\n325\t\n326\t---\n327\t\n328\t## 9. Conclusion\n329\t\n330\tThe MTA My Way application has a **strong foundation** for authorization with:\n331\t- Well-designed middleware architecture\n332\t- Multiple authentication mechanisms\n333\t- Comprehensive security features (MFA, OAuth, session security)\n334\t\n335\tHowever, there are **critical gaps** that must be addressed:\n336\t1. **Resource ownership validation is not implemented** (placeholder code only)\n337\t2. **Read operations exposing user data lack authentication**\n338\t3. **Inconsistent authorization patterns** across endpoints\n339\t4. **In-memory storage** not suitable for production\n340\t\n341\t**Recommended Action Plan:**\n342\t1. Immediately add authentication to read operations exposing user data\n343\t2. Implement database-backed resource ownership validation\n344\t3. Standardize authorization middleware usage\n345\t4. Migrate to persistent storage for credentials and sessions\n346\t5. The application has a **comprehensive authentication and authorization framework** with several **critical gaps** in endpoint coverage and resource ownership validation.\n\n**Overall Assessment:** The authorization middleware is well-designed, but several endpoints lack proper access controls, and the current resource ownership validation is insufficient for production use.\n\n---\n\n## 1. Current Authorization Implementation\n\n### 1.1 Authentication Mechanisms\n\n| Mechanism | Status | Implementation |\n|-----------|--------|----------------|\n| API Key Authentication | ✅ Implemented | PBKDF2 hashing (600k iterations), scope-based (read/write/admin) |\n| Session Management | ✅ Implemented | IP binding, device tracking, CSRF tokens, sliding expiration |\n| OAuth 2.0 | ✅ Implemented | PKCE flow, Google/GitHub providers |\n| TOTP MFA | ✅ Implemented | Backup codes, verification window, per-key configuration |\n| Refresh Tokens | ✅ Implemented | Token rotation, family-based invalidation, encryption support |\n\n### 1.2 Authorization Middleware\n\n| Middleware | Purpose | Status |\n|-----------|---------|--------|\n| `requireResourceAccess` | Resource-based access control | ✅ Implemented |\n| `requireAdmin` | Admin-only operations | ✅ Implemented |\n| `requireWrite` | Write operations | ✅ Implemented |\n| `requireMfa` | MFA-verified operations | ✅ Implemented |\n| `requireSameOrigin` | CSRF protection for state changes | ✅ Implemented |\n| `validateDataAccess` | User-scoped data isolation | ✅ Implemented |\n| `auditLogAccess` | Audit logging for privileged ops | ✅ Implemented |\n\n---\n\n## 2. Endpoint Authorization Coverage\n\n### 2.1 Endpoints WITH Authorization\n\n| Endpoint | Method | Authorization | Notes |\n|----------|--------|---------------|-------|\n| `/api/push/subscribe` | POST | `requireResourceAccess(\"subscription\", \"create\")` | ✅ Protected |\n| `/api/push/unsubscribe` | DELETE | `requireResourceAccess(\"subscription\", \"delete\")` | ✅ Protected |\n| `/api/push/subscription` | PATCH | `requireResourceAccess(\"subscription\", \"update\")` | ✅ Protected |\n| `/api/trips` | POST | `requireResourceAccess(\"trip\", \"create\")` | ✅ Protected |\n| `/api/trips/:tripId/notes` | PATCH | `requireResourceAccess(\"trip\", \"update\")` | ✅ Protected |\n| `/api/trips/:tripId` | DELETE | `requireResourceAccess(\"trip\", \"delete\")` | ✅ Protected |\n| `/api/commute/analyze` | POST | `requireResourceAccess(\"commute\", \"create\")` | ✅ Protected |\n| `/api/context/detect` | POST | `requireResourceAccess(\"context\", \"create\")` | ✅ Protected |\n| `/api/context/override` | POST | `requireResourceAccess(\"context\", \"update\")` + audit | ✅ Protected |\n| `/api/context/settings` | PATCH | `requireResourceAccess(\"context\", \"update\")` + audit | ✅ Protected |\n| `/api/auth/mfa/setup` | POST | `requireResourceAccess(\"admin\", \"create\")` | ✅ Protected |\n| `/api/auth/mfa/enable` | POST | `requireResourceAccess(\"admin\", \"update\")` | ✅ Protected |\n| `/api/auth/mfa/disable` | POST | `requireResourceAccess(\"admin\", \"delete\")` | ✅ Protected |\n\n### 2.2 Endpoints WITHOUT Authorization (CRITICAL GAPS)\n\n| Endpoint | Method | Risk Level | Issue |\n|----------|--------|-----------|-------|\n| `/api/trips` | GET | 🔴 HIGH | No authentication required - anyone can view all trips |\n| `/api/trips/:tripId` | GET | 🔴 HIGH | No authentication required - anyone can view specific trip |\n| `/api/journal/stats` | GET | 🔴 HIGH | No authentication required - exposes user statistics |\n| `/api/journal/dates/:startDate/:endDate` | GET | 🔴 HIGH | No authentication required - exposes trip history by date |\n| `/api/journal/summary` | GET | 🟡 MEDIUM | No authentication required - exposes recent trips and stats |\n| `/api/context` | GET | 🟡 MEDIUM | No authentication required - exposes context settings |\n| `/api/context/clear` | POST | 🟡 MEDIUM | No authorization - state modification without auth |\n| `/api/push/vapid-public-key` | GET | 🟢 LOW | Public by design - VAPID key needed for push |\n| `/api/auth/session` | GET | 🟢 LOW | Returns auth status - appropriate for public access |\n| `/api/auth/session/refresh` | POST | 🔴 HIGH | Requires auth but checks in handler, not middleware |\n| `/api/auth/session/revoke` | POST | 🔴 HIGH | Requires auth but checks in handler, not middleware |\n| `/api/auth/mfa/status` | GET | 🔴 HIGH | Requires auth but checks in handler, not middleware |\n| `/api/auth/mfa/verify` | POST | 🔴 HIGH | Requires auth but checks in handler, not middleware |\n| `/api/auth/oauth/providers` | GET | 🟢 LOW | Public by design - provider listing |\n| `/api/auth/oauth/authorize/:providerId` | GET | 🟢 LOW | Initiates OAuth - no auth needed |\n| `/api/auth/oauth/callback/:providerId` | GET | 🟢 LOW | OAuth callback - validates state |\n\n---\n\n## 3. Critical Security Gaps\n\n### 3.1 Resource Ownership Validation (CRITICAL)\n\n**Current Implementation:**\n```typescript\n// authorization.ts - extractTripOwner()\nfunction extractTripOwner(tripId: string): ResourceOwner {\n  // Trip IDs are UUIDs - ownership is validated via database lookup\n  // In a real system, trips would have a userId field\n  return { ownerId: tripId, isPublic: false };\n}\n```\n\n**Issues:**\n1. ⚠️ **No actual ownership validation** - trips don't have a `userId` field\n2. ⚠️ **Comments indicate \"In a real system\"** - this is placeholder code\n3. ⚠️ **Anyone with `write` scope can access any trip** if they know the trip ID\n4. ⚠️ **Subscription ownership is endpoint-based only** - no database-level validation\n\n**Recommendation:** Implement proper user-scoped resource ownership with database lookups.\n\n### 3.2 Inconsistent Authorization Patterns\n\n**Pattern A: Middleware-based (Recommended)**\n```typescript\napp.post(\"/api/push/subscribe\",\n  requireResourceAccess(\"subscription\", \"create\"),\n  async (c) => { ... }\n);\n```\n\n**Pattern B: Handler-based (Not Recommended)**\n```typescript\napp.get(\"/api/auth/session\", (c) => {\n  const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n  if (!auth) {\n    return c.json({ authenticated: false });\n  }\n  // ...\n});\n```\n\n**Issue:** Some endpoints check auth inside the handler instead of using middleware, making security analysis difficult and increasing the risk of missing checks.\n\n### 3.3 Missing Authorization on Read Operations\n\nMany read operations that expose user data have **no authentication requirement**:\n\n- `/api/trips` - Returns ALL trips\n- `/api/journal/stats` - Returns user commute statistics\n- `/api/journal/dates/:startDate/:endDate` - Returns trip history\n- `/api/journal/summary` - Returns recent trips + stats\n\n**Risk:** Data exposure, privacy violations, potential scraping of user travel patterns.\n\n---\n\n## 4. Authentication Storage Issues\n\n### 4.1 In-Memory Storage (Production Risk)\n\n**Current:** API keys, sessions, OAuth states, TOTP configs all stored in memory.\n\n```typescript\nconst apiKeys = new Map<string, ApiKey>();\nconst sessions = new Map<string, AuthSession>();\nconst oauthStates = new Map<string, OAuthState>();\nconst totpConfigs = new Map<string, TotpConfig>();\n```\n\n**Issues:**\n1. ❌ All credentials lost on server restart\n2. ❌ Cannot scale horizontally (multiple servers)\n3. ❌ No persistence of OAuth sessions\n4. ❌ Audit logs limited to 10,000 entries, in-memory only\n\n**Recommendation:** Migrate to database-backed storage (Redis for sessions, PostgreSQL for persistent data).\n\n---\n\n## 5. Positive Security Implementations\n\n### 5.1 Strong Cryptography\n- ✅ PBKDF2 with 600,000 iterations (OWASP 2024 recommendation)\n- ✅ Cryptographically secure random generation\n- ✅ SHA-256 for token hashing\n- ✅ HMAC for request signatures\n\n### 5.2 Session Security\n- ✅ Session fixation prevention (regeneration after auth)\n- ✅ IP binding option\n- ✅ Device fingerprinting\n- ✅ Idle timeout (30 minutes)\n- ✅ Absolute expiration (24 hours)\n- ✅ Session hijacking detection\n\n### 5.3 OAuth 2.0 Security\n- ✅ PKCE flow (prevents authorization code interception)\n- ✅ State parameter (CSRF protection)\n- ✅ Nonce for ID token validation\n- ✅ Short-lived state expiration (10 minutes)\n\n### 5.4 MFA Implementation\n- ✅ TOTP with backup codes\n- ✅ Time window validation (±30 seconds)\n- ✅ Single-use backup codes\n- ✅ Per-key configuration\n\n---\n\n## 6. Recommendations\n\n### Priority 1: Critical (Implement Immediately)\n\n1. **Add authentication to read operations** exposing user data:\n   ```typescript\n   // Before:\n   app.get(\"/api/trips\", (c) => { ... });\n\n   // After:\n   app.get(\"/api/trips\", apiKeyAuth({ requiredScope: \"read\" }), (c) => {\n     const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n     // Only return trips for this user\n   });\n   ```\n\n2. **Implement proper resource ownership validation**:\n   - Add `userId` field to trips table\n   - Add `userId` field to subscriptions\n   - Validate ownership in `requireResourceAccess` using database lookups\n   - Use `customCheck` function for database-backed ownership validation\n\n3. **Standardize authorization pattern** - Move handler-based auth checks to middleware:\n   ```typescript\n   // Before:\n   app.get(\"/api/auth/session\", (c) => {\n     const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n     if (!auth) return c.json({ authenticated: false });\n   });\n\n   // After:\n   app.get(\"/api/auth/session\", optionalAuth(), (c) => {\n     const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n     return c.json({ authenticated: !!auth, ... });\n   });\n   ```\n\n### Priority 2: High (Implement Soon)\n\n1. **Add database-backed storage** for:\n   - API keys (persistent credentials)\n   - Sessions (Redis or database)\n   - Audit logs (persistent storage)\n   - TOTP configs\n\n2. **Add user-scoped query filtering**:\n   ```typescript\n   app.get(\"/api/trips\", apiKeyAuth(), (c) => {\n     const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n     const query = validateQuery(c, tripQuerySchema);\n     // Force userId to match authenticated user\n     const userQuery = { ...query, userId: auth.keyId };\n     const trips = getTrips(userQuery);\n   });\n   ```\n\n3. **Add authorization tests** for:\n   - All public endpoints (verify no data leak)\n   - Resource ownership validation\n   - Cross-user data access prevention\n\n### Priority 3: Medium (Future Improvements)\n\n1. **Add role-based access control (RBAC)**:\n   - Define roles beyond scopes (admin, user, guest)\n   - Map roles to permissions\n   - Support role inheritance\n\n2. **Add attribute-based access control (ABAC)**:\n   - Policy-based authorization\n   - Dynamic permissions based on context\n   - Time-based access rules\n\n3. **Add rate limiting per user**:\n   - Track usage per API key\n   - Different limits for different tiers\n   - Burst allowance\n\n---\n\n## 7. Testing Recommendations\n\n### 7.1 Authorization Test Coverage Needed\n\n| Scenario | Test Needed |\n|----------|-------------|\n| Unauthenticated access to protected endpoints | ✅ Covered |\n| Insufficient scope for operations | ✅ Covered |\n| Cross-user data access attempts | ❌ Missing |\n| Resource ownership validation | ❌ Missing |\n| Public endpoint data exposure | ❌ Missing |\n| Session hijacking attempts | ✅ Covered |\n| OAuth flow security | ✅ Covered |\n\n### 7.2 Integration Test Gaps\n\n1. **End-to-end user journey tests**:\n   - User registers → creates trip → views only their trips\n   - User A cannot access User B's subscriptions\n   - Admin can view all data (if required)\n\n2. **Negative tests**:\n   - Attempting to access another user's trips\n   - Attempting to modify another user's subscriptions\n   - Attempting to access admin endpoints without admin scope\n\n---\n\n## 8. Compliance Considerations\n\n### 8.1 Data Privacy (GDPR/CCPA)\n\n| Requirement | Status | Notes |\n|-------------|--------|-------|\n| User data access control | ⚠️ Partial | Trips readable without auth |\n| Right to deletion | ❌ Missing | No user data deletion endpoint |\n| Data export | ❌ Missing | No GDPR data export endpoint |\n| Consent management | ❌ Missing | No consent tracking |\n| Data retention policy | ❌ Missing | No automatic data cleanup |\n\n### 8.2 Security Logging\n\n| Requirement | Status | Notes |\n|-------------|--------|-------|\n| Authentication failures | ✅ Logged | Security logger |\n| Authorization failures | ✅ Logged | Security logger |\n| Privileged operations | ✅ Logged | Audit log middleware |\n| Data access | ⚠️ Partial | Some endpoints lack logging |\n\n---\n\n## 9. Conclusion\n\nThe MTA My Way application has a **strong foundation** for authorization with:\n- Well-designed middleware architecture\n- Multiple authentication mechanisms\n- Comprehensive security features (MFA, OAuth, session security)\n\nHowever, there are **critical gaps** that must be addressed:\n1. **Resource ownership validation is not implemented** (placeholder code only)\n2. **Read operations exposing user data lack authentication**\n3. **Inconsistent authorization patterns** across endpoints\n4. **In-memory storage** not suitable for production\n\n**Recommended Action Plan:**\n1. Immediately add authentication to read operations exposing user data\n2. Implement database-backed resource ownership validation\n3. Standardize authorization middleware usage\n4. Migrate to persistent storage for credentials and sessions\n5. Add comprehensive authorization tests\n\n---\n\n## Appendix A: Authorization Middleware Quick Reference\n\n```typescript\n// Require specific resource access\nrequireResourceAccess(resourceType, action, options?)\n\n// Require admin scope\nrequireAdmin()\n\n// Require write scope\nrequireWrite()\n\n// Require MFA verification\nrequireMfa()\n\n// Enforce rate limit tier\nenforceRateLimitTier(maxTier)\n\n// Require same-origin for state changes\nrequireSameOrigin()\n\n// Validate user-scoped data access\nvalidateDataAccess(resourceType)\n\n// Audit log access\nauditLogAccess(resourceType, action)\n\n// Optional authentication (attaches if present)\noptionalAuth()\n\n// Check authorization without throwing\ncheckAuthorization(context, resourceType, action)\n```\n\n---\n\n**Report Generated:** 2026-04-09\n**Next Review Date:** After implementing Priority 1 recommendations\n","numLines":388,"startLine":1,"totalLines":388}}}

* Role definitions and permissions.\n *\n * This module is separated to avoid circular dependencies between\n * authentication.ts and rbac.ts\n */\n\nimport type { Permission, UserRole } from \"./authentication.js\";\n\n/**\n * Guest role - lowest privileges.\n * Guests can only read public data and create their own account.\n */\nconst GUEST_PERMISSIONS: Permission[] = [\n  \"alerts:read\",\n  \"equipment:read\",\n  \"predictions:create\",\n  \"predictions:read:own\",\n  \"oauth:authorize\",\n];\n\n/**\n * User role - standard privileges.\n * Users can manage their own trips, subscriptions, commutes, and journals.\n */\nconst USER_PERMISSIONS: Permission[] = [\n  // All guest permissions\n  ...GUEST_PERMISSIONS,\n  // Trip permissions\n  \"trips:create\",\n  \"trips:read:own\",\n  \"trips:update:own\",\n  \"trips:delete:own\",\n  \"trips:track:own\",\n  // Subscription permissions\n  \"subscriptions:create\",\n  \"subscriptions:read:own\",\n  \"subscriptions:update:own\",\n  \"subscriptions:delete:own\",\n  // Commute permissions\n  \"commutes:create\",\n  \"commutes:read:own\",\n  \"commutes:update:own\",\n  \"commutes:delete:own\",\n  // Journal permissions\n  \"journals:create\",\n  \"journals:read:own\",\n  \"journals:update:own\",\n  \"journals:delete:own\",\n  // MFA\n  \"mfa:setup\",\n  \"mfa:verify\",\n];\n\n/**\n * Admin role - highest privileges.\n * Admins have full access to all resources and system configuration.\n */\nconst ADMIN_PERMISSIONS: Permission[] = [\n  // All user permissions\n  ...USER_PERMISSIONS,\n  // Full trip access\n  \"trips:read\",\n  \"trips:update\",\n  \"trips:delete\",\n  \"trips:track\",\n  // Full subscription access\n  \"subscriptions:read\",\n  \"subscriptions:update\",\n  \"subscriptions:delete\",\n  // Full commute access\n  \"commutes:read\",\n  \"commutes:update\",\n  \"commutes:delete\",\n  // Full journal access\n  \"journals:read\",\n  \"journals:update\",\n  \"journals:delete\",\n  // Alert management\n  \"alerts:create\",\n  \"alerts:update\",\n  \"alerts:delete\",\n  // Equipment management\n  \"equipment:update\",\n  // Full predictions access\n  \"predictions:read\",\n  // Admin permissions\n  \"admin:users:read\",\n  \"admin:users:update\",\n  \"admin:users:delete\",\n  \"admin:apikeys:read\",\n  \"admin:apikeys:create\",\n  \"admin:apikeys:update\",\n  \"admin:apikeys:delete\",\n  \"admin:apikeys:rotate\",\n  \"admin:sessions:read\",\n  \"admin:sessions:revoke\",\n  \"admin:audit:read\",\n  \"admin:system:configure\",\n  \"admin:roles:manage\",\n  // OAuth management\n  \"oauth:revoke\",\n  // MFA management\n  \"mfa:disable\",\n  // Rate limit bypass\n  \"ratelimit:bypass:tier1\",\n  \"ratelimit:bypass:tier2\",\n  \"ratelimit:bypass:tier3\",\n];\n\n/**\n * Role definitions registry.\n */\nconst ROLE_DEFINITIONS: Map<UserRole, { permissions: Permission[]; inheritsFrom?: UserRole }> =\n  new Map([\n    [\n      \"guest\",\n      {\n        permissions: GUEST_PERMISSIONS,\n      },\n    ],\n    [\n      \"user\",\n      {\n        permissions: USER_PERMISSIONS,\n        inheritsFrom: \"guest\",\n      },\n    ],\n    [\n      \"admin\",\n      {\n        permissions: ADMIN_PERMISSIONS,\n        inheritsFrom: \"user\",\n      },\n    ],\n  ]);\n\n/**\n * Get all permissions for a given role.\n * Handles role inheritance.\n */\nexport function getRolePermissions(role: UserRole): Permission[] {\n  const roleDef = ROLE_DEFINITIONS.get(role);\n  if (!roleDef) return [];\n\n  // Get inherited permissions
