\"user\",\n240\t      priority: 3,\n241\t    },\n242\t  ],\n243\t]);\n244\t\n245\t// ============================================================================\n246\t// Permission Utilities\n247\t// ============================================================================\n248\t\n249\t/**\n250\t * Parse a permission string into resource and action.\n251\t * Example: \"trips:create\" -> { resource: \"trips\", action: \"create\" }\n252\t */\n253\texport function parsePermission(permission: Permission): {\n254\t  resource: ResourceCategory;\n255\t  action: PermissionAction;\n256\t  scope?: \"own\";\n257\t} {\n258\t  const parts = permission.split(\":\");\n259\t  if (parts.length < 2) {\n260\t    throw new Error(`Invalid permission format: ${permission}`);\n261\t  }\n262\t\n263\t  const resource = parts[0] as ResourceCategory;\n264\t  const action = parts[1] as PermissionAction;\n265\t  const scope = parts[2] as \"own\" | undefined;\n266\t\n267\t  return { resource, action, scope };\n268\t}\n269\t\n270\t/**\n271\t * Build a permission string from components.\n272\t */\n273\texport function buildPermission(\n274\t  resource: ResourceCategory,\n275\t  action: PermissionAction,\n276\t  scope?: \"own\"\n277\t): Permission {\n278\t  const base = `${resource}:${action}` as Permission;\n279\t  return scope ? (`${base}:${scope}` as Permission) : base;\n280\t}\n281\t\n282\t/**\n283\t * Get role definition by role name.\n284\t */\n285\texport function getRoleDefinition(role: UserRole): RoleDefinition | undefined {\n286\t  return ROLE_DEFINITIONS.get(role);\n287\t}\n288\t\n289\t/**\n290\t * Get all role definitions.\n291\t */\n292\texport function getAllRoleDefinitions(): RoleDefinition[] {\n293\t  return Array.from(ROLE_DEFINITIONS.values());\n294\t}\n295\t\n296\t/**\n297\t * Check if a role has a specific permission.\n298\t */\n299\texport function roleHasPermission(role: UserRole, permission: Permission): boolean {\n300\t  const permissions = getRolePerms(role);\n301\t  return permissions.includes(permission);\n302\t}\n303\t\n304\t/**\n305\t * Check if one role has higher priority than another.\n306\t * Returns true if role1 has higher priority than role2.\n307\t */\n308\texport function isRoleHigher(role1: UserRole, role2: UserRole): boolean {\n309\t  const def1 = ROLE_DEFINITIONS.get(role1);\n310\t  const def2 = ROLE_DEFINITIONS.get(role2);\n311\t\n312\t  if (!def1 || !def2) return false;\n313\t\n314\t  return def1.priority > def2.priority;\n315\t}\n316\t\n317\t/**\n318\t * Get the highest role from a list of roles.\n319\t */\n320\texport function getHighestRole(roles: UserRole[]): UserRole | null {\n321\t  if (roles.length === 0) return null;\n322\t\n323\t  let highest = roles[0];\n324\t  for (const role of roles) {\n325\t    if (isRoleHigher(role, highest)) {\n326\t      highest = role;\n327\t    }\n328\t  }\n329\t\n330\t  return highest;\n331\t}\n332\t\n333\t// ============================================================================\n334\t// Authorization Context Enhancement\n335\t// ============================================================================\n336\t\n337\t/**\n338\t * Extended auth context that includes RBAC role information.\n339\t */\n340\texport interface RbacAuthContext extends AuthContext {\n341\t  /** User role */\n342\t  role?: UserRole;\n343\t  /** All roles assigned to the user */\n344\t  roles?: UserRole[];\n345\t  /** Specific permissions granted to this user (beyond role permissions) */\n346\t  additionalPermissions?: Permission[];\n347\t}\n348\t\n349\t/**\n350\t * Get RBAC auth context from Hono context.\n351\t */\n352\texport function getRbacAuthContext(c: Context): RbacAuthContext | undefined {\n353\t  const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n354\t  if (!auth) return undefined;\n355\t\n356\t  // Add role information from auth context if available\n357\t  return {\n358\t    ...auth,\n359\t    role: auth.role as UserRole | undefined,\n360\t    roles: auth.roles as UserRole[] | undefined,\n361\t    additionalPermissions: auth.additionalPermissions as Permission[] | undefined,\n362\t  };\n363\t}\n364\t\n365\t/**\n366\t * Set RBAC auth context on Hono context (for testing purposes).\n367\t */\n368\texport function setRbacAuthContext(c: Context, authContext: RbacAuthContext): void {\n369\t  c.set(\"auth\", authContext);\n370\t}\n371\t\n372\t// ============================================================================\n373\t// Permission Checking\n374\t// ============================================================================\n375\t\n376\t/**\n377\t * Check if the current context has a specific permission.\n378\t */\n379\texport function hasPermission(c: Context, permission: Permission, options?: RbacOptions): boolean {\n380\t  const auth = getRbacAuthContext(c);\n381\t\n382\t  // Unauthenticated users only have guest permissions\n383\t  const role = auth?.role || \"guest\";\n384\t  const rolePermissions = getRolePerms(role);\n385\t\n386\t  // Check role permissions\n387\t  if (rolePermissions.includes(permission)) {\n388\t    if (options?.logAuthorizationChecks) {\n389\t      logger.debug(\"Permission granted via role\", { role, permission });\n390\t    }\n391\t    return true;\n392\t  }\n393\t\n394\t  // Check additional permissions if available\n395\t  if (auth?.additionalPermissions?.includes(permission)) {\n396\t    if (options?.logAuthorizationChecks) {\n397\t      logger.debug(\"Permission granted via additional permissions\", { permission });\n398\t    }\n399\t    return true;\n400\t  }\n401\t\n402\t  if (options?.logAuthorizationChecks) {\n403\t    logger.debug(\"Permission denied\", { role, permission });\n404\t  }\n405\t\n406\t  return false;\n407\t}\n408\t\n409\t/**\n410\t * Check if the current context has any of the specified permissions.\n411\t */\n412\texport function hasAnyPermission(\n413\t  c: Context,\n414\t  permissions: Permission[],\n415\t  options?: RbacOptions\n416\t): boolean {\n417\t  return permissions.some((permission) => hasPermission(c, permission, options));\n418\t}\n419\t\n420\t/**\n421\t * Check if the current context has all of the specified permissions.\n422\t */\n423\texport function hasAllPermissions(\n424\t  c: Context,\n425\t  permissions: Permission[],\n426\t  options?: RbacOptions\n427\t): boolean {\n428\t  return permissions.every((permission) => hasPermission(c, permission, options));\n429\t}\n430\t\n431\t// ============================================================================\n432\t// RBAC Middleware\n433\t// ============================================================================\n434\t\n435\t/**\n436\t * Require a specific permission for access.\n437\t *\n438\t * @example\n439\t * ```ts\n440\t * app.post(\"/trips\", requirePermission(\"trips:create\"), async (c) => {\n441\t *   // Handler code\n442\t * });\n443\t * ```\n444\t */\n445\texport function requirePermission(\n446\t  permission: Permission,\n447\t  options?: RbacOptions\n448\t): MiddlewareHandler {\n449\t  return async (c, next) => {\n450\t    const auth = getRbacAuthContext(c);\n451\t\n452\t    if (!auth) {\n453\t      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"rbac\", permission);\n454\t      throw new HTTPException(401, { message: \"Authentication required\" });\n455\t    }\n456\t\n457\t    // Use custom permission checker if provided\n458\t    if (options?.customPermissionChecker) {\n459\t      const hasCustomPermission = await options.customPermissionChecker(c, permission, auth);\n460\t      if (!hasCustomPermission) {\n461\t        securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"rbac\", permission);\n462\t        throw new HTTPException(403, {\n463\t          message: `Permission denied: ${permission}`,\n464\t        });\n465\t      }\n466\t      return next();\n467\t    }\n468\t\n469\t    // Standard permission check\n470\t    if (!hasPermission(c, permission, options)) {\n471\t      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"rbac\", permission);\n472\t      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n473\t        message: `Permission denied: ${permission}`,\n474\t      });\n475\t    }\n476\t\n477\t    return next();\n478\t  };\n479\t}\n480\t\n481\t/**\n482\t * Require any of the specified permissions for access.\n483\t */\n484\texport function requireAnyPermission(\n485\t  permissions: Permission[],\n486\t  options?: RbacOptions\n487\t): MiddlewareHandler {\n488\t  return async (c, next) => {\n489\t    const auth = getRbacAuthContext(c);\n490\t\n491\t    if (!auth) {\n492\t      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"rbac\", \"any_permission\");\n493\t      throw new HTTPException(401, { message: \"Authentication required\" });\n494\t    }\n495\t\n496\t    if (!hasAnyPermission(c, permissions, options)) {\n497\t      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"rbac\", \"any_permission\");\n498\t      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n499\t        message: `Permission denied. Required: ${permissions.join(\" or \")}`,\n500\t      });\n501\t    }\n502\t\n503\t    return next();\n504\t  };\n505\t}\n506\t\n507\t/**\n508\t * Require all of the specified permissions for access.\n509\t */\n510\texport function requireAllPermissions(\n511\t  permissions: Permission[],\n512\t  options?: RbacOptions\n513\t): MiddlewareHandler {\n514\t  return async (c, next) => {\n515\t    const auth = getRbacAuthContext(c);\n516\t\n517\t    if (!auth) {\n518\t      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"rbac\", \"all_permissions\");\n519\t      throw new HTTPException(401, { message: \"Authentication required\" });\n520\t    }\n521\t\n522\t    if (!hasAllPermissions(c, permissions, options)) {\n523\t      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"rbac\", \"all_permissions\");\n524\t      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n525\t        message: `Permission denied. Required: ${permissions.join(\" and \")}`,\n526\t      });\n527\t    }\n528\t\n529\t    return next();\n530\t  };\n531\t}\n532\t\n533\t/**\n534\t * Require a specific role for access.\n535\t *\n536\t * @example\n537\t * ```ts\n538\t * app.delete(\"/admin/users/:id\", requireRole(\"admin\"), async (c) => {\n539\t *   // Handler code\n540\t * });\n541\t * ```\n542\t */\n543\texport function requireRole(requiredRole: UserRole): MiddlewareHandler {\n544\t  return async (c, next) => {\n545\t    const auth = getRbacAuthContext(c);\n546\t\n547\t    if (!auth) {\n548\t      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"rbac\", requiredRole);\n549\t      throw new HTTPException(401, { message: \"Authentication required\" });\n550\t    }\n551\t\n552\t    const userRole = auth.role || \"guest\";\n553\t\n554\t    // Check if user has the required role or higher\n555\t    if (!isRoleHigher(userRole, requiredRole) && userRole !== requiredRole) {\n556\t      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"rbac\", requiredRole);\n557\t      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n558\t        message: `Role required: ${requiredRole}`,\n559\t      });\n560\t    }\n561\t\n562\t    return next();\n563\t  };\n564\t}\n565\t\n566\t/**\n567\t * Require at least the specified role level.\n568\t * Allows access to users with the role or any higher-priority role.\n569\t */\n570\texport function requireRoleLevel(minRole: UserRole): MiddlewareHandler {\n571\t  return async (c, next) => {\n572\t    const auth = getRbacAuthContext(c);\n573\t\n574\t    if (!auth) {\n575\t      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"rbac\", minRole);\n576\t      throw new HTTPException(401, { message: \"Authentication required\" });\n577\t    }\n578\t\n579\t    const userRole = auth.role || \"guest\";\n580\t\n581\t    // Check if user has at least the minimum required role\n582\t    if (!isRoleHigher(userRole, minRole) && userRole !== minRole) {\n583\t      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"rbac\", minRole);\n584\t      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n585\t        message: `Minimum role required: ${minRole}`,\n586\t      });\n587\t    }\n588\t\n589\t    return next();\n590\t  };\n591\t}\n592\t\n593\t/**\n594\t * Require ownership of a resource or admin role.\n595\t *\n596\t * This middleware checks if the authenticated user owns the resource\n597\t * being accessed. Admin users bypass the ownership check.\n598\t *\n599\t * @param resourceType - The type of resource being accessed\n600\t * @param ownerIdParam - The parameter name containing the owner ID (default: \"userId\")\n601\t * @param getOwnerId - Custom function to extract owner ID from context\n602\t */\n603\texport function requireOwnershipOrAdmin(\n604\t  resourceType: ResourceCategory,\n605\t  options?: {\n606\t    ownerIdParam?: string;\n607\t    getOwnerId?: (c: Context) => string | Promise<string>;\n608\t    adminBypass?: boolean;\n609\t  }\n610\t): MiddlewareHandler {\n611\t  const { ownerIdParam = \"userId\", getOwnerId, adminBypass = true } = options || {};\n612\t\n613\t  return async (c, next) => {\n614\t    const auth = getRbacAuthContext(c);\n615\t\n616\t    if (!auth) {\n617\t      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, resourceType, \"ownership\");\n618\t      throw new HTTPException(401, { message: \"Authentication required\" });\n619\t    }\n620\t\n621\t    // Admin bypass check\n622\t    if (adminBypass && auth.role === \"admin\") {\n623\t      logger.debug(\"Admin bypass for ownership check\", { resourceType });\n624\t      return next();\n625\t    }\n626\t\n627\t    // Get the owner ID\n628\t    let ownerId: string;\n629\t    if (getOwnerId) {\n630\t      ownerId = await getOwnerId(c);\n631\t    } else {\n632\t      ownerId = c.req.param(ownerIdParam) || \"\";\n633\t    }\n634\t\n635\t    // Check ownership\n636\t    // If ownerId is empty, the resource doesn't exist - let handler return 404\n637\t    if (ownerId && auth.keyId !== ownerId) {\n638\t      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, resourceType, \"ownership\");\n639\t      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n640\t        message: `Access denied: ${resourceType} ownership required`,\n641\t      });\n642\t    }\n643\t\n644\t    return next();\n645\t  };\n646\t}\n647\t\n648\t/**\n649\t * Conditional permission check middleware.\n650\t *\n651\t * Executes different middleware based on the user's role.\n652\t * Useful for implementing role-specific behavior.\n653\t *\n654\t * @example\n655\t * ```ts\n656\t * app.get(\n657\t *   \"/trips\",\n658\t *   conditionalByRole({\n659\t *     admin: adminTripsHandler,\n660\t *     user: userTripsHandler,\n661\t *     guest: guestTripsHandler,\n662\t *   })\n663\t * );\n664\t * ```\n665\t */\n666\texport function conditionalByRole(\n667\t  handlers: Partial<Record<UserRole, MiddlewareHandler>>\n668\t): MiddlewareHandler {\n669\t  return async (c, next) => {\n670\t    const auth = getRbacAuthContext(c);\n671\t    const role = auth?.role || \"guest\";\n672\t\n673\t    const handler = handlers[role];\n674\t\n675\t    if (handler) {\n676\t      return handler(c, next);\n677\t    }\n678\t\n679\t    // Fall through to next middleware if no handler for this role\n680\t    return next();\n681\t  };\n682\t}\n683\t\n684\t/**\n685\t * Require context ownership or admin role.\n686\t *\n687\t * This middleware checks if the authenticated user owns the context\n688\t * being accessed. Admin users bypass the ownership check.\n689\t *\n690\t * @param getContextOwnerId - Function to extract context owner ID from context\n691\t * @param adminBypass - Whether admin role bypasses ownership check (default: true)\n692\t */\n693\texport function requireContextOwnershipOrAdmin(\n694\t  getContextOwnerId: (c: Context) => string | Promise<string>,\n695\t  adminBypass: boolean = true\n696\t): MiddlewareHandler {\n697\t  return async (c, next) => {\n698\t    const auth = getRbacAuthContext(c);\n699\t\n700\t    if (!auth) {\n701\t      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"context\", \"ownership\");\n702\t      throw new HTTPException(401, { message: \"Authentication required\" });\n703\t    }\n704\t\n705\t    // Admin bypass check\n706\t    if (adminBypass && auth.role === \"admin\") {\n707\t      logger.debug(\"Admin bypass for context ownership check\");\n708\t      return next();\n709\t    }\n710\t\n711\t    // Get the context owner ID\n712\t    const contextOwnerId = await getContextOwnerId(c);\n713\t\n714\t    // Check ownership\n715\t    if (auth.keyId !== contextOwnerId && contextOwnerId !== \"anonymous\") {\n716\t      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"context\", \"ownership\");\n717\t      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n718\t        message: \"Access denied: context ownership required\",\n719\t      });\n720\t    }\n721\t\n722\t    return next();\n723\t  };\n724\t}\n725\t\n726\t// ============================================================================\n727\t// Role Management Functions\n728\t// ============================================================================\n729\t\n730\t/**\n731\t * Assign a role to an API key.\n732\t * Note: In production, this should update a database.\n733\t * This function delegates to the authentication module.\n734\t */\n735\texport function assignRoleToApiKey(keyId: string, role: UserRole): boolean {\n736\t  const roleDef = ROLE_DEFINITIONS.get(role);\n737\t  if (!roleDef || !roleDef.assignable) {\n738\t    logger.warn(\"Attempted to assign non-assignable role\", { keyId, role });\n739\t    return false;\n740\t  }\n741\t\n742\t  return authAssignRole(keyId, role);\n743\t}\n744\t\n745\t/**\n746\t * Grant additional permissions to an API key.\n747\t * Note: In production, this should update a database.\n748\t * This function delegates to the authentication module.\n749\t */\n750\texport function grantPermissionsToApiKey(keyId: string, permissions: Permission[]): boolean {\n751\t  return authGrantPermissions(keyId, permissions);\n752\t}\n753\t\n754\t/**\n755\t * Revoke additional permissions from an API key.\n756\t * This function delegates to the authentication module.\n757\t */\n758\texport function revokePermissionsFromApiKey(keyId: string, permissions: Permission[]): boolean {\n759\t  return authRevokePermissions(keyId, permissions);\n760\t}\n761\t\n762\t/**\n763\t * Get all permissions for an API key (role + additional).\n764\t * This function delegates to the authentication module.\n765\t */\n766\texport function getApiKeyPermissions(keyId: string): Permission[] {\n767\t  return authGetApiKeyPermissions(keyId);\n768\t}\n769\t\n770\t// ============================================================================\n771\t// Permission Groups for Common Use Cases\n772\t// ============================================================================\n773\t\n774\t/**\n775\t * Permission groups for common functionality areas.\n776\t */\n777\texport const PermissionGroups = {\n778\t  /** Full trip access (create, read, update, delete, track) */\n779\t  TRIPS_FULL: [\n780\t    \"trips:create\",\n781\t    \"trips:read\",\n782\t    \"trips:update\",\n783\t    \"trips:delete\",\n784\t    \"trips:track\",\n785\t  ] as Permission[],\n786\t\n787\t  /** Own trip access only */\n788\t  TRIPS_OWN: [\n789\t    \"trips:create\",\n790\t    \"trips:read:own\",\n791\t    \"trips:update:own\",\n792\t    \"trips:delete:own\",\n793\t    \"trips:track:own\",\n794\t  ] as Permission[],\n795\t\n796\t  /** Full subscription access */\n797\t  SUBSCRIPTIONS_FULL: [\n798\t    \"subscriptions:create\",\n799\t    \"subscriptions:read\",\n800\t    \"subscriptions:update\",\n801\t    \"subscriptions:delete\",\n802\t  ] as Permission[],\n803\t\n804\t  /** Full admin access */\n805\t  ADMIN_FULL: [\n806\t    \"admin:users:read\",\n807\t    \"admin:users:update\",\n808\t    \"admin:users:delete\",\n809\t    \"admin:apikeys:read\",\n810\t    \"admin:apikeys:create\",\n811\t    \"admin:apikeys:update\",\n812\t    \"admin:apikeys:delete\",\n813\t    \"admin:apikeys:rotate\",\n814\t    \"admin:sessions:read\",\n815\t    \"admin:sessions:revoke\",\n816\t    \"admin:audit:read\",\n817\t    \"admin:system:configure\",\n818\t    \"admin:roles:manage\",\n819\t  ] as Permission[],\n820\t} as const;\n821\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"19b864e5-f5f9-4747-afef-54b401f04266","uuid":"fe17d315-c2dd-4658-a18d-832b34bb69ea","timestamp":"2026-05-09T05:30:28.139Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/mta-my-way/packages/server/src/middleware/rbac.ts","content":"/**\n * Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) system.\n *\n * Provides:\n * - User role definitions (admin, user, guest)\n * - Granular permission system\n * - Role-to-permission mappings\n * - Role hierarchy with inheritance\n * - RBAC middleware for Hono\n * - Permission checking utilities\n *\n * This module extends the existing scope-based authorization with a more\n * flexible role and permission system that allows for fine-grained access control.\n */\n\nimport type { Context, MiddlewareHandler } from \"hono\";\nimport { HTTPException } from \"hono/http-exception\";\nimport { logger } from \"../observability/logger.js\";\nimport {\n  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\n  type ApiKey,\n  type AuthContext,\n  assignRoleToApiKey as authAssignRole,\n  getApiKeyPermissions as authGetApiKeyPermissions,\n  grantPermissionsToApiKey as authGrantPermissions,\n  revokePermissionsFromApiKey as authRevokePermissions,\n  getAuthContext,\n} from \"./authentication.js\";\nimport { getRolePermissions as getRolePerms } from \"./roles.js\";\nimport { securityLogger } from \"./security-logging.js\";\n\n// Re-export the types from authentication for convenience\nexport type { UserRole, Permission } from \"./authentication.js\";\n\n// Re-export getRolePermissions from roles.ts for backward compatibility\nexport { getRolePerms as getRolePermissions };\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Type Definitions\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Resource categories for grouping permissions.\n */\nexport type ResourceCategory =\n  | \"trips\"\n  | \"subscriptions\"\n  | \"commutes\"\n  | \"journals\"\n  | \"alerts\"\n  | \"equipment\"\n  | \"predictions\"\n  | \"admin\"\n  | \"oauth\"\n  | \"mfa\"\n  | \"ratelimit\";\n\n/**\n * Action types for permissions.\n */\nexport type PermissionAction = \"create\" | \"read\" | \"update\" | \"delete\" | \"manage\";\n\n/**\n * Role definition with permissions and metadata.\n */\nexport interface RoleDefinition {\n  /** Role identifier */\n  role: UserRole;\n  /** Display name */\n  displayName: string;\n  /** Description of the role */\n  description: string;\n  /** Permissions granted to this role */\n  permissions: Permission[];\n  /** Whether this role can be assigned to users */\n  assignable: boolean;\n  /** Parent role for inheritance (optional) */\n  inheritsFrom?: UserRole;\n  /** Priority level for role hierarchy (higher = more privileges) */\n  priority: number;\n}\n\n/**\n * RBAC configuration options.\n */\nexport interface RbacOptions {\n  /** Whether to allow role inheritance */\n  enableInheritance?: boolean;\n  /** Whether to log all authorization checks */\n  logAuthorizationChecks?: boolean;\n  /** Custom permission checker function */\n  customPermissionChecker?: (\n    context: Context,\n    permission: Permission,\n    auth: AuthContext\n  ) => boolean | Promise<boolean>;\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Role Definitions\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Guest role - lowest privileges.\n * Guests can only read public data and create their own account.\n */\nconst GUEST_PERMISSIONS: Permission[] = [\n  \"alerts:read\",\n  \"equipment:read\",\n  \"predictions:create\",\n  \"predictions:read:own\",\n  \"oauth:authorize\",\n];\n\n/**\n * User role - standard privileges.\n * Users can manage their own trips, subscriptions, commutes, and journals.\n */\nconst USER_PERMISSIONS: Permission[] = [\n  // All guest permissions\n  ...GUEST_PERMISSIONS,\n  // Trip permissions\n  \"trips:create\",\n  \"trips:read:own\",\n  \"trips:update:own\",\n  \"trips:delete:own\",\n  \"trips:track:own\",\n  // Subscription permissions\n  \"subscriptions:create\",\n  \"subscriptions:read:own\",\n  \"subscriptions:update:own\",\n  \"subscriptions:delete:own\",\n  // Commute permissions\n  \"commutes:create\",\n  \"commutes:read:own\",\n  \"commutes:update:own\",\n  \"commutes:delete:own\",\n  // Journal permissions\n  \"journals:create\",\n  \"journals:read:own\",\n  \"journals:update:own\",\n  \"journals:delete:own\",\n  // MFA\n  \"mfa:setup\",\n  \"mfa:verify\",\n];\n\n/**\n * Admin role - highest privileges.\n * Admins have full access to all resources and system configuration.\n */\nconst ADMIN_PERMISSIONS: Permission[] = [\n  // All user permissions\n  ...USER_PERMISSIONS,\n  // Full trip access\n  \"trips:read\",\n  \"trips:update\",\n  \"trips:delete\",\n  \"trips:track\",\n  // Full subscription access\n  \"subscriptions:read\",\n  \"subscriptions:update\",\n  \"subscriptions:delete\",\n  // Full commute access\n  \"commutes:read\",\n  \"commutes:update\",\n  \"commutes:delete\",\n  // Full journal access\n  \"journals:read\",\n  \"journals:update\",\n  \"journals:delete\",\n  // Alert management\n  \"alerts:create\",\n  \"alerts:update\",\n  \"alerts:delete\",\n  // Equipment management\n  \"equipment:update\",\n  // Full predictions access\n  \"predictions:read\",\n  // Admin permissions\n  \"admin:users:read\",\n  \"admin:users:update\",\n  \"admin:users:delete\",\n  \"admin:apikeys:read\",\n  \"admin:apikeys:create\",\n  \"admin:apikeys:update\",\n  \"admin:apikeys:delete\",\n  \"admin:apikeys:rotate\",\n  \"admin:sessions:read\",\n  \"admin:sessions:revoke\",\n  \"admin:audit:read\",\n  \"admin:system:configure\",\n  \"admin:roles:manage\",\n  // OAuth management\n  \"oauth:revoke\",\n  // MFA management\n  \"mfa:disable\",\n  // Rate limit bypass\n  \"ratelimit:bypass:tier1\",\n  \"ratelimit:bypass:tier2\",\n  \"ratelimit:bypass:tier3\",\n];\n\n/**\n * Role definitions registry.\n */\nconst ROLE_DEFINITIONS: Map<UserRole, RoleDefinition> = new Map([\n  [\n    \"guest\",\n    {\n      role: \"guest\",\n      displayName: \"Guest\",\n      description: \"Unauthenticated users with minimal access\",\n      permissions: GUEST_PERMISSIONS,\n      assignable: true,\n      priority: 1,\n    },\n  ],\n  [\n    \"user\",\n    {\n      role: \"user\",\n      displayName: \"User\",\n      description: \"Authenticated users with standard access\",\n      permissions: USER_PERMISSIONS,\n      assignable: true,\n      inheritsFrom: \"guest\",\n      priority: 2,\n    },\n  ],\n  [\n    \"admin\",\n    {\n      role: \"admin\",\n      displayName: \"Administrator\",\n      description: \"System administrators with full access\",\n      permissions: ADMIN_PERMISSIONS,\n      assignable: false, // Admin role should be manually assigned\n      inheritsFrom: \"user\",\n      priority: 3,\n    },\n  ],\n]);\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Permission Utilities\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Parse a permission string into resource and action.\n * Example: \"trips:create\" -> { resource: \"trips\", action: \"create\" }\n */\nexport function parsePermission(permission: Permission): {\n  resource: ResourceCategory;\n  action: PermissionAction;\n  scope?: \"own\";\n} {\n  const parts = permission.split(\":\");\n  if (parts.length < 2) {\n    throw new Error(`Invalid permission format: ${permission}`);\n  }\n\n  const resource = parts[0] as ResourceCategory;\n  const action = parts[1] as PermissionAction;\n  const scope = parts[2] as \"own\" | undefined;\n\n  return { resource, action, scope };\n}\n\n/**\n * Build a permission string from components.\n */\nexport function buildPermission(\n  resource: ResourceCategory,\n  action: PermissionAction,\n  scope?: \"own\"\n): Permission {\n  const base = `${resource}:${action}` as Permission;\n  return scope ? (`${base}:${scope}` as Permission) : base;\n}\n\n/**\n * Get role definition by role name.\n */\nexport function getRoleDefinition(role: UserRole): RoleDefinition | undefined {\n  return ROLE_DEFINITIONS.get(role);\n}\n\n/**\n * Get all role definitions.\n */\nexport function getAllRoleDefinitions(): RoleDefinition[] {\n  return Array.from(ROLE_DEFINITIONS.values());\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if a role has a specific permission.\n */\nexport function roleHasPermission(role: UserRole, permission: Permission): boolean {\n  const permissions = getRolePerms(role);\n  return permissions.includes(permission);\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if one role has higher priority than another.\n * Returns true if role1 has higher priority than role2.\n */\nexport function isRoleHigher(role1: UserRole, role2: UserRole): boolean {\n  const def1 = ROLE_DEFINITIONS.get(role1);\n  const def2 = ROLE_DEFINITIONS.get(role2);\n\n  if (!def1 || !def2) return false;\n\n  return def1.priority > def2.priority;\n}\n\n/**\n * Get the highest role from a list of roles.\n */\nexport function getHighestRole(roles: UserRole[]): UserRole | null {\n  if (roles.length === 0) return null;\n\n  let highest = roles[0];\n  for (const role of roles) {\n    if (isRoleHigher(role, highest)) {\n      highest = role;\n    }\n  }\n\n  return highest;\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Authorization Context Enhancement\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Extended auth context that includes RBAC role information.\n */\nexport interface RbacAuthContext extends AuthContext {\n  /** User role */\n  role?: UserRole;\n  /** All roles assigned to the user */\n  roles?: UserRole[];\n  /** Specific permissions granted to this user (beyond role permissions) */\n  additionalPermissions?: Permission[];\n}\n\n/**\n * Get RBAC auth context from Hono context.\n */\nexport function getRbacAuthContext(c: Context): RbacAuthContext | undefined {\n  const auth = getAuthContext(c);\n  if (!auth) return undefined;\n\n  // Add role information from auth context if available\n  return {\n    ...auth,\n    role: auth.role as UserRole | undefined,\n    roles: auth.roles as UserRole[] | undefined,\n    additionalPermissions: auth.additionalPermissions as Permission[] | undefined,\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Set RBAC auth context on Hono context (for testing purposes).\n */\nexport function setRbacAuthContext(c: Context, authContext: RbacAuthContext): void {\n  c.set(\"auth\", authContext);\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Permission Checking\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Check if the current context has a specific permission.\n */\nexport function hasPermission(c: Context, permission: Permission, options?: RbacOptions): boolean {\n  const auth = getRbacAuthContext(c);\n\n  // Unauthenticated users only have guest permissions\n  const role = auth?.role || \"guest\";\n  const rolePermissions = getRolePerms(role);\n\n  // Check role permissions\n  if (rolePermissions.includes(permission)) {\n    if (options?.logAuthorizationChecks) {\n      logger.debug(\"Permission granted via role\", { role, permission });\n    }\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  // Check additional permissions if available\n  if (auth?.additionalPermissions?.includes(permission)) {\n    if (options?.logAuthorizationChecks) {\n      logger.debug(\"Permission granted via additional permissions\", { permission });\n    }\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  if (options?.logAuthorizationChecks) {\n    logger.debug(\"Permission denied\", { role, permission });\n  }\n\n  return false;\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if the current context has any of the specified permissions.\n */\nexport function hasAnyPermission(\n  c: Context,\n  permissions: Permission[],\n  options?: RbacOptions\n): boolean {\n  return permissions.some((permission) => hasPermission(c, permission, options));\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if the current context has all of the specified permissions.\n */\nexport function hasAllPermissions(\n  c: Context,\n  permissions: Permission[],\n  options?: RbacOptions\n): boolean {\n  return permissions.every((permission) => hasPermission(c, permission, options));\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// RBAC Middleware\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Require a specific permission for access.\n *\n * @example\n * ```ts\n * app.post(\"/trips\", requirePermission(\"trips:create\"), async (c) => {\n *   // Handler code\n * });\n * ```\n */\nexport function requirePermission(\n  permission: Permission,\n  options?: RbacOptions\n): MiddlewareHandler {\n  return async (c, next) => {\n    const auth = getRbacAuthContext(c);\n\n    if (!auth) {\n      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"rbac\", permission);\n      throw new HTTPException(401, { message: \"Authentication required\" });\n    }\n\n    // Use custom permission checker if provided\n    if (options?.customPermissionChecker) {\n      const hasCustomPermission = await options.customPermissionChecker(c, permission, auth);\n      if (!hasCustomPermission) {\n        securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"rbac\", permission);\n        throw new HTTPException(403, {\n          message: `Permission denied: ${permission}`,\n        });\n      }\n      return next();\n    }\n\n    // Standard permission check\n    if (!hasPermission(c, permission, options)) {\n      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"rbac\", permission);\n      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n        message: `Permission denied: ${permission}`,\n      });\n    }\n\n    return next();\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Require any of the specified permissions for access.\n */\nexport function requireAnyPermission(\n  permissions: Permission[],\n  options?: RbacOptions\n): MiddlewareHandler {\n  return async (c, next) => {\n    const auth = getRbacAuthContext(c);\n\n    if (!auth) {\n      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"rbac\", \"any_permission\");\n      throw new HTTPException(401, { message: \"Authentication required\" });\n    }\n\n    if (!hasAnyPermission(c, permissions, options)) {\n      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"rbac\", \"any_permission\");\n      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n        message: `Permission denied. Required: ${permissions.join(\" or \")}`,\n      });\n    }\n\n    return next();\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Require all of the specified permissions for access.\n */\nexport function requireAllPermissions(\n  permissions: Permission[],\n  options?: RbacOptions\n): MiddlewareHandler {\n  return async (c, next) => {\n    const auth = getRbacAuthContext(c);\n\n    if (!auth) {\n      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"rbac\", \"all_permissions\");\n      throw new HTTPException(401, { message: \"Authentication required\" });\n    }\n\n    if (!hasAllPermissions(c, permissions, options)) {\n      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"rbac\", \"all_permissions\");\n      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n        message: `Permission denied. Required: ${permissions.join(\" and \")}`,\n      });\n    }\n\n    return next();\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Require a specific role for access.\n *\n * @example\n * ```ts\n * app.delete(\"/admin/users/:id\", requireRole(\"admin\"), async (c) => {\n *   // Handler code\n * });\n * ```\n */\nexport function requireRole(requiredRole: UserRole): MiddlewareHandler {\n  return async (c, next) => {\n    const auth = getRbacAuthContext(c);\n\n    if (!auth) {\n      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"rbac\", requiredRole);\n      throw new HTTPException(401, { message: \"Authentication required\" });\n    }\n\n    const userRole = auth.role || \"guest\";\n\n    // Check if user has the required role or higher\n    if (!isRoleHigher(userRole, requiredRole) && userRole !== requiredRole) {\n      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"rbac\", requiredRole);\n      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n        message: `Role required: ${requiredRole}`,\n      });\n    }\n\n    return next();\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Require at least the specified role level.\n * Allows access to users with the role or any higher-priority role.\n */\nexport function requireRoleLevel(minRole: UserRole): MiddlewareHandler {\n  return async (c, next) => {\n    const auth = getRbacAuthContext(c);\n\n    if (!auth) {\n      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"rbac\", minRole);\n      throw new HTTPException(401, { message: \"Authentication required\" });\n    }\n\n    const userRole = auth.role || \"guest\";\n\n    // Check if user has at least the minimum required role\n    if (!isRoleHigher(userRole, minRole) && userRole !== minRole) {\n      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"rbac\", minRole);\n      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n        message: `Minimum role required: ${minRole}`,\n      });\n    }\n\n    return next();\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Require ownership of a resource or admin role.\n *\n * This middleware checks if the authenticated user owns the resource\n * being accessed. Admin users bypass the ownership check.\n *\n * @param resourceType - The type of resource being accessed\n * @param ownerIdParam - The parameter name containing the owner ID (default: \"userId\")\n * @param getOwnerId - Custom function to extract owner ID from context\n */\nexport function requireOwnershipOrAdmin(\n  resourceType: ResourceCategory,\n  options?: {\n    ownerIdParam?: string;\n    getOwnerId?: (c: Context) => string | Promise<string>;\n    adminBypass?: boolean;\n  }\n): MiddlewareHandler {\n  const { ownerIdParam = \"userId\", getOwnerId, adminBypass = true } = options || {};\n\n  return async (c, next) => {\n    const auth = getRbacAuthContext(c);\n\n    if (!auth) {\n      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, resourceType, \"ownership\");\n      throw new HTTPException(401, { message: \"Authentication required\" });\n    }\n\n    // Admin bypass check\n    if (adminBypass && auth.role === \"admin\") {\n      logger.debug(\"Admin bypass for ownership check\", { resourceType });\n      return next();\n    }\n\n    // Get the owner ID\n    let ownerId: string;\n    if (getOwnerId) {\n      ownerId = await getOwnerId(c);\n    } else {\n      ownerId = c.req.param(ownerIdParam) || \"\";\n    }\n\n    // Check ownership\n    // If ownerId is empty, the resource doesn't exist - let handler return 404\n    if (ownerId && auth.keyId !== ownerId) {\n      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, resourceType, \"ownership\");\n      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n        message: `Access denied: ${resourceType} ownership required`,\n      });\n    }\n\n    return next();\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Conditional permission check middleware.\n *\n * Executes different middleware based on the user's role.\n * Useful for implementing role-specific behavior.\n *\n * @example\n * ```ts\n * app.get(\n *   \"/trips\",\n *   conditionalByRole({\n *     admin: adminTripsHandler,\n *     user: userTripsHandler,\n *     guest: guestTripsHandler,\n *   })\n * );\n * ```\n */\nexport function conditionalByRole(\n  handlers: Partial<Record<UserRole, MiddlewareHandler>>\n): MiddlewareHandler {\n  return async (c, next) => {\n    const auth = getRbacAuthContext(c);\n    const role = auth?.role || \"guest\";\n\n    const handler = handlers[role];\n\n    if (handler) {\n      return handler(c, next);\n    }\n\n    // Fall through to next middleware if no handler for this role\n    return next();\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Require context ownership or admin role.\n *\n * This middleware checks if the authenticated user owns the context\n * being accessed. Admin users bypass the ownership check.\n *\n * @param getContextOwnerId - Function to extract context owner ID from context\n * @param adminBypass - Whether admin role bypasses ownership check (default: true)\n */\nexport function requireContextOwnershipOrAdmin(\n  getContextOwnerId: (c: Context) => string | Promise<string>,\n  adminBypass: boolean = true\n): MiddlewareHandler {\n  return async (c, next) => {\n    const auth = getRbacAuthContext(c);\n\n    if (!auth) {\n      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"context\", \"ownership\");\n      throw new HTTPException(401, { message: \"Authentication required\" });\n    }\n\n    // Admin bypass check\n    if (adminBypass && auth.role === \"admin\") {\n      logger.debug(\"Admin bypass for context ownership check\");\n      return next();\n    }\n\n    // Get the context owner ID\n    const contextOwnerId = await getContextOwnerId(c);\n\n    // Check ownership\n    if (auth.keyId !== contextOwnerId && contextOwnerId !== \"anonymous\") {\n      securityLogger.logAuthzFailure(c, \"context\", \"ownership\");\n      throw new HTTPException(403, {\n        message: \"Access denied: context ownership required\",\n      });\n    }\n\n    return next();\n  };\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Role Management Functions\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Assign a role to an API key.\n * Note: In production, this should update a database.\n * This function delegates to the authentication module.\n */\nexport function assignRoleToApiKey(keyId: string, role: UserRole): boolean {\n  const roleDef = ROLE_DEFINITIONS.get(role);\n  if (!roleDef || !roleDef.assignable) {\n    logger.warn(\"Attempted to assign non-assignable role\", { keyId, role });\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  return authAssignRole(keyId, role);\n}\n\n/**\n * Grant additional permissions to an API key.\n * Note: In production, this should update a database.\n * This function delegates to the authentication module.\n */\nexport function grantPermissionsToApiKey(keyId: string, permissions: Permission[]): boolean {\n  return authGrantPermissions(keyId, permissions);\n}\n\n/**\n * Revoke additional permissions from an API key.\n * This function delegates to the authentication module.\n */\nexport function revokePermissionsFromApiKey(keyId: string, permissions: Permission[]): boolean {\n  return authRevokePermissions(keyId, permissions);\n}\n\n/**\n * Get all permissions for an API key (role + additional).\n * This function delegates to the authentication module.\n */\nexport function getApiKeyPermissions(keyId: string): Permission[] {\n  return authGetApiKeyPermissions(keyId);\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Permission Groups for Common Use Cases\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Permission groups for common functionality areas.\n */\nexport const PermissionGroups = {\n  /** Full trip access (create, read, update, delete, track) */\n  TRIPS_FULL: [\n    \"trips:create\",\n    \"trips:read\",\n    \"trips:update\",\n    \"trips:delete\",\n    \"trips:track\",\n  ] as Permission[],\n\n  /** Own trip access only */\n  TRIPS_OWN: [\n    \"trips:create\",\n    \"trips:read:own\",\n    \"trips:update:own\",\n    \"trips:delete:own\",\n    \"trips:track:own\",\n  ] as   err instanceof Error ? err : undefined\n45\t      );\n46\t    });\n47\t  });\n48\t\n49\t  // Set initial active trips count\n50\t  const totalTrips = getTotalTripCount();\n51\t actual_duration_minutes,\n81\t          scheduled_duration_minutes, source, notes, created_at, updated_at, owner_id\n82\t        ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`\n83\t    ).run(\n84\t      id,\n85\t      trip.date,\n86\t      trip.origin.stationId,\n87\t      trip.origin.stationName,\n88\t      trip.destination.stationId,\n89\t      trip.destination.stationName,\n90\t      trip.line,\n91\t      \"N\", // Default direction for manual trips\n92\t      trip.departureTime,\n93\t      trip.arrivalTime,\n94\t      trip.actualDurationMinutes,\n95\t      trip.scheduledDurationMinutes ?? null,\n96\t      trip.source,\n97\t      trip.notes ?? null,\n98\t      now,\n99\t      now,\n100\t      ownerId\n101\t    );\n102\t\n103\t    // Invalidate commute 1000).toISOString().split(\"T\")[0]!,\n154\t    origin: { stationId: params.originId, stationName: originStation.name },\n155\t    destination: { stationId: params.destinationId, stationName: destStation.name },\n156\t    line: params.routeId,\n157\t    departureTime: params.departureTime * 1000,\n158\t    arrivalTime: params.arrivalTime * 1000,\n159\t    actualDurationMinutes,\n160\t    source: \"inferred\",\n161\t  };\n162\t\n163\t  return recordTrip(trip, ownerId);\n164\t}\n165\t\n166\t/**\n167\t * Update trip notes with ownership check.\n168\t *\n169\t * @param tripId - Trip ID to function updateTripNotes(tripId: string, notes: string, ownerId?: string): boolean {\n174\t  if (!db) return false;\n175\t\n176\t  try {\n177\t    let stmt: Database.Statement;\n178\t    const now = Date.now();\n179\t\n180\t    if (ownerId) {\n181\t      // Only update if the trip belongs to the owner\n182\t      stmt = db.prepare(\"UPDATE trips SET notes = ?, updated_at = ? WHERE id = ? AND owner_id = ?\");\n183\t      const result = stmt.run(notes, now, tripId, ownerId);\n184\t\n185\t      if (result.changes === 0) {\n186\t        logger.warn(\"Trip notes update failed: ownership check failed\", { tripId, ownerId });\n187\t      }\n188\t\n189\t      return result.changes > 0;\n190\t    } else {\n191\t      // No ownership check (for admin operations or backward compatibility)\n192\t      stmt = db.prepare(\"UPDATE trips SET notes = ?, updated_at = ? WHERE id = ?\");\n193\t      const result = stmt.run(notes, now, tripId);\n194\t\n195\t      return result.changes > 0;\n196\t    }\n197\t  } catch (error) {\n198\t    logger.error(\"Failed to update trip notes\", error instanceof Error ? error : undefined);\n199\t    return false;\n200\t  }\n201\t}\n202\t\n203\t/**\n204\t * Delete a trip from the journal with ownership check.\n205\t *\n206\t * @param tripId - Trip ID to delete\n207\t * @param ownerId - Owner ID for access control (optional, skips check if not provided for backward compatibility)\n208\t */\n209\texport function deleteTrip(tripId: string, ownerId?: string): boolean {\n210\t  if (!db) return false;\n211\t\n212\t  try {\n213\t    let stmt: Database.Statement;\n214\t\n215\t    if (ownerId) {\n216\t      // Only delete if the trip belongs to the owner\n217\t      stmt = db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM trips WHERE id = ? AND owner_id = ?\");\n218\t      const result = stmt.run(tripId, ownerId);\n219\t\n220\t      if (result.changes === 0) {\n221\t        logger.warn(\"Trip deletion failed: ownership check failed\", { tripId, ownerId });\n222\t        return false;\n223\t      }\n224\t\n225\t      invalidateCommuteStats(DEFAULT_COMMUTE_ID);\n226\t      logger.info(\"Trip deleted\", { tripId, ownerId });\n227\t      return true;\n228\t    } else {\n229\t      // No ownership check (for admin operations or backward compatibility)\n230\t      const result = db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM trips WHERE id = ?\").run(tripId);\n231\t\n232\t      if (result.changes > 0) {\n233\t        invalidateCommuteStats(DEFAULT_COMMUTE_ID);\n234\t        logger.info(\"Trip deleted (admin)\", { tripId });\n235\t        return true;\n236\t      }\n237\t      return false;\n238\t    }\n239\t  } catch (error) {\n240\t    logger.error(\"Failed to delete trip\", error instanceof Error ? error : undefined);\n241\t    return false;\n242\t  }\n243\t}\n244\t\n245\t// ============================================================================\n246\t// Journal Query API\n247\t// ============================================================================\n248\t\n249\t/**\n250\t * Get trip records with pagination and optional ownership filtering.\n251\t *\n252\t * @param params - Query parameters including optional ownerId for access control\n253\t */\n254\texport function getTrips(params: {\n255\t  limit?: number;\n256\t  offset?: number;\n257\t  startDate?: string;\n258\t  endDate?: string;\n259\t  originId?: string;\n260\t  destinationId?: string;\n261\t  line?: string;\n262\t  source?: TripSource;\n263\t  ownerId?: string; // For ownership filtering - if not provided, returns all trips (admin only)\n264\t}): TripRecord[] {\n265\t  const startTime = Date.now();\n266\t\n267\t  if (!db) {\n268\t    recordTripQueryDuration(0);\n269\t    recordTripQueried(false);\n270\t    return [];\n271\t  }\n272\t\n273\t  const {\n274\t    limit = 50,\n275\t    offset = 0,\n276\t    startDate,\n277\t    endDate,\n278\t    originId,\n279\t    destinationId,\n280\t    line,\n281\t    source,\n282\t    ownerId,\n283\t  } = params;\n284\t\n285\t  const conditions: string[] = [\"1=1\"];\n286\t  const sqlParams: unknown[] = [];\n287\t\n288\t  if (ownerId) {\n289\t    conditions.push(\"owner_id = ?\");\n290\t    sqlParams.push(ownerId);\n291\t  }\n292\t\n293\t  if (startDate) {\n294\t    conditions.push(\"date >= ?\");\n295\t    sqlParams.push(startDate);\n296\t  }\n297\t  if (endDate) {\n298\t    conditions.push(\"date <= ?\");\n299\t    sqlParams.push(endDate);\n300\t  }\n301\t  if (originId) {\n302\t    conditions.push(\"origin_station_id = ?\");\n303\t    sqlParams.push(originId);\n304\t  }\n305\t  if (destinationId) {\n306\t    conditions.push(\"destination_station_id = ?\");\n307\t    sqlParams.push(destinationId);\n308\t  }\n309\t  if (line) {\n310\t    conditions.push(\"line = ?\");\n311\t    sqlParams.push(line);\n312\t  }\n313\t  if (source) {\n314\t    conditions.push(\"source = ?\");\n315\t    sqlParams.push(source);\n316\t  }\n317\t\n318\t  sqlParams.push(limit, offset);\n319\t\n320\t  const rows = db\n321\t    .prepare(\n322\t      `SELECT\n323\t        id, date, origin_station_id, origin_station_name,\n324\t        destination_station_id, destination_station_name,\n325\t        line, direction, departure_time, arrival_time,\n326\t        actual_duration_minutes, scheduled_duration_minutes,\n327\t        source, notes, owner_id\n328\t      FROM trips\n329\t      WHERE ${conditions.join(\" AND \")}\n330\t      ORDER BY departure_time DESC\n331\t      LIMIT ? OFFSET ?`\n332\t    )\n333\t    .all(...sqlParams) as TripRecordRow[];\n334\t\n335\t  const duration = (Date.now() - startTime) / 1000;\n336\t  recordTripQueryDuration(duration);\n337\t  recordTripQueried(true);\n338\t\n339\t  return rows.map(rowToTripRecord);\n340\t}\n341\t\n342\tinterface TripRecordRow {\n343\t  id: string;\n344\t  date: string;\n345\t  origin_station_id: number;\n352\t  arrival_time: number;\n353\t  actual_duration_minutes: number;\n354\t  scheduled_duration_minutes: number | null;\n355\t  source: string;\n356\t  notes: string | null;\n357\t  owner_id: string;\n358\t}\n359\t\n360\tfunction rowToTripRecord(row: TripRecordRow): TripRecord & { ownerId?: string } {\n361\t  return {\n362\t    id: row.id,\n363\t    date: row.date,\n364\t    origin: { stationId: row.origin_station_id, stationName: row.origin_station_name },\n365\t    destination: {\n366\t      stationId: row.destination_station_id,\n367\t      stationName: row.destination_station_name,\n368\t    },\n369\t    line: row.line,\n370\t    departureTime: row.departure_time,\n371\t    arrivalTime: row.arrival_time,\n372\t    actualDurationMinutes: row.actual_duration_minutes,\n373\t    scheduledDurationMinutes: row.scheduled_duration_minutes ?? undefined,\n374\t    source: row.source as TripSource,\n375\t    notes: row.notes ?? undefined,\n376\t    ownerId: row.owner_id,\n377\t  };\n378\t}\n379\t\n380\t/**\n381\t * Get a single trip by ID.\n382\t */\n383\texport function getTripById(tripId: string): (TripRecord & { ownerId?: string }) | null {\n384\t  const startTime = Date.now();\n385\t\n386\t  if (!db) {\n387\t    recordTripQueryDuration(0);\n388\t    recordTripQueried(false);\n389\t    return null;\n390\t  }\n391\t\n392\t  const row = db\n393\t    .prepare(\n394\t      `SELECT\n395\t        id, date, origin_station_id, origin_station_name,\n396\t        destination_station_id,    FROM trips\n401\t      WHERE id = ?`\n402\t    )\n403\t    .get(tripId) as TripRecordRow | undefined;\n404\t\n405\t  const duration = (Date.now() - startTime) / 1000;\n406\t checkTripOwnership(tripId: string, ownerId: string): boolean {\n438\t  if (!db) return false;\n439\t\n440\t  const row = db.prepare(\"SELECT owner_id FROM trips WHERE id = ?\").get(tripId) as\n441\t    | { owner_id: string }\n442\t    | undefined;\n443\t\n444\t  return row?.owner_id === ownerId || row?.owner_id === DEFAULT_OWNER_ID;\n445\t}\n446\t\n447\t/**\n448\t * Get the owner ID of a ============================================================================\n466\t\n467\t/**\n468\t * Calculate commute statistics for a route.\n469\t *\n470\t * @param commuteId - Commute ID to DEFAULT_COMMUTE_ID,\n475\t  ownerId?: string\n476\t): CommuteStats | null {\n477\t  if (!db) return null;\n478\t\n479\t  // Check cache first (skip cache if owner-specific query)\n480\t  const cached = !ownerId ? getCachedCommuteStats(commuteId) : null;\n481\t  if (cached && Date.now() - cached.last_updated < 300_000) {\n482\t    // Cache valid for 5 minutes\n483\t    return {\n484\t      commuteId: cached.commute_id,\n485\t      averageDurationMinutes: cached.average_duration_minutes,\n486\t      medianDurationMinutes: cached.median_duration_minutes,\n487\t      stdDevMinutes: cached.std_dev_minutes,\n488\t      totalTrips: cached.total_trips,\n489\t      tripsThisWeek: cached.trips_this_week,\n490\t      trend: cached.trend,\n491\t      averageDelayMinutes: cached.average_delay_minutes,\n492\t      maxDelayMinutes: cached.max_delay_minutes,\n493\t      onTimePercentage: cached.on_time_percentage,\n494\t      records: getTrips({ limit: 90, ownerId }),\n495\t    };\n496\t  }\n497\t\n498\t  // Get all trip records for statistics with ownership filter\n499\t  const allTrips = getTrips({ limit: 500, ownerId });\n500\t\n501\t  if (allTrips.length === 0) {\n502\t    return {\n503\t      commuteId,\n504\t      averageDurationMinutes: 0,\n505\t      medianDurationMinutes: 0,\n506\t      stdDevMinutes: 0,\n507\t      totalTrips: 0,\n508\t      tripsThisWeek: 0,\n509\t      trend: 0,\n510\t      averageDelayMinutes: 0,\n511\t      maxDelayMinutes: 0,\n512\t      onTimePercentage: 0,\n513\t      records: [],\n514\t    };\n515\t  }\n516\t\n517\t  // Calculate duration statistics\n518\t  const durations = allTrips.map((t) => t.actualDurationMinutes).sort((a, b) => a - b);\n519\t  const avgDuration = durations.reduce((sum, d) => sum + d, 0) / durations.length;\n520\t  // Calculate median: average of two middle values for even-length arrays\n521\t  const mid = Math.floor(durations.length / 2);\n522\t  const medianDuration =\n523\t    durations.length % 2 === 0\n524\t      ? (durations[mid - 1]! + durations[mid]!) / 2\n525\t      : (durations[mid] ?? 0);\n526\t\n527\t  // Calculate standard deviation\n528\t  const variance =\n529\t    durations.reduce((sum, d) => sum + Math.pow(d - avgDuration, 2), 0) / durations.length;\n530\t  const stdDev = Math.sqrt(variance);\n531\t\n532\t  // Calculate delay statistics - filter trips that have scheduledDurationMinutes set\n533\t  const tripsWithSchedule = allTrips.filter((t) => t.scheduledDurationMinutes != null);\n534\t  const delays = tripsWithSchedule.map((t) => ({\n535\t    delay: t.actualDurationMinutes - (t.scheduledDurationMinutes ?? 0),\n536\t    onTime: Math.abs(t.actualDurationMinutes - (t.scheduledDurationMinutes ?? 0)) <= 2,\n537\t  }));\n538\t\n539\t  const avgDelay =\n540\t    delays.length > 0 ? delays.reduce((sum, d) => sum + d.delay, 0) / delays.length : 0;\n541\t  const maxDelay = delays.length > 0 ? Math.max(...delays.map((d) => d.delay)) : 0;\n542\t  const onTimeCount = delays.filter((d) => d.onTime).length;\n543\t  const onTimePercentage = delays.length > 0 ? (onTimeCount / delays.length) * 100 : 0;\n544\t\n545\t  // Calculate trips this week\n546\t  const now = new Date();\n547\t  const weekStart = new Date(now);\n548\t  weekStart.setDate(now.getDate() - now.getDay());\n549\t  weekStart.setHours(0, 0, 0, 0);\n550\t  const weekStartISO = weekStart.toISOString().split(\"T\")[0]!;\n551\t  const tripsThisWeek = allTrips.filter((t) => t.date >= weekStartISO).length;\n552\t\n553\t  // Calculate trend vs prior 4 weeks\n554\t  const fourWeeksAgo = new Date(weekStart);\n555\t  fourWeeksAgo.setDate(weekStart.getDate() - 28);\n556\t  const fourWeeksAgoISO = fourWeeksAgo.toISOString().split(\"T\")[0]!;\n557\t\n558\t  const priorWeekTrips = allTrips.filter(\n559\t    (t) => t.date >= fourWeeksAgoISO && t.date < weekStartISO\n560\t  ).length;\n561\t  const prior4WeekAvg = priorWeekTrips / 4;\n562\t  // If no prior week data, treat as positive trend when we have current week data\n563\t  const trend =\n564\t    prior4WeekAvg > 0\n565\t      ? ((tripsThisWeek - prior4WeekAvg) / prior4WeekAvg) * 100\n566\t      : tripsThisWeek > 0\n567\t        ? 100\n568\t        : 0;\n569\t\n570\t  const stats: CommuteStats = {\n571\t    commuteId,\n572\t    averageDurationMinutes: Math.round(avgDuration * 10) / 10,\n573\t    medianDurationMinutes: medianDuration,\n574\t    stdDevMinutes: Math.round(stdDev * 10) / 10,\n575\t    totalTrips: allTrips.length,\n576\t    tripsThisWeek,\n577\t    trend: Math.round(trend * 10) / 10,\n578\t    averageDelayMinutes: Math.round(avgDelay * 10) / 10,\n579\t    maxDelayMinutes: Math.round(maxDelay),\n580\t    onTimePercentage: Math.round(onTimePercentage * 10) / 10,\n581\t    records: allTrips.slice(0, 90),\n582\t  };\n583\t\n584\t  // Cache the stats (only for non-owner-specific queries)\n585\t  if (!ownerId) {\n586\t    cacheCommuteStats(stats);\n587\t  }\n588\t\n589\t  return stats;\n590\t}\n591\t\n592\t/**\n593\t * Get cached commute statistics.\n594\t */\n595\tfunction getCachedCommuteStats(commuteId: string): {\n596\t  commute_id: string;\n597\t  average_duration_minutes: number;\n598\t  median_duration_minutes: number;\n599\t  std_dev_minutes: number;\n600\t  total_trips: number;\n601\t  trips_this_week: number;\n602\t  trend: number;\n603\t  average_delay_minutes: number;\n604\t  max_delay_minutes: number;\n605\t  on_time_percentage: number;\n606\t  last_updated: number;\n607\t} | null {\n608\t  if (!db) return null;\n609\t\n610\t  return db\n611\t    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM commute_stats WHERE commute_id = ?\")\n612\t    .get(commuteId) as ReturnType<typeof getCachedCommuteStats>;\n613\t}\n614\t\n615\t/**\n616\t * Cache commute statistics.\n617\t */\n618\tfunction cacheCommuteStats(stats: CommuteStats): void {\n619\t  if (!db) return;\n620\t\n621\t  const now = Date.now();\n622\t\n623\t  db.prepare(\n624\t    `INSERT OR REPLACE INTO commute_stats (\n625\t      commute_id, average_duration_minutes, median_duration_minutes,\n626\t      std_dev_minutes, total_trips, trips_this_week, trend,\n627\t      average_delay_minutes, max_delay_minutes, on_time_percentage,\n628\t      last_updated\n629\t    ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`\n630\t  ).run(\n631\t    stats.commuteId,\n632\t    stats.averageDurationMinutes,\n633\t    stats.medianDurationMinutes,\n634\t    stats.stdDevMinutes,\n635\t    stats.totalTrips,\n636\t    stats.tripsThisWeek,\n637\t    stats.trend,\n638\t    stats.averageDelayMinutes,\n639\t    stats.maxDelayMinutes,\n640\t    stats.onTimePercentage,\n641\t    now\n642\t  );\n643\t}\n644\t\n645\t/**\n646\t * Invalidate commute stats cache for all commutes.\n647\t * Since trips are global (not tied to specific commutes), any result.count;\n680\t}\n681\t\n682\t/**\n683\t * Clean up old trip records (older than 90 days, keep last 500).\n684\t */\n685\texport function cleanupOldTrips(): number {\n686\t  if (!db) return // Get count of trips older than 90 days\n693\t  const oldTripsCount = db\n694\t    .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM trips WHERE date < ?\")\n695\t    .get(cutoffDate) as { count: number };\n696\t\n697\t  const totalTrips = getTotalTripCount();\n698\t\n699\t  // Only delete if we have more than 500 total AND some are old\n700\t  if (totalTrips > 500 && oldTripsCount.count > 0) {\n701\t    const toDelete = totalTrips - 500;\n702\t    const result = db\n703\t      .prepare(\n704\t        `DELETE FROM trips\n705\t         WHERE date < ?\n706\t         ORDER BY departure_time remainingCount: getTotalTripCount(),\n714\t    });\n715\t\n716\t    return result.changes;\n717\t  }\n718\t\n719\t  return
